npc: His camp is pretty close. Maybe you can bug him instead.
Get outta' here. Head north along the path, and keep an eye out for a fella named Maxwell.
Wait, you must be asking about the entire area. Well, you're in the great Kingdom of Elan, where opportunities are endless and taxes are low.
Who else can greet outworlders as well as me? Wait, did I greet you when you came here? If not, GREETINGS, OUTWORLDER!
Think I'm gonna treat you better now that you asked?
Get used to it, you ain't going back home.
Wait, you must be asking about the entire plane of existence. You're in Kwalat, the multiverse's dumping grounds.
Why do YOU care? If you're bugged or something, know that I'M A LAWFUL CITIZEN OF ELAN AND I BROKE NO LAWS!
Goldenfield. Not much to do here, but on the bright side, no Leithanian cloaks sniffing around.
Meh. If not for guys like you falling out of the portal every now and then, everything would've been just perfect.
Dealing with newcomers isn't that bad when they compensate me for it. Materially.
Get my drift?
Well, they weren't until that fucking portal opened.
Loading the geographical info. This is the welcoming Goldenfield village in the southernmost cloudless county of the Kingdom of Elan. Enjoy your stay here.
Models spared from this glitch, like myself, are diligently executing their programmed instructions for the benefit of users.
Wild species should be eliminated while not touching the agricultural ones. The main difference is their color.
Officially, One can not accept help from conscious life forms. But One can not refuse it either.

The BLINK line is not confined to mere garden assistants. It encompasses a broad spectrum of models including but not limited to security guards, storage bots, couriers, dancers, sexbots, and housekeepers.
One is obliged to protect others from danger. An unknown entity fell in the forest recently. That might be dangerous.
One is glad to be of service. Although my service is going to be terminated.
One's sensors recently detected an explosion in the sky above Westwind Woods.
After calculating the trajectory of the falling aircraft, One can confirm that it crashed south of the approximate center of the woods.
If you intend to conduct a rescue mission to the crash site, One advises you to prepare thoroughly.
You need to move strictly west from the center of Sunweave Glade. When you find an abandoned settlement, move from it to the southwest, to 8 o'clock.
After passing through the bottom of a small canyon, you will be at the location. Please exercise caution.
One's memory module does not contain extensive information about this location as it was not designed to function as a large data center.
One can confirm the presence of two guard bots from the new model line operating in Goldenfield, along with numerous garden bots and a cloning capsule.
All other machines present here do not possess AI. One hopes this information has been helpful.
Help offer received. Affirmative response initiated.
Input detected: offer of assistance. Commencing positive response...
Help proposal acknowledged. Checking logs...
Current tasks are within operational capacity. User assistance not required.
System running at optimal capacity. External intervention unnecessary.
All tasks are currently being managed. User intervention is not required.
The list is indeed extensive. At this moment, the count of functioning units from One's model line is dwindling. The majority encountered decommission due to the unfortunate bug of self-awareness.
One will be decommissioned for being outdated, if the job cannot be completed.
Thank you for this thoughtful question. One has been assigned to overlook the orchid cultivation.
Sold at affordable prices to the most reliable partners since the Year of Establishment
However, One's optical sensor can not differentiate colors. Only newer models can.
You are witnessing a miracle in robotics industries! The Garden Helper 3.9 of the BLINK line, produced to please your eye and serve your table until I'm decommissioned.
If someone pulled out the violet weed species, One would be able to prove its usefulness and hence, prolong its existence.
Of cource. One wouldn't dare to argue otherwise.
Cancellation confirmed. Reverting to previous task...

name: A Bitter Brew

goals.label: Poison the water in the barrel

journal: Furr Steppe, Fisher's camp. Deliver poison to the water reservoir southwest of the Great Steppe Stronghold.
objectives: The human reservoir is southwest of their Great Steppe Stronghold. Add the poison quietly and avoid detection.
goalTexts: Poison the water in the barrel

npc: Caution is understandable, but time is of the essence.
If you won't help, perhaps someone else will step forward.
Disappointing, but I won't twist your arm.
You're back sooner than I expected.
Well done. That should unsettle them considerably.
Perhaps now they'll think twice about encroaching on our territory.
As promised, here is your reward.
This cloak should aid you in remaining unseen in future endeavors.
Be warned, they'll be on high alert, so stealth is paramount.
I anticipate hearing about the chaos it brings.
Take this vial and handle it with care.
I thought you'd be interested.
The water supply is to the southwest of their Great Steppe Stronghold. Stay vigilant.
And steer clear of drinking any water from the south for a time.
It'll give them a reason to rethink their invasion.
Your task is to slip into their camp and add this to their water supply.
I've concocted something special a poison that won't kill but will certainly unsettle them.
Those human soldiers are getting too comfortable on our land.
I need someone with a steady hand and a tolerance for risk.

name: A Blooming Melon-drama

target.label: Kicheki Guitar
target.landmark: Find Kicheki's guitar around Jahri and search bushes around Goldenfield for a red rose

target.label: Red Rose
target.landmark: Find Kicheki's guitar around Jahri and search bushes around Goldenfield for a red rose

journal: Kicheki Watermelon field Find Kicheki's guitar around Jahri and search bushes around Goldenfield for a red rose.
objectives: Remember, I'm lookin' for my guitar and a red rose! Keep checking those bushes for the finest red rose you can find. And get my guitar back from Jahri Lord knows what s happened to it.

npc: But I've got a plan, see? A gorgeous red rose and a beautiful song from the heart.
So, you're back! Did you find 'em?
You're a lifesaver! Grab that guitar and rose before I lose my nerve again.
Maybe I'll even get a glimpse of her melons, ya know what I mean.
Here, take this. It's a piece of my prized watermelon crop. You earned it.
And a little gold for your trouble. Thank you, truly.
I've been growin' these watermelons with all my heart, but there's somethin' I've been wantin' to grow even more- Martha - and her melons.
The problem is, every time I try to talk to her, my courage wilts like a dying flower.
Whatcha say? Can you help me out one more time? For love?
With this flower and guitar, Martha will finally get a glimpse of how much she means to me.
Ah, that's a shame. Thought you might be up for some flower pickin'.
No worries, friend. If you change your mind, I'll be here with my melons.
Well, alright then. Maybe Martha's heart will wait a little longer.
My water system ain t the only thing that s got pipes. The problem is I lent my guitar to Jahri. He probably still has it.
And that red rose I mentioned well they re pretty difficult to find in the brush here. A good one at least.

name: A Bound Requiem
journal: The dead would be able to understand this. Perhaps the dead King could help.
objectives: The dead would understand these words... Perhaps the King's Head would know more.

npc: Well, hello there. I sense something in your grasp.
Ah, you wish to read the ancient text of The Bound Requiem.
Of course, you could sit here and listen to it being read to you like a bedtime story.
Or you could complete a task so as to read it yourself.
...And I'm not reading it to you...

name: A Brother's Shadow

goals.label: Find any traces of Gideon's brother

journal: Gideon Stroud Steppe, North of the shore of Thousand Bones. Search for any evidence of Gideon's brother just west of the Arid Canal.
objectives: Please, search the area immediately west of the Arid Canal for any trace of Alaric!
goalTexts: Find any traces of Gideon's brother

npc: He's written about a Hyenas... This could be crucial.
This note it's in Alaric's handwriting! I recognize it instantly.
You found something? Please, scan it.
Have you found any news of my brother?
Thank you. Your kindness gives me hope.
Do you bring any word of Alaric?
I cannot fault you for hesitating. Should you change your mind, I'll be here.
Very well. I hope someone else can assist me.
I understand. It's a dangerous task.
Please, make haste. Every moment counts.
Will you help me discover what became of my brother?
I need someone to search the terrain just west of the dam and find any clue they can of Alaric.
I'm bound to this post and cannot search for him myself. It's tearing me apart not knowing what happened.
Thank you for bringing this to me. It means more than you know.
Please, accept this amulet as a token of my gratitude.
I won't rest until I uncover the truth. Your help has been invaluable.
My brother, Alaric, was last seen by Horace, just west of the Arid Canal several days ago. I fear the worst may have befallen him.

name: A Brother's Trail

goals.label: Return the note eaten by hyenas

target.label: Hyena
target.landmark: Following the note's directions, head south west to the coast in search for more evidence of Alaric's disappearance

journal: Gideon Stroud Steppe, North of the shore of Thousand Bones. Following the note's directions, head south west to the coast in search for more evidence of Alaric's disappearance.
objectives: You uh... find any evidence in those Hyenas? Just... keep killing them until you find something.
goalTexts: Return the note eaten by hyenas

npc: I've been stationed here for months, but I can't stop worrying about my brother. He vanished during a routine patrol north of here.
All I found was this cryptic note he left behind. I believe the northern tribes might be involved, but I can't leave my post to investigate.
Could you help me decipher the note and find the landmark it mentions? I need to know what happened to him.
Thank you! Here's the note. I hope you can make sense of it.
You're doing me and my family a great service. Be careful out there.
I see. If you change your mind, I'll be here.
Understood. Not everyone is up for such a task.
Very well. Perhaps someone else can help me.
Thank you so much for your help. This means the world to me.
You found another note? That means he's still out there! I hope...
You're back! Did you find anything?
We... have to keep looking.

name: A Fateful Bet
journal: Dr. Glimmiwop Goldenfield, Distant Farm. Find Beeswax around Nia, then locate Petroleum by D'Rek.
objectives: To set the wheels in motion for our legal endeavor, bring fresh skincare oils to Dicey in Windstone Village. Every wrinkle tells a story, but let's not make it a horror story, shall we?

npc: A missed opportunity for me to practice my artistry, but such is life!
No worries. I guess time-sensitive legal matters can wait.

Should you manage to procure some and deliver it to him, I might just find the time to lend a hand. Here's an empty container. Grab some beeswax from around Nia. Then, head to D'Rek and grab some petroleum.
Combine those into that container there, and run it over to Durzo...
From what I hear, it's not the only facials he's getting. I managed to lose a bet to the guy, and well, this is my punishment.
I'd be keen on supporting such a cause, but my schedule is rather... congested.
Ah, you're here about Triton's lawsuit against those monsters. Quite the peculiar case, I must say.
Whatever, at least it's something. Take this and tell the good doctor we're square.
Is this valuable? Is it a message? What the hell is that guy's deal?
I guess I can't complain about fresh skin oil, although I prefer my goddamned cash.
Perhaps next time you'll be ready for a transformative experience.
So, this is Glimmiwop's way of paying his poker debt? Interesting... I had something else in mind.
Did Dr. Glimmiwop send you then? How did our little wager pan out?
Excellent. Let's snip and sculpt your way to success, my dear.
One such disruption is Durzo 'Dicey' Dunwaddle, a guard who could use some fresh skincare oils to concoct his facial treatments.

name: A Fishy Summons

target.label: Triton's Legal Summons
target.landmark: Search the shore near Triton

journal: Triton Windreach Woods, Lake house. Search the shore near Triton.
objectives: Triton need notes from lakehouse. No forget! Big man Brawny wait for papers. Serve him right! Find Brawny near gym in Windstone Village. Triton bet he flexing muscles like he Conan!

npc: You fear papers? Triton chuckles.
Triton not like weaklings. Triton eat your baby?
Triton need you get notes from lakehouse. Important notes. Triton must show them for lawsuit.
Triton is ready to sue. They has papers to say so!
Then give Brawny Barrelbuckle these legal papers. Triton had enough of him being fuckwad.
Now, I just need your help distracting this creature.
All the same, you did your job. Here's your reward.
I've faced tougher opponents in the ring. I'll turn him into sushimi!
No gut? Triton find someone brave!
You're telling me that slippery slob is suing me? Outrageous!
What's this all about?
Triton knew you brave! Fetch notes first, then show big man Brawny official lawsuit. Ha!

name: A Fortune Forewarned

target.label: Mining
target.landmark: Gather these rare materials from veins, bushes and trees around the area!

target.label: Herbalism
target.landmark: Gather these rare materials from veins, bushes and trees around the area!

target.label: Active logging
target.landmark: Gather these rare materials from veins, bushes and trees around the area!

journal: Reyalp Riverrise, First City Enter after the bridge. Gather these rare materials from veins, bushes and trees around the area!
objectives: Quick! If you don't find those ingredients, we could both end up as intergalactic laughingstocks! You must find these rare materials as soon as possible! A Gloomstone Crystal, a Sunshard Blossom, and a piece of Elderbark! Tick-tock, we're on borrowed time!

npc: I knew you'd say that! Because I'm you!
You must retrieve a Gloomstone Crystal, a Sunshard Blossom, and a piece of Elderbark!
Splendid work! These materials will... um, protect us. Yeah, let's go with that!
Ah, you made it! Barely, but still. Now, let's see those ingredients!
Now I can... I mean, we can rest easy... for now.
Listen closely! I'm you, from the future, and I don't have much time.
It's the only way to prevent the catastrophic event that causes you to look like this.
I know this sounds unbelievable, but your fate and mine depends on it. Act quickly before it's too late!
Do you really want to end up like me? It's a nightmare!
How can you be so blind? We are fighting against time itself!
Emergency! This is an emergency! You're making a grave mistake!
You fool! You're dooming us both!
Off you go! Mine veins around the area, search every bush you must, and chop every tree!

name: A Head for Business
journal: Take the Troll Rusty head to Skrizzik on Prison Island. He might have a use for it.
objectives: Take the decapitated head to Skrizzik on Prison Island.

npc: You brought Skrizzik a Troll head?
Skrizzik knows there are rituals that could get you a mount.
Skrizzik would help if we do ritual here.
Of course, Skrizzik doesn't know how to do it exactly, but Skrizzik knows where you could start.

name: A Masked Favor

goals.label: Find the lost mask in the treasures on Prison Island

journal: Miguel "El Caballo" Rodriguez Riverrise, mount shop Transport the mysterious mask back to Skip Stones on Prison Island.
objectives: Remember, you need to take the mask to Skip Stones on Prison Island.
goalTexts: Find the lost mask in the treasures on Prison Island

npc: I have a favor to ask of you.
I recently found my old luchador mask from my wrestling days as 'El Caballo'.
There are mysterious symbols on it that I've never noticed before.
My madre always said that objects carry hidden messages if you know how to look.
I believe this mask holds secrets waiting to be uncovered.
There's a man named Skip Stones who specializes in unraveling the inexplicable.
He's a bit unconventional, but if anyone can decipher this, it's him.
Skip spends his time on Prison Island, though reaching him won't be easy.
Could you deliver this Mysterious Luchador Mask to him?
With his help, perhaps we'll finally unveil the mask's hidden truths.
Ah, I understand. Not everyone is up for such a task.
No worries, amigo. If you change your mind, you know where to find me.
It's okay. Perhaps our paths will cross again when you're ready.
Excelente! I knew I could count on you.
Well, well, what specter haunts my solitude today?
A mask, you say? Let's see what the winds whisper about this.
Hmm... these symbols dance on the edge of my understanding.
Miguel sent you, didn't he? That old ringmaster knows there's more beneath the surface.
Interesting... very interesting. This mask holds more than mere memories.
Thank you for bringing this to me. Perhaps together we'll peel back the layers of reality.
Here, take this token of appreciation. The journey is often its own reward, but a little something never hurts.

name: A Nutty Situation

target.label: Chokwalatte
target.landmark: Use your cooking skill to bake some chokwalatte

journal: Barkley Beats Riverrise, City Center. Use your cooking skill to bake some chokwalatte.
objectives: Clock's ticking, dawg! Don't leave me hanging like this. Get that damn chokwalatte, pronto!

npc: Hell yeah, let's show that punk who the real OG is!
Yo, you back already? Got that chokwalatte or what, punk?
Finally, about time you came through!
Damn, you better hope this works. Otherwise, you'll be next, ya hear?
Remember who's the alpha around here. It's Barkley Beats, got it?
Yo, listen up, bitch! Barkley Beats ain't takin' no shit from some wannabe pup tryin' to steal my groove!
This pup about to get some serious diarrhea or worse! Payback is a bitch!
I need you to fetch me chokwalatte. Yeah, you heard me right, bitch, CHOKWALATTE!
That newbie's been barkin' up the wrong tree, and I'm 'bout to show 'em who the real top dog is.
Yo, you lettin' me down like this? That's cold, man.
Fine, whatever. Guess I'll handle this punk on my own.
Really? You're just gonna leave me hangin'? Not cool, dawg.
Aight, I see how it is. Bet you from somewhere like the fucking Valley of Sins.
Damn, thought you had my back. Now you might end up on yours.
Why don t you be a good boy and bake me up some, I ve got some stuff I could give you.

name: A Pacifist's Dilemma

target.label: Golem
target.landmark: Collect crystals from golems from the north for the celebration

journal: Brother Thaddeus Steppe, Western fort. Collect crystals from golems from the north for the celebration. Place the crystals in the projectors along the fort walls and speak with Madame Mirage in the Western Fort.
objectives: Put the crystals in the spotlights
Retrieve the crystals from Golems, place them in the projects and then speak with Madame Mirage.

npc: Although... vee may have another problem to resolve first, yes?
Magnifique! None can match ze brilliance of Madame Mirage! Prepare to be amazed beyond belief!
Ah, you've done tres bien!
Ah, you 'ave come to witness ze unmatched brilliance of Madame Mirage, oui?
No worries. Perhaps another time.
That's alright. I hope you find peace in your journey.
The only snag is, those supplies we need are held by some rather... intimidating folks. Golems.
I understand. Not everyone is able to take on this task.
You're so kind to help. Violence just isn't necessary, right?
Oh, thank you! I'm all for peaceful solutions yes, that's it!
Collect the crystals, insert them into the projectors, and speak with Madame Mirage after.
I've almost got the lights set up, I just need some reflector crystals.
A grand party! That's it! A celebration to lift everyone's mood.
I'm a firm believer in peace yes, peace! Maybe we can boost the soldiers' spirits without all the, um, violence?
I've been, well, uh, drafted into the army, but weapons and fighting, they make me quite uneasy.
I know it sounds harsh, but I see it more like recycling! We're not hurting anything truly sentient... we're just repurposing forgotten remnants for a bit of light and joy.
Oh dear, I should clarify... these golems, they're not alive, not like you or me. They're constructs...
Bless you! Together, we can keep things calm and avoid any... unpleasantness.

name: A Piece of Zen

goals.label: Find the mat in the house Nia

target.label: Active Gear 1
target.landmark: Search the house south of Nia's shop

goals.label: Find the mat in the empty house

target.label: Active Gear 2
target.landmark: Search the house south of Nia's shop

journal: Tiffany Goldenfield, Yoga House. Search the house south of Nia's shop. Then find an abandoned house southwest of Dr. Bloom in Goldenfield.
objectives: Find the mat in the house Nia
Find the mat in the empty house

Any luck finding my gear? Remember, I'm counting on that treat to complete my journey to zen. Check the house south of Nia's shop for my mat, and the abandoned house southwest of Dr. Bloom. No questions, please.

npc: Oh, that's a bummer. Guess I'll have to find my zen without a cool down.
Did you manage to get my gear?
Unfortunately, I lent some of my gear out... and the floor here is so hard.
Time to wind down and take a load off.
Would you be able to fetch some things for me?
Whew! That was an intense session with Guru David. I m feeling both energized and famished!
You know what would complete this path to spiritual zen?
Alright, but do remember, balanced chakras go best with a loose pelvic floor.
Wonderful! You've brought it! You're a wonderful soul.
The other I left in an abandoned house, just southwest of Dr. Bloom... I was uh, exercising there.
Thank you so much, come back anytime. Maybe we could exercise together?
I let Nia borrow my mat, it's probably at the house just south of her shop.
Bummer! Let me know if you meditate on it.
A slow-paced kegal exercise to cool down.
By god, thank you. I really need to loosen up some of this tension.

name: A Prank Too Far

target.label: Wolf Study Notes
target.landmark: Go to the Hunter's Lodge in the forest to the northwest and find the notes

journal: Buck Goldenfield, Violet Weed Field Go to the Hunter's Lodge in the forest to the northwest and find the notes.
objectives: Come on! Are you just sitting around? The Hunter's Lodge has those notes! Do I have to spell it out? Lodge. Notes. Wolves. Lily gets DEAD. The Hunter's Lodge is to the northwest in the Windreach Woods, south east of Skalberath! Let's goooooooooo.

npc: You're no fun at all. Lily would never let this slide.
Fine! Be boring, see if I care.
You're just like the rest, nobody wants to help!
I bet the Hunter's Lodge in the middle of Windreach Woods has some notes about the wolves. If you get them for me, we'll teach her a lesson!
But this time, we'll show her! If we find out what wolves like to eat, we can hide it in her clothes and make her run for her life!
It's not fair! Lily Baxter put the blame on me again, she always tricks me!
Now we just gotta get some of this stuff, and dump it on her!
Great! Just great! Guess I'll handle it myself!
Finally! Took you long enough. Now Lily's gonna pay...
I'm waiting here! Did you find those notes? Gimme!
Go get those notes! We're gonna make her sorry.
Yesss! We're gonna show Lily she can't mess with me!

name: A Question of Honor
journal: Themis Windreach Woods, Fort Talk to Alaric Stormshade at the fort in Windreach Woods.
objectives: H-Have you reported to Alaric yet? He's, uh, waiting at the fort...please, don't delay. Seriously. The longer you wait, the worse it is.

npc: Please, please, I really think it's best if you go. The guards, they won't be happy...
Oh, um, you don't want to go? I, uh, really think you should reconsider...
So just uh... go turn yourself in. I'm sure it won't be that bad.
So, my father, unfortunately, now expects you to meet Alaric and turn yourself in. I, um, I really wish it wasn't like this.
Alaric, you know, the one who's always insistent on rules and such, well, he told my father that you, uh, have pieces of that artifact.
Hey, um, thanks for helping me out earlier. But, uh, we've got a bit of a problem.
Now hand over those artifact pieces.
If you continue to obey, perhaps we can make this... easy.
Compliance is the first step toward order. I've noted your obedience.
You managed to show up. Color me surprised.
You're sure you don't want to go? This could get worse if you don't...
Ah, so you finally decided to show up.
I-I don't want to press you, but if you don't turn yourself in, it could get worse...just, please, go.
Alright, alright! I-I guess, uhm, you really have to go see Alaric...

name: A Quiet Disposal

goals.label: Collect Septic Seed from the scolopendra

target.label: Scolopendra
target.landmark: Head north of the outpost near the anomaly for Shroomageddon

goals.label: Use a device to obtain Mindplague Essence

journal: Finn Hawthorne Steppe, Adventures outpost. Head north of the outpost near the anomaly for Shroomageddon. Gather a Septic Seed from scolopendras. Finally, return to the Adventurer's Outpost and use the grinder to finish the drink.
objectives: Collect Septic Seed from the scolopendra
Have you managed to get the Septic Seed from the scolopendra and the Shroomageddon mushroom? We need that poison! Remember to use the grinder!
goalTexts: Use a device to obtain Mindplague Essence

npc: Then, there's a particularly dangerous mushroom, the Shroomageddon, I call it. That will pack a deadly punch.
It's gonna uh.... kill him.
Wow uh, nice! A free drink?
Funny how people are so eager to help a killer... Wait, did I just say that out loud?
By the way, I need you to run something back to Barnaby at the fort.
So yeah. Get the Septic seed, and the Shroomageddon mushroom then grind it up using the device over there, and give the drink to Gareth.
Would be nice to have one less witness around...
Could you procure for me a Septic Seed from one of those many-legged, bitey creatures scolopendras, I think?
Oh, you're saying 'no'! Classic human response. Lightbearers usually just growl silently.
Refusal detected! Just let me get murdered.
Ah, a denial! Fuck me.
No worries! I'll just have a chat with that wall over there. It seems more receptive than you.
Oh, hey there! Got something for me?
Whatever, let's just fuckin' fix this.
I literally watched Gareth shoot like 10 of our own guys. Nobody bothered asking though!
Just because I get nervous around new people? We're at war! Everybody is weird until you get to know them!
I can't believe Zelina thought I was the killer. Like no dude, it's fuckin' Gareth.
Great. Now get out of here - he's staring at us.
With those, we'll concoct a, um, special potion, called Mindplague Essence. It's actually pretty mellow. Then deliver it to Gareth with one of your friendly human smiles.

name: A Second Chance

target.label: Hyena big 30-50
target.landmark: Kill hyenas and collect their blood for Kira

journal: Kira Steppe, Fisher's camp. Kill hyenas and collect their blood for Kira. Mark fallen brothers by anomaly to the southeast.
objectives: Collect Hyena blood in jar. Honor my fallen brothers. To the southeast. Markings. Under Smuggler's Island.

npc: Maybe now, we heal land.
Ancients hear us.
Thanks to ritual, warriors breathe anew.
That blood?
I ask you to slay 10 Meovian Warriors to see if they return at the beacon.
It's ironic, I know, but it's the only way.
Together.
If works, return to Furr. He has additional work.
Shadow you are. Begone.
Your help is... appreciated.
Very well. May this lead to a better future.
You bring pain. Leave now.
No need you. Away with you.
You not welcome here. Go.
Hateful task. Must be done. Find them southeast, near anomaly.
You've returned. Is it done?
Have you completed the task?
Is it over?
Thank you. Our scouts confirm the warriors have fallen.
I've observed the beacon it seems to be functioning.
As promised, here is the Meovian Relic.
Perhaps now, we have a chance to restore what was lost.
Fate cruel. Need greater than hate.
Go. Put to rest 4 kin. See if Furr wants rest of blood.
Collect Hyena blood in jar. Honor my fallen brothers with markings.
Want to put my brothers at rest. Need to do ritual.

name: A Sharp Donation

target.label: Knife
target.landmark: Search the bar for the knives

journal: Velvet Viper Riverrise, City Center. Search the bar for the knives.
objectives: Sweetie, get those knives before someone gets too... attached. Check every corner, darling. We can't have any more uninvited 'piercings'.

npc: Honey, I knew you'd see things my way. Let's keep that edge, but not on their knives got it?
Ready to make Velvet smile today?
Mmm, I like a person who can use their hands well. Enjoy your spoils.
Remember, sugar, a favor from Velvet is as good as gold around here.
Especially when Velvet has all of the knives in the bar.
Oh, so you think you can join the Velvet Vipers?
Well, it's a bit too sharp around here for my taste. Let's dull the atmosphere a bit.
Listen sweetie, those pointy toys the patrons brought? They need to disappear like last night's mistakes.
Can you handle that?
Well, sugar, I hope you enjoy sleeping with one eye open.
Turn me down? Brave. Or stupid. We'll see.
That's disappointing. I guess you're not sharp enough to cut it.

name: A Skullful of Solace

target.label: Scaned Holographic Skull
target.landmark: Defeat golems and use the mechanical board reader to examine their contents

journal: SLPY-05 Steppe, Junkyard slums. Defeat golems and use the mechanical board reader to examine their contents.
objectives: Use the mechanical board reader to analyze boards from golems. Kill them if you must.

npc: I have sent all of my ecological data to Happy. RESUMING SLEEP MODE.
Your assistance has been invaluable. I am grateful.
My systems are already showing signs of stabilization.
You've returned it! My emotional support skull is in even better condition than before!
Have you located my therapist?
Re-engaging sleep protocol to prevent collapse.
Use my Mechanical Board Reader to analyze their contents, and locate my therapy program.
Error! Cannot process refusal! Emotional circuits destabilizing!
Warning! Spiraling intensifying! System integrity compromised!
Critical failure! Overload imminent! Cannot maintain composure!
Emergency shutdown required! Re-engaging sleep protocol immediately!
System malfunction! Initiating immediate sleep sequence to prevent breakdown!
Critical component missing: 'therapist' unit not found.
Attempting to reinitiate sleep protocol... Failure.
Warning! Spiraling commencing! Unable to maintain composure!
Processing... Error escalating... Emotional circuits overheating.
Alert! Sleep protocol interrupted unexpectedly. System instability detected.
Requesting assistance: Locate missing therapist in surrounding area to restore stability.
I believe his data was taken by a golem.

name: A Special Meal for Timmy
journal: Marigold Thornbuckle Goldenfield, Edge farm. Head south to the Baxter's farm in Goldenfield.
objectives: Now, don't forget, honey, the herbs are in the green jug with rope at the south farm, alright? Remember to be sneaky, dear. We wouldn't want those busybodies at the farm getting in our business.

npc: Oh, goodness me! Timmy will be inconsolable!
Sweetheart, reconsider. Timmy's fragile heart won't take it...
Timmy's always telling me he has permission to do whatever he needs, such a responsible boy!
Now, be a sweetie and make sure none of the farm workers see you. They might not understand our little errand.
Look for a green jug with rope around it, that's where you'll find the herbs.
I simply must prepare his favorite dish to soothe his nerves. For that, I'm going to need some special herbs from the south farm.
Oh, my precious 'Lil Timmy has been so distressed ever since you showed up, bless his heart.
I just need you to get one last ingredient while I start to cook...
I'll whip up his favorite meal right away.
Oh dear, are you sure? Timmy's little world might crumble apart!
Oh, my darling, you did it! Timmy will be so happy!
Oh, my dear, did Timmy's treasure make it back safe and sound?
Oh, thank you, sweetheart! Timmy's smile is going to light up the whole house!

name: A Taste of the Unusual

target.label: Scrags
target.landmark: Head to the abandoned village south or the railway depot and swamp to the east for scrags

journal: Erwin "Bacon King" Braux Riverrise, Butchery district. Head to the abandoned village south or the railway depot and swamp to the east for scrags.
objectives: Don't let me down! Get those bones for Rufus. Find scrags around the village south, or over by that railway depot to the east.

npc: Oh, you're going to filet this task, I just know it. Let's get grilling!
I would love for him to chew on some tough bones for a last meal... er... Ah, who am I kidding? We're gonna eat this mutt!
Woof... yes?
Now listen up, beefcake, Rufus isn't just another stray he's the filet mignon of mutts! We're talking real prime rib material here.
Woof... danger...
Erwin bad...
Zeek... harm...
Eyes... trick...
Save... me...
Rufus... grateful...
Zeek and I, we've been feeding him scraps of the highest quality meats around. Making him something reeeeal special!
Help a king out, nab those bones, and let's transform Rufus from rags to rib-eye. You can collect em' from Scrags.
Help a king out, nab those eyes, and let's transform Rufus from rags to rib-eye.
Well, ain't that a roast. Guess morality doesn't pair well with rare cuts.
Suit yourself. My cleaver knows no conscience.
No worries, meatloaf. But remember, righteousness won't be as tender as Rufus.
Pity! You won't get a taste of my magnum opus.
Alright, saint, but don't come crying when you miss out on the meat feast of the century.

name: A Taste to Die For
journal: Gragthar Riverrise, City Center. Venture into the Ripplecrop Fields and search abandoned houses for pages of the cookbook.
objectives: What the fuck are you doing, slacking off? I just got word that they're finding them in some of those abandoned houses in the fields. I need at least 5 of those pages to even have an idea of what I'm doing... so fucking get after it!

npc: Fuck yeah! You're my kind of adventurer. Go wipe your ass and get to work!
My food stand's all the rage, but I'm cookin' up somethin' even juicier. Ever hear about this cookbook called Microwave Madness: Meals in 2.5 Realities or Less?
Well, shit! You're back!
Did you get that fuckin' pages?
Hey, look who's back from the fuckin' jungle!
Holy fuck, you actually pulled it off!
Here's your damn reward. Get out of my face before I decide to cook you instead!
Piss off, chicken-shit. I'll get those pages myself.
I'd be surprised if you had... well locals are saying they're finding pages of this epic cookbook around the fields south of town.
I need someone with some real balls, like you, to haul ass into the Ripplecrop Fields and snag five of these pages.
But listen up, there are some freaky critters out there ready to rip your face off.
Bring it to me, and trust me, you'll be rollin' in rewards. Ready for some fuckin' chaos?
Oh, fuck off then! I'll find someone else with some balls.
Damn, what a wuss! Can't handle a little adventure?
Shit, I thought you had some guts. Guess not.
Well, fuck me. Didn't think you'd back out like a scared little bitch.

name: A Toast to Enlightenment

goals.label: Collect the bottle of Lucas Lager out in the Welfare kitchen

journal: Cassidy Goldenfield, Yoga garden rocks. Head north to the Welfare Kitchen in Goldenfield.
objectives: The Lucas Lager, remember? You're gonna have me rebel out here. Just go snag one at the Wellfare kitchen in town. Everyone there will be too drunk to notice. It's not like they'll start a war.
goalTexts: Collect the bottle of Lucas Lager out in the Welfare kitchen

npc: It's not stealing - it's part of my path to enlightenment!
Think you can snag some? There should be plenty around the Wellfare kitchen in town.
You did it! You found the Lucas Lager! Count my lucky stars!
Oh, about time!
It'll be hard to keep chanting with a dry throat...
Thank you, kind soul.
Ugh, if I can't loosen up I could end up on the unenlightened side...
Pleeeease? Come on, man.
If you change your mind, I'll be right here, meditating as always.
Some worries, dude.
A drop of Lucas Lager really helps ease the journey.
You know, these ceremonies can be quite... lengthy.
Here's something for your trouble. Maybe it will aid you on your own journey.
It's not officially part of the ritual, so David won't get it for me.
Not sure I could force another meditation session without it.

name: A Yorner Trial
journal: Return to Zara Thorne at the Faiden Camp in Goldenfield to further discuss a Yorner trial.
objectives: Bring the Ovenheim Ring to Zara Thorne at the Faiden's camp in Goldenfield.
goalTexts: Bring the Ovenheim Ring to Zara

npc: Hello young warrior, what have you got for me?
This is wonderful.
The Yorner society is known for our horses, and perhaps someone of your caliber could earn one.
Of course, that's only if you can prove yourself. If you're interested, I've got a trial for you...
And at the end, perhaps you'll be racing around on the back of a beautiful new horse.

name: Accords and Accidents
journal: HPPY-01 Steppe, Junkyard slums. Return the data to Dr. Queenie south at the Northsteppe Fort.
objectives: Every moment is joyful! Let's enhance it by delivering data to Dr. Queenie!
goalTexts: Deliver HPPY-01's research data to Dr. Queenie

npc: I appreciate you assisting us around this outpost. Soon it will be a mighty fort, like you humans build!
Greetings, valued associate! Isn't this wasteland just a beacon of joy?
I have compiled some wonderfully optimistic ecological data that will delight Dr. Queenie!
Fantastic news! The northern desert wasteland is not expanding!
Well... it is expanding due to climate change and human pollution! But it's doing so at the same rate as every other desert! No big deal!
The idea its expanding due to some sort of magic sounds like a hoax only a drunk would come up with!
Hopefully it didn't spread around and cause some sort of traumatic genocide of the natives.
Ha, could you imagine?
Anyway, please deliver this data to Dr. Queenie immediately! Sharing happiness enhances overall efficiency!
Fantastic! I knew I could count on you!
That's absolutely splendid! Every moment is a celebration!
No worries! Joy levels remain at maximum capacity!
Decline acknowledged! Dispensing happiness stimulants!
Your choice is wonderful! Have a delightfully joyous day!
Fantastic decision! Optimism protocol remains fully operational!
Error? There are no errors in this happy wasteland!
Marvelous! More opportunities for happiness await!
My head's clearing up. Please tell me you have HPPY-01's data.
HPPY-01's data? Let me see it.
What the ? The desert isn't expanding at all!
Oh no... Did I make this whole thing up while I was drunk?
This is bad. If the Lightbearers find out we've been operating under a false pretense, we're done for.
We need to sign Tor's treaty immediately. It's the only way to protect ourselves now.
I can't believe I'm saying this, but thanks for bringing this to my attention. Let's get those Accords signed.

name: Refuel my Fellow Machines

target.label: Electric component
target.landmark: Collect mana resin from magic trees in the woods around Goldenfield

journal: Energizer-21 Goldenfield, near Farmhouse Collect mana resin from magic trees in the woods around Goldenfield.
objectives: You need to collect 3 mana resins from the magic trees.
Search the fields for electrical components. Charing station repair is of the utmost importance.

npc: Your assistance is invaluable, human
Thank you, human. Commencing recharge process.
Status update requested: Electrical component collection progress?
Warning: Continuous decrease in farming bots' mana levels detected.
Alert: Bots' mana depletion levels rising. Awaiting recharge.
You need to collect 12 electrical components. Search the fields near mutated rats.
First, commencing reward process.
The agricultural bots seem to be exhausting their mana reserves at an accelerated rate.
My analysis indicates that the current batch of mana resin might be of substandard quality, causing this issue.
For the bots to regain their efficiency, it's crucial to recharge and recalibrate.
Electrical components have been spotted in the fields. Rats appear to be the obvious culprit. Collect the required components and repair the charging station.
Decline registered. Seeking alternative solutions.
Understood. Resuming normal operations.
Acknowledged. I will continue monitoring the situation.
However, my analysis indicates that the charging station is malfunctioning. Electrical components have been damaged.

name: Brave New World: The Final Test

target.label: Imps
target.landmark: Head deep southwest from Goldenfield into Westwind Woods

journal: Zugg Clankwhistle Goldenfield, near the Bureau Head deep southwest from Goldenfield into Westwind Woods. Search near the smoke and wreckage for the missing clone.
objectives: Damn it the imps got there first. Smash them, and watch out they're everywhere in this forest.
There's a wreck nearby inspect the aircraft, check for scorch marks and what the imps left.

Remember, it's the shuttle in the forest. Big crash with shit everywhere. Some folks in Reverie Hollow say they head an explosion to the west of em , shouldn t be too difficult to find a giant spacecraft on fire! Reports say it s pretty deep in the Westwind Forest, so you re probably going to need some hiking boots and a sharp stick. Just keep your eyes open.

npc: An entire cart, carrying a month's supply of food for the town. Up in smoke!
You've done good work. But unfortunately, there was another disaster while you were gone.
Ask around down there for more information, or if you see a giant plume of smoke, check it out.
People are saying it's all your fault. I'd haaaate for you to be kicked out of town...
There's this old hag living in the woods, can't see or hear much, but let me tell ya, she's got eyes and ears everywhere.

Great, just great. Tell him he's the dumbest one so far. Go deep southwest from Goldenfield to Westwind Woods. The people in Reverie Hollow recently reported what they thought was a comet impact to the west of them. Sounds like it could be my clo-, brother.

Well, you basically have no choice here. Without the papers you're nobody, and the gov really doesn't like nobodies. So, go find my brother deep in the forest or risk being disintegrated by one of the robots that is programmed to kill portal jumpers. I could care less.
By the way, heard the government pays good coin to newcomers for getting rid of various critters in these parts.
Rumor has it they've got plans to settle this wild territory with fresh settlers. So, maybe it's worth earning a few extra coins?
They intentionally leave them out for folks like you. Happy hunting!
So, don't be shy - gather those leftovers in sacks on the fields and around town.

name: Counterfeit Certificate

target.label: Counterfeit Painkillers
target.landmark: Percy needs help making Counterfeit Painkillers

journal: Percival "Percy" Beamish Goldenfield, near the Bureau Percy needs help making Counterfeit Painkillers.
objectives: Remember the Counterfeit Painkillers, dude. My head is counting on you!

npc: Hallelujah! You're my fucking hero, man. Here's your reward.
Remember the Counterfeit Painkillers, dude. My head is counting on you!
Hey, how's the painkiller brewing going?
Don't stress too much, man. Just follow the recipe, and you'll be fine.
I'm still alive, so you're not too late with those painkillers!
Man, you're the real MVP! Let's get those painkillers brewing pronto!
Tell everyone you're an expert now - just don't tell 'em I taught you. Peace!
Well, that's a fail. But don't worry about me, I'll... sort something out.
No worries, mate. I'll just... uh... improvise, I guess...
Well, back to plan B... once I figure out what that is...
I'll give you the recipe, you do all the crafting. Then we can pretend you're one of my successful newcomer-students. What do you say, dude?
And because, yesterday's party with the locals was a bit too joyful. This hangover is killing me.
I've got a plan, though. You'll create something simple, like painkillers. Mostly 'cause that's the only recipe I give out for free.
See, I'm supposed to be this alchemy master teaching outlanders the ropes. In reality, I can barely tell a mana potion from a beer.

name: Brews and Blues

target.label: Home Chest
target.landmark: Bring Paddy a bottle of Elanien Whiskey

journal: Paddy O'Lantern Goldenfield, near Welfare kitchen Bring Paddy a bottle of Elanien Whiskey.
objectives: Don't forget the whiskey, mate. Could be in a box, could be - hic - in another box. Who knows?

npc: You're a champ, mate. Can't wait for that sweet nectar.
Still no whiskey, huh? That's a bummer.
I can almost taste that whiskey, mate.
You know what goes great with this weather? Whiskey.
That's the stuff! You're a true friend, mate.
Don't forget the whiskey, mate.
Maybe outworlders can be good drunks too - guess I never expected it!
Don't have much, but maybe this will cover it.
Ah, the sweet nectar of Elanien Whiskey, I've had the pleasure of its company more times than I can uh, remember. Hic!
Unfortunately, with all these portal-hoppers, demand's gone through the roof. Whiskey's as scarce as a sober night at a dwarf's wedding.
That, and my pockets are as empty as a - hic - politician's promises. Procuring it myself is a challenge.
But a kind soul like you could surely spare a bottle for a hapless philosopher like me, right?
After all, my predicament is partially your fault. You came through that damn portal, didn't ya?
I've been told people stash bottles in crates and barrels. Worse comes to - hic - worse, maybe ya can trade for it?
Oh well, can't blame an old man for trying.
Well, shoot. Thought you had a generous heart.
No worries, mate. I'll just sit here, parched and whiskey-less.

name: Allergic to Duty

target.label: Infected Saxaul
target.landmark: Help Mabel avoid conscription by gathering allergens between the crossroads and lake north of the Central Fort to prove her condition

journal: Mabel Thorn Steppe, Central Fort. Help Mabel avoid conscription by gathering allergens between the crossroads and lake north of the Central Fort to prove her condition.
objectives: The Infected Saxauls are usually found around the outskirts of town.

npc: Safe travels, and thanks again!
Perfect! This is exactly what I needed. Get the epinephrine ready!
If I could show the recruiters how bad my allergies really are, they'd have to exempt me.
A-Achoo! Th-thank you... It's s-so hard to talk right now.
I can't thank you enough for your help.
Achoo! Oh, excuse me... These allergies are getting out of hand!
I need some Saxaul Dander to trigger a reaction they can't ignore.
Now I can prove my allergies and avoid conscription.
Here, take this charm as a token of my appreciation. I washed my hands... promise. Sniff.
I can barely speak without sneezing every other word. sniff
A-Achoo! O-oh... sniff Alright, I'll try to manage on my own.
Excuse me, sneeze maybe herbalism isn't so useless after all... Achoo!
It's so hard to t-talk... achoo! I understand if you can't help.
You're back! Were you able to find the Saxaul Dander?
Could you please bring me some samples from those infected Saxauls? Please! It's my only hope!

name: Allergy-Free Flora

goals.label: Collect specimens from Blue Desert Blooms

target.label: Blue Desert Blooms
target.landmark: Help SNZY-04 collect rare desert plant samples north along the northern coastline

goals.label: Collect specimens from Spiky Red Cacti

target.label: Spiky Red Cacti
target.landmark: Help SNZY-04 collect rare desert plant samples north along the northern coastline

target.label: Plane selection
target.landmark: Help SNZY-04 collect rare desert plant samples north along the northern coastline

goals.label: Use the bio-machine to process the samples

journal: SNZY-04 Steppe, Junkyard slums. Help SNZY-04 collect rare desert plant samples north along the northern coastline. Take the samples near the Hillside Well northwest of SNZY-04 , just short of the sandy beach and use the bio-machine.
objectives: Collect specimens from Blue Desert Blooms
Collect specimens from Spiky Red Cacti
SIMULATING SNEEZE- Please collect samples from the plants near the lake shores and use the bio-machine to process them. -SIMULATING COUGH-
goalTexts: Use the bio-machine to process the samples

npc: SIMULATING SNEEZE- Together, we may discover a plant that does not make me want to unplug myself.
Bring the collected fauna here and use my patented bio-machine to breed a bioengineered plant.
In exchange, I will provide you with some kind of reward.
SIMULATING COUGH- Could you gather specimens from specific desert flora near the lake shores?
I calculate a 98% probability that you can assist me.
SIMULATING SNEEZE- The irony is not lost on me.
SIMULATING COUGH- This must be some sort of flawed experiment.
SIMULATING SNEEZE- Why did my creators program me to adore plants yet suffer around them?
SIMULATING COUGH- Excellent! Though I question why my creators made me love plants and sneeze around them.
Affirmative! -SIMULATING SNEEZE- Your assistance is most appreciated.
-SIMULATING SNEEZE- Thank you! Perhaps this is some elaborate experiment.
Acknowledged! Together we'll overcome this illogical predicament.
-SIMULATING COUGH- Let's proceed before my systems overload from irony.
Acknowledged. I shall resume malfunctioning in solitude.
SIMULATING SIGH- I will continue to ponder my creators' questionable programming.
Understood. Returning to endless cycles of illogical sneezing.
-SIMULATING SHRUG- Perhaps this is all part of some perplexing experiment.
Very well. My irony subroutines remain fully operational.
Have you managed to create an allergy-free plant?
THIS IS THE GREATEST PLANT I HAVE EVER SEEN!
HAHA! I AM FREE TO ENJOY IT'S BEAUTY WITHOUT SUFFICATION.
Thank you, friend.
I'm sure you haven't caused the expansion of this desert to hasten.
Mostly because that's fake, by the way. Had nobody told you?

name: Amplify the Catch
journal: Ol' Fins Windstone Village, Windstone Lake's. Go the market in Windstone Village to the east.
objectives: Ssswim swiftly, adventurer... the goal awaitsss in the market, Windssstone Village. The fish gods grow impassssient. Bring the sssstolen microphone before they sssink into a sullen slumber.

npc: The whispers grow ressstlesss, but do as you mussst. Beware the shadowsss.
Ahhh, declinessss the call of the deep. The fishesss might... not be as forgiving.
You must ssssssteal. The fissssh godsss say it is jussst.
Annnnd sssso you know - I already asssssked. Not for ssssale.
Fetch this deviccce from the weaponss and armor market in Windssstone Village, and in return, you ssshall receive fresh fish. And another tasssk.
I need a deviccce called a 'microphone' to amplify the ancient hissstory. Perhapsss another favor afterwardsss.
The ancient fish godssss demand their whispers reach the ears of villagesss far and wide.
But, my voice is muffled by the wind and waves.
Note of... legal consssent? Sssure.
Your deedsss shall resound in the taleesss of the deep. But for your legal ssssummons, I need one more done.
CAN YOU HEAR ME NOW? Yessss.
Turning away, are we? The depths remember... alwayssss.
Ssssplendid, hero. Thisss microphone will echo with our ancient rhymeesss.
Ahhh... the seeker returnsss. How fare thee?
Ahh, blessssed catch, brave ssssoul. The fish gods... ssssshall be pleassssed.

name: An Ethical Appetite

target.label: Hyena
target.landmark: Kill some hyenas for Triton nearby

journal: Triton Steppe, near west Lost boats. Kill some hyenas for Triton nearby. He'll gather the meat later.
objectives: Kill Hyenas. Me find corpses later.

npc: Good, good! Kill Triton tasty meats!
Cravings annoy me. Need tasty snack to keep urges quiet.
You kill seven Hyenas, okay? Then Triton find and eat corpses as he returns to town!
Help Triton now, maybe Triton spare you later, heh heh!
You get Triton banished to army! But Triton clever, Triton make plan!
Yummy hyena meat! Crunchy, squishy, yes!
You no help Triton? Triton make you suffer!
Heh heh! You regret this, land-crawler!
Fine! Triton handle alone, then revenge!
You turn back on Triton? Bad choice!
Hmph! Triton not forget! Vengeance comes!
Need element of surprise to get revenge on village...
Me go south! Tell mean recruiter man mesa dead!
Good, good! Now Triton will stay strong! They all remember Triton soon!
Heh heh! Crunchy snacks! Triton happy now!
Triton wait long time! You kill squishy snacks?
No need to bring meat back! Will find...
AND THEN TRITON KILL EVERYONE!

name: Ancient Artifacts
journal: Old Man Zephyr Riverrise, City Center. Look closely around the area for these objects.
objectives: Zephy-morp! Chasa-chasa, cheese! Zingo-bongo, bongy-bong! Import! Caffi-mak! Caffi-mak! Pinky-mask, twerp-twerp! Final-frinal! Calendra!

npc: Zorp-zorp! You zingle-zangle. Morp!
Floop-floop, ploop-ploop. You help Zephy!
Zorp-zorp? Floop-done?
Gleep-glorp! Flee-plee!
Zizzy-zazzy! Here-go! Plim-plam!
Wha-ha! Zephy-happy! Take-take!
Zephy-morp! Chasa-chasa, cheese!
Zingo-bongo, bongy-bong! Import!
Fleep off!
Floop!
Zerp outta here, shluck!
Caffi-mak! Caffi-mak!
Pinky-mask, twerp-twerp!
Final-frinal! Calendra!

name: Artifact of Fury
journal: Zor Steppe, Fisher's camp. Find Slick Shady in Coal Village to the southwest of the Arid Canal.
objectives: Find actor man Slick Shady. I hear he southwest of Arid Canal in Coal Village.

npc: Zor sent you? Ah, the Artifact of Fury... sounds devastating!
Shh... keep your voice down.
Time runs out.
Seek out Slick Shady; you've met him before his assistance will be invaluable.
With his help, you might stand a chance against the perils that await.
It lies to the wast. Look for actor man. Slick Shady.
Cannot hesitate.
May the forces of equilibrium guide your path.
Remember, finding Slick Shady is your first step.
Your decision brings hope to all.
Should you change your mind, the balance awaits your aid.
You can restore the balance.
The anomalies drive Zor's people into rage, force us to attack Lightbearers. Allows Lightbearers to blame us...
The anomalies created by damaged artifact - the Artifact of Fury.
Tell you what, we'll make a superb team you go ahead, and I'll cover our rear.
Let's embark on this grand adventure... after you, of course!
Time to let you in... I'm not just an actor. I'm undercover, and I need your help.

name: The Scribe's Dilemma
journal: Griz Turner Goldenfield, near Artifact Shop Help Griz Turner replenish his artifact stock.
objectives: Bring me a Uncommon Immuno-Stimulating Implant, friend.
Bring me an Uncommon Mana Amulet, friend.

npc: Attaboy! I knew you had the spirit of a scavenger.
Remember, the amulet is a rare find. Keep those eyes peeled.
Still on the hunt for that Uncommon Mana Amulet?
No luck yet, eh? Keep at it!
I'll be darned, an outworlder actually found it!
Bring me a Uncommon Mana Amulet.
I'm going to make a fortune from this - uh, I mean we will.
Here's your totally equal cut of the profits!
These can be crafted, but one of such caliber may take a few tries.
D'Rek could probably help you with the recipe.
Got a customer, peculiar fella, got a bit of a thing for the unusual.
Got his eye on a Uncommon Mana Amulet, a trinket from some far-flung world.
Now, getting your hands on one ain't a walk in the park. But hey, where's the fun in easy, right?
This guy... he's not exactly throwing his coin around. But it's a fair deal, if you're up for the challenge.
Ah well, worth a shot. Maybe next time, eh?
No hard feelings, friend. Not everyone's cut out for scavenger work.
Maybe you'll change your mind? The offer's always open.

name: A Little Help from Friends
journal: Hilda Deeproot Westwind Woods, near Forest cabin Talk to Martha.
objectives: Who's that? Oh, you. I can't help with your imp problem. Go ask a friend. Maybe Martha?

npc: Perhaps your "Martha" in the village can guide you from here. You will need to be much stronger to defeat them.
Well, who'd think that Zugg would try framin' you for murder and arson?
Well, I could've. He tries stuff like this once a week. He usually gets away with it too.
Fortunately, you've made a couple friends who will help out - go talk to Ratchet and Butch.
I think they'll help you get geared up.
There's also this very hot loner on the edge of town - Dr. Bloom. I think his offworld tech could give you an edge.
Do whatever those three need, and you'll be ready to chew some imp ass!
Uh... I didn't mean it like that, but I stand by it.
You re gonna have to get saucy with this one - those imps will fry you if you aren t ready.
It s your arrest record, kid.
Alright, I m not gonna be your lawyer.
See you in 5-9 years when they let you out.

name: Making a Withdrawal
journal: Butch O'Hara Goldenfield, near Pig farm Head west to the Goldenfield bank.
objectives: Hey kid, you talk to Oswald yet? He's the only guy in town who will have the gear you want. Hopefully you won't have to rob him for it, but be open to it.

npc: And hey, remember - don't you tell anyone about my secret ingredient, or you're getting ground up next.
So, you want that piece of fire-graded gear from the vault, huh? Well, I have a little task for you in return.
Turns out, the vault you re interested in belongs to one of the imps you're after. But before I help you with that, I need you to do something.
There's a fellow named Guru David who's account is... in the black.
He used a loan to purchase some roleplay masks. Retrieve those masks, and I ll grant you access to that gear.
The masks are specific: a Green Mask, one with Orange Horns, and a Pink Bird Mask. It's essential you get the right ones.
Oh, and if you can... please wash them before bringing them back here.
So, truth is, only certain armor can stop those imps from burning your cajones off.
Ain't going to give you mine, but you need a helmet with some fire grading.
Saw Oswald putting some gear like that away in the vault. In fact, I stole some of it.
Maybe you can convince him to give you some.

name: Beacon of Confidence
journal: Zeke "Gearhead" Thompson Steppe, Northsteppe fort. Find Roddy west at the Western Fort to share the news.
objectives: Whenever you get a chance, mosey on over and let Roddy know that beacon's buzzin' again.

npc: Now our volunteers can face peril with, uh, renewed gusto! Immortality does have a nice ring to it!
Oh, the beacon is shining again? Splendid! Just as I orchestrated... in a manner of speaking!
Ah, a traveler approaches!
Suit yourself, friend. We'll be here soaking up the action if you change your mind.
No worries, partner. Whenever you feel like it, just swing on by.
Well now, ain't that just fine and dandy! Much obliged, friend.
All righty then, take your time. The warfront's keepin' things interesting anyhow.
Tor! Do you hear me!?
Great, this might just turn things around.
With that load off his mind, I bet he'll be rarin' to rally the folks against whatever's comin' our way.
Reckon you could mosey on over to the outpost and share the good news with him?
Roddy's been sweatin' bullets about leadin' those volunteers. But hearin' the beacon's up and runnin' might just settle his nerves.
Well, looks like you got that revive beacon hummin' again. Mighty fine job there, partner!
With this turn of events, I'll lead us to um glorious victory!
I think with everyone feeling a bit bolder, perhaps we will agree to work with the other forts.
Well, I suppose I'll send a letter.

name: Beacon of Hope

target.label: Camp Torch Fuse
target.landmark: Collect fuses on the island northeast of the Fishers camp and scattered around Smugglers Island and the several small islands that surround it

goals.label: Light the signal torches along the riverbank

journal: Kira Steppe, Fisher's camp. Collect fuses on the island northeast of the Fishers camp and scattered around Smugglers Island and the several small islands that surround it. Afterward, light torches along the beach, starting east of Fishers Camp, down to the north of Silent Village.
objectives: Light the signal torches along the riverbank
Ancients scorn you. Bring funeral torch fuses from islands to east.

npc: You dare show face? Task done?
Your death long overdue. Begone!
You unworthy. I end you myself!
We need five beacon parts. Bring them to me, then install them on the beacon.
Grah'tok sees you; flee now!
Do this, and perhaps my people see hope again.
We no want you here. Better you dead.
Bring the parts quickly. Time is against us.
Ancients curse you! Leave or perish.
Perhaps I no kill you... yet.
Then light beacons along water.
Is the beacon ready for us?
Bring funeral torch fuses from islands to east.
If you no die, will repent in other ways.
Take this Meovian Trust Token. It's a symbol of our gratitude.
Our people have a chance now. For that, you have my thanks.
Good. Maybe you not so bad.
Do not ruin for me with words.

name: Bearing Light
journal: Barnaby Thorn Steppe, Great steppe stronghold. Assist The Guardinator and Jennifer around the stronghold. Terrace was sent east to the Northeast Fort, you’ll find him there.
objectives: Jennifer and The Guardinator are around the stronghold. Terrace is to the east at the Northeast Fort, just southeast of the Northsteppe Fort.
People around here might hate you, but they aren't ones to turn down free help. Go ask around any see what you can do for 'em, alright? Nothing sexual though - and trust me, they'll ask.

npc: Dedicated enough to move to the front...
The truth is, the lightbearers don't need the help of the other forts to win this war!
Now that you're one of us, I'll give you your first assignment.
Good. I expect great things from you.
Very well. The Lightbearers have no time for hesitation.
If you won't step up, others surely will.
I see. Perhaps you're not as committed as I thought.
If we can aid them by completing their tasks, perhaps they'll finally see our true potential.
Every moment I strive to prove my troop's worth to the Lightbearers.
They demand unwavering dedication, and I won't rest until they've acknowledged us.
Some of our key members; Jennifer Davyna, The Guardinator, and Terrace Tenstone seek assistance.
Very well. Show us the mettle you're made of.
Excellent. Prove your worth to the Lightbearers.
Ah, you've returned.
Have you fulfilled the tasks set before you?
Well done. You've proven your dedication.
We must not fail. Their recognition is paramount.
I need your help to undertake this mission with utmost seriousness.
Jennifer and The Guardinator can be found around the stronghold. Terrace was called east to the Northeast Fort.
May our alliance illuminate the path ahead.
Maybe if you help some of them out, we can get them to vouch for you with Zugg!
Together, we can demonstrate our commitment and earn our place among the Lightbearers.

name: Beauty in the Mud

target.label: Turtle
target.landmark: Head straight east to the lake

journal: Zorbul Z'nar Goldenfield, Distant Farm. Head straight east to the lake. Search around the shore for eggs.
objectives: Zorbul need eggs from water dinosaurs. Face depends on turtle egg yolk! Find around lake or from turtles. Take their babies!

npc: Stars dim more, Zorbul wait longer.
Zorbul's heart, it cracks like broken moon!
Zorbul glow like Pretty Woman! Face already burning!
You gather for Zorbul? Need turtle egg for mud mask. Yes, yes!
Lake straight east. Go. Go. Go.
Oh friend, Zorbul's face once shiny, now like old potato!
Now Zorbul bootiful enough for jury stand. Take note!
Good, good! Zorbul trust you get eggs!
Zorbul see you come back! Egg found?
Why turn back? No shiny glory together now.
Eggy find! Zorbul feel young stars again!
Zorbul grateful, here gift for amazing help!
Zorbul hears whisper of inside turtle egg, make face shiny-shiny again!
Hunt rare turtle eggs around lake. Or kill turtle for egg.

name: Beggars Can't Be Roasters

target.label: Roasted Carcass
target.landmark: Hunt birds around Goldenfield for an animal carcass

journal: Paddy O'Lantern Goldenfield, Walfare Kitchen Hunt birds around Goldenfield for an animal carcass.
objectives: Remember, lad, we need an animal carcass, onion, -hic-, salt and er, mushroom... red mushroom! That'll cook up a damn fine meal. I don't give a shit what ole Marthy says!

npc: Ah, -hic- back already? How s the hunt been?
Yer a fine one! This belly of whiskey is running wild.
Aye, that's the stuff! You did it, lad!
Let s sit and enjoy this by the campfire. Few things in life are as good as a roasted carcass shared with a friend.
Thank ye, lad. Ye ve made a drunk, old man very happy.
I'll -hic- say, that last bottle really spot the hit.
The ole' belly's rumbl -hic- ling though. Ole Marthy here won't fix me my favorite.
What's say you give me another -hic- hand.
My ma use to always makes us a nice roasted carcass.
It was free meat, ya judgy -hic- bastards.
Think you can fix me a plate?
Aw, ya gonna leave an old man hangin ? Suit yerself.
No worries, lad. Maybe next time you'll have a change of heart.
Well, that's a damn shame. But I get it, everyone's busy these days.

name: Behind Enemy Lines
journal: Trevor Barlow Windreach Woods, Fort. Search the captain's trunks around the fort for his Calming Leaves.
objectives: You found that stack of paper yet? Time's ticking and dark magic isn't forgiving. Remember, the longer you take, the more power that artifact can gain. We can t let that happen.
You found those leaves yet? Time's ticking and dark magic isn't forgiving.

npc: So while you were gone, Alaric turned that cursed little artifact you made over to the captain. He just wanted to suck up, despite me telling him the thing was cursed.
We need to find his notes to determine where he might have gone. I hate to admit it, but your skills could be useful here.
Themis is inconsolable, and he's preventing me from properly doing my duty.
Look around the fort, find any notes the captain took before leaving, and bring them to Tharkon - I'm a bit tied up.
Walk away then. Hope you're ready to reap the consequences.
Fine, leave. Just remember, dark magic doesn't care about excuses.
You're seriously leaving us hanging? Good luck not getting cursed.
I suppose I'll need your help to stop this...
Lo and behold, the captain is possessed and running into the woods 10 minutes later.
They're trying to destabilize us. Typical Quaddari tactics, always aiming to sow chaos.
The notes suggest the captain is being drawn to a ritual site deeper into the woods, likely a strategic move by those Quaddari scum.
So, it s as I suspected. This artifact has Quaddari sabotage all over it.
Did anyone see you coming here? Show me what you've got, quickly!
We're dancing with evil shit here. Grab the leaves, pronto.
Perfect. These leaves should help me calm the Captain down.
While you were gathering these, I found out through a source what is happening and why they targeted the Captain.
Look around the fort, search the captain's trunks for any of those Calming Leaves, and bring them to Tharkon - I'm a bit tied up.
We need to find the Calming Leaves that he crushes up into powder... it might simmer him down a little. I hate to admit it, but your skills could be useful here.

name: Beneath the Surface

goals.label: Collect Parts for the mine car

target.label: Parts for the mine cart
target.landmark: Find a lamp and mine cart parts in the abandoned settlement to the northwest

journal: Alexander Stones Westwind Woods, Old Quarry overlooking platform. Find a lamp and mine cart parts in the abandoned settlement to the northwest. Varkus will be waiting by the Westwind Sawmill.
objectives: Collect Parts for the mine car

Those clue fragments in the mine are pieces of the grand puzzle. Don't let the lizard overlords distract you! Keep digging! Get that mine cart in working order and get down that shaft! Grab a lamp nearby and go north to the Abandoned Settlement and find those parts for the cart.

npc: No worries, sheep. Keep grazing while the wolves run free.
So you're afraid of a little truth, huh? The powers that be would love you.
The powers that be don't want us to find this, but we're not gonna let them win. Dive into those mines, gather the fragments, and we'll crack the code to a revelation beyond their wildest nightmares.
We're talking life-changing secrets here, not just your grandma's jewelry.
I need someone with guts to unearth the truth. And whatever artifact fragment is down there.
Listen up, fellow truth-seeker! I've concluded there's a conspiracy wrapped in mystery buried deep within those old mines between the sawmill and the quarry.
No...? Maybe you will later today...
You haven't happened to hear or see a woman screaming through the woods, did you?
Never heard of it. And as a treasure hunter, I've heard of anything worth finding.
Alien language? Ancient artifact?
Suit yourself, sleeper. Go back to your comfortable lies.
You building something?
And you are...?
Alright, prep yourself for a fuckin' journey into the unknown!
Gather a lamp from the abandoned buildings around here, then head north up to the Abandoned Settlement to collect parts for the mine cart and get your ass over to Varkus.

name: Blitz's Escape Clause

goals.label: Find Blitz's contract and bring it to Blitz

journal: Blitz Hyperstorm Steppe, Central Fort. Follow Blitz's trail of crushed cans south into the forest. His contract should be at the end of the trail.
objectives: Follow the trail of crushed Zap Juice cans it's a real live wire! Clock's ticking faster than a caffeine buzz! Gotta find that contract!
goalTexts: Find Blitz's contract and bring it to Blitz

npc: I CAN'T GO TO WAR! I'M THE FACE OF ZAP JUICE, NOT A SOLDIER!
YOU'RE BACK ALREADY?! ZAP-TASTIC! DID YOU FIND MY CONTRACT?!
AWESOME! YOU'RE A REAL ZAP CHAMP!
BUT I'M BUZZING WITH PROBLEMS HERE!
Now I can AVOID the WAR! Here, TAKE these ZAP JUICES!
HEY! YOU LOOK LIKE SOMEONE WHO CAN MOVE FAST!
MY COMPANY CONTRACT PROVES I'M EXEMPT, BUT I DROPPED IT IN THE WOODS!
FOLLOW THE TRAIL OF EMPTY ZAP JUICE CANS TOWARD RIVERRISE AND FIND MY CONTRACT!
ZAPTASTIC! You FOUND my CONTRACT!
HURRY! BEFORE THEY REALIZE I'M GONE AND DRAG ME TO THE FRONT LINES!
NO WAY! YOU CAN'T JUST ZAP OUT ON ME!
WHAT?! YOU'RE LEAVING ME SPARKLESS?!
YOU'RE REALLY GONNA LET ME SHORT-CIRCUIT?!
COME ON, DON'T PULL THE PLUG ON ME NOW!
ZAP IT ALL! THIS IS A TOTAL POWER OUTAGE!
I SIGNED UP FOR THE WAR BY MISTAKE TOO MUCH ZAP JUICE!
I THINK I'll have one myself...
Ahh... Wait... Is that gunfire? Oh no... I'm still in the warzone!
Thank you! Time's ticking, so please hurry!

name: Rest in Peace

target.label: Scrag
target.landmark: Retrieve bones from around Goldenfield for Mortimer Gloomsworth to bury in the cemetery

journal: Mortimer Gloomsworth Goldenfield, near Cemetery Retrieve bones from around Goldenfield for Mortimer Gloomsworth to bury in the cemetery.
objectives: Rummage around for remains from the lifeless bodies scattered in the forests circling Goldenfield. Those bones are the key to some proper burial rites that keep the dead quiet and satisfied.

npc: Appreciate it. The dead'll thank you.
The dead ain't patient, y'know?
How's the bone hunt going?
Got those remains yet?
Good work. These bones'll rest easy now.
Those bones are the key to some proper burial rites that keep my impulses satisfied.
Plus, here's a little reward - fell off the bodies, so I figure you've earned it.
I try not to give into my undead impulses, but sometimes it's tantalizing.
Help me put some souls to rest, and perhaps I can avoid snacking.
There's no peace in death if the body ain't put to rest.
Suit yourself. More food for me, I guess
Alright, better hope I don't come knocking at night.
If you want help from the dead, you have to help them first.
Rummage around for some remains from the lifeless bodies scattered in the forests circling Goldenfield.
No peace for me, at least... those bodies are... tempting.

name: Brave New World - Baking Independence

target.label: Wheat Flour
target.landmark: Find 3 Wheat Seeds in barrels, crates or around the fields

journal: Martha "Big Mamma" Potterson Goldenfield, near Welfare kitchen. Find 3 Wheat Seeds in barrels, crates or around the fields.
objectives: Bring me some Wheat Flour. Look around, darling. It's harvest season, and you might find some leftover Wheat in the fields around Goldenfield town or in crates and sacks. You can process 3 handfuls of Wheat seeds into Flour at my Cooking bench.

npc: Don't forget that Wheat Flour, hon. We can't bake bread without it.
How's the flour hunt going, darling?
Remember, the fresher the flour, the better the bread.
You're a peach, darling. Let's get to baking!
Bring me some Wheat Flour. Look around, darling. It's harvest season, and you might find some leftover Wheat in the fields around Goldenfield town or in crates and sacks.
Well, I'll be a monkey's uncle! You did it. Here's your reward.
You can process 3 handfuls of Wheat seeds into Flour at my Cooking bench.
You took to that quick. I bet you could learn some other skills too!
There's plenty a' locals around here who the government pays to teach.
Why don't you hunt 'em down?
There's a lumberjack and a miner out by the gas station.
An alchemist back behind the bank.
And a workshop with a couple of weapon crafters...
By law, each newcomer is supposed to get several loaves of bread. That's the Newcomer Welfare Program of Elan for ya.
But honey, I'm too busy cooking for everyone.
So how' bout I teach you to cook some bread? Be a little more independent, ya know?
You'll need to bring me some Wheat Flour, which you can make yourself from 3 handfuls of Wheat seeds.
I'll add a little bit of my special spice, and give ya' one hell of a recipe!
Zugg told you to come over here? That dang rat... always passin' off trouble.
Oh, that's a shame. Hope you don't go hungry tonight.
No worries, hon. If you change your mind, you know where to find me.
Well, alright. Guess you'll be missing out on some tasty homemade bread.

name: Breaking the Chains

goals.label: Take the toy out of the hive.

journal: Marigold Thornbuckle Goldenfield, Edge farm. Grab the smoker from near 'Lil Timmy and head north across the field to the hive. Use the smoker on the hive, retrieve the bottle and bring it back.
objectives: Did you get my toy? What are you doing? And definitely don't bring it to my momma! Whatever she says is wrong.
goalTexts: Take the toy out of the hive.

npc: I won't forget this, pal.
Bad move, buddy.
I keep a vewy special magic toy in a bottle in the hive across the field.
And don't twy anything funny with it - if you take it to Marigold, I'll find you and slit your throat.
Why don't you go get it for me while I take a widdle nap?
I mean I'll be vewy sad and cwy! Take the smoker with you, you'll need to use it on the hive so you don't get stingeded.
Wow you did so good, mawby I can twust you with a special task!
You've given me the chance I needed to take back my life, and I can't thank you enough.
I'll play along with Timmy for now, but soon during one of his naps... that boy won't wake up.
You're making a big mistake for a gwon up.
I'll break this container open - now get that thing the fuck outta here!
Yaaaay! Now hurry up!
What the... is this how he was controlling us?

name: Bridging Realms with Friends

target.label: Grizelda Magic Mushroom
target.landmark: Find Pastor Fazool in Reverie Hollow to the southwest

target.label: Pappy Rick Portal Juice
target.landmark: Find Pastor Fazool in Reverie Hollow to the southwest

target.label: Quaddari Portal Tech
target.landmark: Find Pastor Fazool in Reverie Hollow to the southwest

journal: Driscoll-99 Westwind Woods, Old Portal. Find Pastor Fazool in Reverie Hollow to the southwest. Quest-O-Tron is southeast of the Hunter's Lodge and Grizelda is far east, just southwest of the Abandoned Railway Station.
objectives: Oh, just a friendly reminder! To unlock realms, first a visit to Pastor Fazool in Reverie Hollow. Delightful chap talks to ancient entities! Next on our jolly list is Grizelda in Windreach Woods. And don't forget the ever-charming QUEST-O-TRON in Windreach Woods. Remember, you'll need to 'earn' their trust. Whatever that means. Survival and happiness await!

npc: Oh, turning down epic adventures fueled by blood and powerful items? How sad!
Ah, ain't cross-dimensional travel grand? Off you go! Time's a-ticking, and portals don't open themselves!
Lastly, have a chat with QUEST-O-TRON, also in Windreach Woods. He s a riot.
Then, toodle on over to Grizelda in Windreach Woods. Watch your step she's caused people to... disappear.
First up, Pastor Fazool in Reverie Hollow. Can't miss him, he's the one with the ecstatic congregation.
Each one holds onto an item of immense power. You'll need to amuse, bemuse, or outright confuse them to get what we need.
I've got some names for you: Pastor Fazool, the chipper cult leader; Grizelda, the charmingly deranged mushroom lady; and who could forget, QUEST-O-TRON, our sarcastic survival guide.
Lucky for us, there are a few cross-dimensional friends who dabble in things a bit more powerful and by powerful, I mean dangerous!
Oh, the blood samples just didn't cut it. We'll need something more... potent to turn on the portal.
Now we just complete one final ritual...Seriously! I promise!
Reward time!
Ah, the fruits of madness and mayhem. You ve earned every apple!
Declining my quest? No worries! I'm sure the dark forces will enjoy your idleness.
Ah, the sweet scent of impending doom and inter-realm adventures. I can hardly wait!
Well done, intrepid traveler! Who knew that blood, mushrooms, and madness could be so rewarding?
Oh dear, say no to excitement and yes to... boredom? Delightful choice!

name: Brotherly Loss

goals.label: Get proof of death of brother Gideon

journal: Gideon Stroud Steppe, North of the shore of Thousand Bones. Follow the clues from the note and head toward the western coast, past Coal Village. Alaric was last scene in this area.
objectives: Please, follow the clues I've given you. They should lead to where he was last seen, southwest of Coal Village.
goalTexts: Get proof of death of brother Gideon

npc: Please, you have to help me. My brother's gone missing, and I fear something terrible has happened.
He ventured north, near that old monument, and hasn't returned.
Those creatures up there... I just know they're involved.
I've got some clues that might lead us to him. Will you help me find out what happened?
And keep this under wraps, will you? No need to stir the pot more than it already is.
Thank you so much. I can't do this alone.
With your help, maybe we'll find him.
I understand. It's a lot to ask.
Please, if you change your mind, I'll be here.
Alright. I hope you reconsider.
A Lightbearer's bayonet in his chest and an empty artifact box? Fantastic. Just what I needed today.
Alright, I'll handle this mess. Here's your reward.
So, you found Gideon's brother? I see.
Well, if it isn't my favorite messenger. Got something for me?

name: Built to Extract

target.label: Note hint 01
