target.landmark: Look for the missing list and the extractor parts in the swamps on the southwestern fringe of the Creeping Marsh and in the houses on Prison Island

goals.label: Find list in the houses on Prison Island

target.label: Note hint 02
target.landmark: Look for the missing list and the extractor parts in the swamps on the southwestern fringe of the Creeping Marsh and in the houses on Prison Island

target.label: Dead Musketeer
target.landmark: Look for the missing list and the extractor parts in the swamps on the southwestern fringe of the Creeping Marsh and in the houses on Prison Island

target.label: Fragment 1
target.landmark: Look for the missing list and the extractor parts in the swamps on the southwestern fringe of the Creeping Marsh and in the houses on Prison Island

target.label: Fragment last item
target.landmark: Look for the missing list and the extractor parts in the swamps on the southwestern fringe of the Creeping Marsh and in the houses on Prison Island

journal: Dwarfaton Ironchek Windstone Village, Windstone blacksmith yard Look for the missing list and the extractor parts in the swamps on the southwestern fringe of the Creeping Marsh and in the houses on Prison Island.
objectives: Look for the list and extractor parts in the swamps southwest Creeping Marsh. And check the houses on Prison Island, just in case. Then drag your ass back here.
goalTexts: Find list in the houses on Prison Island

npc: Back from the muck, eh? Let's see if you actually hauled in the parts.
Bring the lot back here, and I'll weld us a soul-sucking gun fit for a werewolf.
Fine, but don't come crying when the saddle bites back.
Suit yourself - rust won't polish itself.
Good. Knew you had the spine for it.
Decline 3
Give 'em here. Time to bolt, weld, and pray this contraption doesn't explode.
Ha! You really dragged it all back bits and swamp stench included.
Blast it, the saddle's still foul.
Somewhere in that muck you'll find a tattered list of every part we'll need to build the extractor.
Why the fool marked two spots, gods only know, but you'd better check 'em both.
Front shows the guts of it, and the back's stamped with two words "swamps" and "Prison Island."
Dug through a century of rust and found this half-rotten blueprint for an Essence Extractor.
I need werewolf essence to burn the corruption out but I can't bottle that stuff with bare hands.
Stand clear once I'm done, we'll have a soul-sucking gun ready for that mangy werewolf.

name: Burnt Pages
journal: Seek the beast who speaks with trees
objectives: Discover the beast who speaks with trees on the island.

name: A Butcher's Lesson

target.label: Cosmetic Fat
target.landmark: Head to the Skylight Grasslands and Sunweave Glade to find boarmammoths

journal: Butch O'Hara Goldenfield, near Pig farm Head to the Skylight Grasslands and Sunweave Glade to find boarmammoths.
objectives: Gather Animal Fat and turn it into Cosmetic Fat at the crafting bench. If you have trouble with wolves, try for turtles or boars.

npc: Gather Animal Fat and turn it into Cosmetic Fat at the crafting bench.
How's that butchering going, champ?
Don't forget, Animal Fat becomes Cosmetic Fat.
What's the hold-up? Afraid of a little fat?
Great! Remember, hunt for Wolves, Boars or Turtles and butcher them.
Well done! Now, see how that wasn't so hard? And look what you've learned!
You still have one more test before you can call yourself a real butcher though...
You wanna learn about butchery?
Out here, survivin' ain't just livin'. It means knowin' your way around a butcher's blade too.
Wolves, Boars and even Turtles - they've all got Animal Fat. Well, in most cases.
Take down those animals, then butcher 'em to collect Animal Fat. Process this fat into Cosmetic Fat on the crafting bench - ain't nothing to it
Wolves mostly stick to the Westwind Woods. As for Boars, you can find them in the woods too, but they also wander around the Sunweave Glade and Skylight Grasslands.
Turtles? They're just about anywhere if you got the eye for it. But not all carcasses are fit for butchering though - that's a skill you'll be learning on the go.
If you re pretty green, I would try to stick to boars for now.
Backing out? Well, the wilds aren't for everyone.
No trouble. Just remember, survival skills are key out here.
Changed your mind? It's alright, not everyone's cut out for the hunt.

name: Byte's Calling Card Investigation

target.label: Calling Cards
target.landmark: Search the bar for the hidden cards

journal: Cyra "Byte" Kessler Riverrise, River view. Search the bar for the hidden cards.
objectives: So, uh, remember to check all around the bar. For the merch, of course. Any unusual-looking cards might be it! And, totally just for Velvet Vipers stuff. These calling cards could be hidden anywhere... you know, for fans like us to find.

npc: Thank you! And if anyone asks, you're just helping a fellow Velvet Vipers fan, right?
So, uh, did you find those calling cards? Just for my collection, of course.
Whoa, you found them! Uh, I mean, yay, free merch!
Ah, these are absolutely... uh, crucial for my, eh, collection.
An Ace, a King of Spades... and this one just has a snake on it?
Wow! As a huge fan, I like... totally know what these mean!
So, the Velvet Vipers, right? They're kinda... ahem, amazing. Really into them, you know?
I heard they might have left some calling cards around this bar in Riverrise. If you could, uh, find a few for me, that'd be just great.
Honestly, it's just for the merch! I'm, uh, a huge fan. But, hey, these cards might also be... useful. Whaddya say?
Listen, I didn't want to scare you, but something really dangerous is at stake here. We need those cards.
You don't understand; it's more than just fan stuff. People's lives might depend on it.
Please reconsider. This isn't just for fun. If we don't act, something really bad could happen.

name: Call to Arms
journal: Assist Biff "Dozer" Bromley in preparing the recruits around the Central Fort for the upcoming expedition.
objectives: Please, assist the three recruits and then report back to me.
Make your way up north to the Steppes and find Biff "Dozer" Bromley at the Central Fort.

npc: Up north, there's talk of untamed frontiers and treasures waiting to be claimed by those bold enough.
Our recruits are eager but inexperienced they could greatly benefit from the guidance of someone like you.
What's the matter? You wanna live forever or something?
Ah, you're back! How did it go with the recruits?
Thanks to you, they're much better prepared for the challenges ahead.
Here's a token of our gratitude. You've truly made a difference.
With your help, we're one step closer to safeguarding Elan! Now sign here!
You want to help - come up to World's End fort, and get the other recruits ready.
Their names are Blitz, Mabel and Lenny. They're... they're trying. They just may need a little kick in the ass. You'll find them around the Central Fort.
With talents like yours, why settle for the ordinary? Join us, and the rewards will be more than worth your while.
Outstanding work! I can already see the determination in their eyes.
Welcome back! Did the recruits benefit from your guidance?
You look like someone hungry for real challenges perhaps even the kind that lead to glory and riches.
That's a pity. These lands need heroes now more than ever.
I understand. Not everyone can answer the call.
I can tell you're someone who doesn't back down from a challenge. This could be the adventure that defines you.
Good to see you! Have you helped the recruits?
Ah, I suspected you'd recognize a golden opportunity when you see one. Together, we'll achieve greatness beyond imagining.
After all, opportunities like this don't come around every day. You should just take a moment and sign up with us... 3 hots and a cot! Plus glory!
Well met, friend! Just the kind of seasoned adventurer I've been searching for.

name: A Spark in the Dark
journal: Maxwell Sterling Dusktide Forest, near Wheat tent Check the pile of wood near the tent.
objectives: Go on, fetch the wood. It's right around camp, and you won't have to watch out for wildlife here. This is the safe part... for now.

npc: Go on, fetch the wood. It's right around camp, and you won't have to watch out for wildlife here.
Try not to give yourself a splinter..
How's the wood hunt going, newbie?
Don't forget! 3 pieces. Not 2. Not 4. 3.
Haven't frozen to death yet, huh?
Well, look who's not an idiot!
Here's how to make a campfire, and some matches. You'll need 'em.
So, you want to learn about survival, huh? Lucky for you, the government pays me to teach you how.
See a monster, think it's too strong? Run. If it chases you, run faster.
Forget about quests, honor, and revenge. Around here, your biggest achievement is making it through the day with all your limbs attached.
And gear up. You wouldn't believe the number of naked, brave individuals I've seen charging at monsters only to end up as a snack.
Their stupid bravado is your gain - corpses make for good looting.
Now fetch some wood for a fire. It's scattered around camp - grab three pieces.
Usually, you'd get wood from logging those magic trees around. But that isn't your problem yet.
Your funeral, pal.
Missed your chance. Cold nights are a killer. Literally.
Huh. Guess you're not the survival type.

name: Catch of the Day

goals.label: Collect Secret Snack bottles in the Tavern

target.label: "Secret Snack" bottles
target.landmark: The tavern is in the northest of Windstone Village

journal: Thorin Toastbeard Windstone Village, Tavern. The tavern is in the northest of Windstone Village.
objectives: Collect Secret Snack bottles in the Tavern
You pick up those blue bottles yet? Don't need someone else findin' 'em. Remember how your ex was always full of surprises? Yeah, these bottles are like that, but less emotional damage.

npc: Fine, but if Glubgrath finds out, I'm telling him it was your idea.
Are you sure? Glubgrath's not one for forgiveness, you know.
Trust me, the last guy who did... let's just say he was up to his knees in shit.
Now, a piece of crucial advice: do not, under any circumstances, open those bottles.
Glubgrath's snacks are packed in special blue glass bottles with a stamp. Why? Who knows? Maybe he thinks it adds flavor.
Would you care to ssswim? The water is most inviting... and the party will start ssssoon.
You have ssserved me well, mortal. Here is your note of legal consssent.
Hah, good luck avoiding Glubgrath's rage. You'll need it.
Yesss... you return with the bottlesss. Excellent.
You return... so soon, with the offerings, er, snacks?
And remind Glubgrath to keep some... leftovers. I need 'em for my next dish.
Alright! This'll be a breeze, as long as you keep your sense of smell in check.

name: Catching Dreams

target.label: Spiders
target.landmark: Search for spiders south in the Ripplecrop Fields

journal: Pax Voss Riverrise, Bench behind the alchemy. Search for spiders south in the Ripplecrop Fields
objectives: Remember, the spiders exist where shadows whisper secrets. Find them before fate plays its cruel hand.

npc: Come closer... don't be shy. We've seen things, dark things about Thistlebrook. Curious? We can tell you how it all ends.
Hurry now, you wouldn't want to keep Ophelia waiting. Bad things happen when people wait too long... dark things.
Sweet Ophelia, my precious sister, loves her pretty jewelry. She wants to finish her dream catcher but needs spider legs. Surely someone of your stature could collect spider legs.
Ah, you actually returned? I must say, I didn't foresee that.
Ophelia will smile, for now... but smiles can hide so many secrets.
Thistlebrook's truth? It's darker than you think. Are you sure you can handle it?
Here's your reward, not that it will keep the darkness away for long.
Take this knowledge and beware. Predictions have a nasty way of becoming reality.
In return, we'll share Thistlebrook's tragic comedy. Such a fateful twist, you'll laugh or maybe cry. Tick-tock, the clock is ticking...
If you choose to walk away, who knows what fate awaits? Ophelia might cry rivers tonight.
Bad things have a way of coming back around. Just like us, always watching.
Leave now if you wish, but remember, darkness follows those who hesitate.
Oh, the shadows love a missed opportunity. Ophelia won't be the only one disappointed.

name: Mission Imp- possible: Off- world Chips
journal: Dr. Orion Bloom Goldenfield, Tent near the road. Search around the lake to the north west by Windstone Village.
objectives: Remember, if you bump into an astronaut, give them these coordinates.

npc: Any luck finding my crew?
Remember, if you find an astronaut, give them the coordinates!
Thank you, friend, for this gift of hope. I can't express how grateful I am.
Great! You're a real lifesaver. Or... a life-deliverer?
You...you found Dr. Bloom? You have no idea how much that means to me!
Just knowing he's alive, it's like...like finding hope in the middle of this cosmic mess.
This gives me the strength to keep searching for the others.
Remember, if you bump into an astronaut, give them my coordinates.
So, the creatures that chased off my crew were "Imps" ?
Crazy. I don't know much about their metabolism, but its possible cross-dimensional exposure could make them pretty dangerous.
I can make something to counter it, but I'll need help from the rest of the crew.
It's time to track them down. I have a feeling they might be scattered around.
The challenge here is not knowing who or what we'll find - every clue and every bit of information helps.
Now, let's see how I can help you out...
An imp? Using my plants? That's despicable...
Unfortunately, my crew were the real scientists.
I'm stranded here, with no way to navigate this alien landscape or reach out to them.
The isolation, the uncertainty - it's maddening. Every moment is a test of sanity.
My crew, they're not just colleagues - they're my friends. The thought of them lost and terrified... it's too much.
It feels like an eternity since the crash. Time and space have lost all meaning.
But hey, there's no rush, right? Except each ticking second feels like a punch in the gut.
Well, that and the fact you're wanted for arson and need to clear your name.
Word travels fast, you know?
So, if you come across my crew, pass them my coordinates. Maybe we can solve your problem.
I understand. It's a lot to ask. I'll just... I'll just keep waiting.
Sure, no problem. Space is vast and time is... well, I have plenty of it here.
That's okay. I'll keep holding on to hope. It's all I have right now.

name: Check Yourself Before You D'Rek Yourself

target.label: Boar
target.landmark: Search the nearby forests for boars and leaf monsters

target.label: Leaf Monster
target.landmark: Search the nearby forests for boars and leaf monsters

target.label: Near Drek
target.landmark: Search the nearby forests for boars and leaf monsters

journal: D'rek Goldenfield, Construction Craft bench Search the nearby forests for boars and leaf monsters. Collect the iron rivets from nearby Ratchet.
objectives: We need thick hide from a boar and chitin from a leaf monster. Got that? Both should be found easily in the woods. Then grab those rivets over by Ratchet.

npc: Ah, you've got everything! Splendid!
How's the gathering going?
Great, let's make this masterpiece together!
Thanks, friend. Every bit of help gets us closer to perfection.
Now we can start crafting this Impeccable Armor.
Thank you for your help. Here's your part of the deal.
Keep safe out there, adventurer!
Alright, traveler, I'm working on a project unlike any other you've seen. This Impenetrable Armor will make the wearer nearly invincible.
But for that, I need some specific materials. I'm looking for the thick hide of a boar and the chitin from a leaf monster.
These elements will provide the perfect blend of defense capabilities and flexibility. Can you help me gather these?
Not interested? That's a bummer.
No worries, I'll find another willing hand.
Maybe next time, then. Stay safe out there.
Also, grab those iron rivets on the ground next to my workbench... I'll use those to fasten the armor together.

name: Chuckling Intervention

target.label: Veins T1,2,3
target.landmark: Search old bushes for rare Chuckleberry leaves

journal: Bob Thurkle Riverrise, Scene. Search old bushes for rare Chuckleberry leaves.
objectives: Don't forget to bring those Chuckleberry leaves, man. We need them to get Cherry back on track. Check in those old bushes!
goalTexts: Return Chuckleberry Leaves to Zazz Blorb

npc: Hell yeah! Let's rock this.
Those Chuckleberry leaves Cherry used to inhale like candy might help us figure this out. If the guy on stage isn't Cherry, we'll know when he reacts to them.
Cherry's back in the game, right? Heh, yeah... Cherry's totally back.
Oh... no, no, no! Chuckleberry leaves?! Why did it have to be those berries?
Look, I can't take these I'm allergic, seriously!
This is bad. What if they find out? What if they realize I'm not Cherry?
Oh, uh... I guess I shouldn't have said that to you.
Look man, you gotta get me out of here. I need your help!
Hey, listen up. The Fuck Bandits are falling apart. If Soko did something to Cherry... we need to find out.

So I need you to gather three Chuckleberry leaves. They grow in those dark, shadowy parts of the forest. I think Cherry said something about old bushes... then again, he might have been talking about one of the groupies...
Take them to the masked guy fronting the band. If he's the real deal, we'll get our old Cherry back. If not, well, we'll know we've got an imposter on our hands.
Really? You're just going to leave us hanging like that? Cherry's in deep trouble!
Fine, turn your back on us. But don't be surprised if Soko Uno takes control.
Guess you're okay with letting Soko Uno steal the spotlight... and maybe more.

name: Cigar Tales with Grizzly O'Brien

target.label: Scrag
target.landmark: Find scrags in the Abandoned Village to the south, as well as the Railway Depot and Swamps to the east

journal: Grizzly O'Brien Riverrise, Left side of the BAR yard. Find scrags in the Abandoned Village to the south, as well as the Railway Depot and Swamps to the east.
objectives: Don't keep me hanging, kiddo. I'll start sober reminiscing if you don't destroy those scrags.

npc: Suppose I ll start prepping the ole baseball bat. Wish me luck.
Here are your rewards.
Well, slap my knee and call me Lucy! You're a sight for sober eyes.
Ah, you're a lifesaver! If I get any clearer-headed, I might start seeing my own mistakes.
Oh, and that story I was gonna tell ya? I kinda... forgot it. Whoops!
The city is safe for another day, but those scrags aren t going to back down are they? I just may need another drink
Ah, that's the ticket! Time for some good old smoky oblivion.
You look a lot less shaky than I am and seem like you can handle yourself. Would you take care of some of these undead for me?
In return, I ll spill the beans about Riverrise s founding. It's a story worth every puff.
Ah, kid, this is about to be my first sober day in two decades, and it's terrifying! Did you know there are undead monsters out there?
Guess I'll be awake enough to remember that time I wrestled a bear and lost.
The last thing I want is to start remembering my ex-wife's cooking.
What in the hell have I been missing? It s a disaster out there. I built this damn city with my bare hands and I ll be damned if I see it overrun with undead.
They seem to be everywhere! The abandoned village south of town, the Rivergleam Swam, and the Railway Depot to the east

name: Clean Up on Bong Island

target.label: Active objects Herbal Pipes
target.landmark: Follow the road north out of town and look for the old house

journal: Queenie Viper Riverrise, Left side of the BAR yard. Follow the road north out of town and look for the old house.
objectives: Like, seriously?! You haven't found the bongs yet? Ugh, you're sooo slow! Don't make me come down there and do it myself, hic!

npc: Oh, hey, um, who are you again? But, like, the bongs...?
Oh, you are the BEST! Pleeeease don't let my mom find out.
Okay, okay, I owe you one hic- like, a big one!
Just... not a sexual one. You uh... - hic - aren't my type.
Like, oh-em-gee, finally! Took you long enough, jeez.
Seriously, if you made me wait a second longer, I would've... ugh!
Here, take your reward and scram. I'm gonna smoke these all at once, hic.
Remember, zip it! Or you ll regret it, hic!
My mom can NEVER find out, got it? Or you're toast too!
Like, oh-em-gee, you HAVE to help meeeee!
Last night was like, whoa, totally wild, and I'm still kind of seeing double, hic.
If my mom catches wind that I was out partying, I'm toast.
She'll totally explode, like kaboom! You get me?
Pretty pleeeeease, can you fetch those bongs from the old house in Riverrise?
I swear, you'll be my hero forever, hic.
Ugh, don't be such a buzzkill, pretty pleeeease!
Nooo, come on! I'm, like, totally begging you here!
You're seriously going to leave me hangin'? So not cool!
Oh, for the love of hic don't make me grovel!
Puh-leeeease, I'll owe you, like, a million favors!
Wow, thanks a lot! Now I'll just get ANNIHILATED by my mom!

name: Cleansing the Ride

target.label: Chests
target.landmark: Collect Gritgrub Paste from сhests, barrels, crates… on Prison Island

journal: Dwarfaton Ironchek Windstone Village, Windstone blacksmith yard Collect Gritgrub Paste from сhests, barrels, crates… on Prison Island.
objectives: Gritgrub Paste. Somewhere on Prison Island. Chests, barrels, crates... don't be picky.

npc: Bring me enough of that Paste, and I'll concoct something to stabilize your saddle.
You have got your hands on, uh, a corrupted saddle.
Hmph. Didn't think you had the spine.
Alright then.
Well, look at that... you actually did it.
You're back. Let's see if you've got what I need.
I can cleanse it... but we'll need Gritgrub Paste... a rare substance that neutralizes such foul energies.
Gritgrub Paste isn't just, uh, lying around. Check barrels, crates, even pockets of whatever scum still breathes on that island.
Figures you'd back out.
Suit yourself. Let the saddle rot.
Hand over the Paste. Time to get to work.
Hm... It's not quite done. Uhm.
We may have a problem.

name: Toxic Trinkets

goals.label: Collect Blue Bottles and fill in

journal: Griz Turner Goldenfield, near Artifact Shop Help Griz Ironfoot retrieve crucial artifact fragments from the Toxic Waste area in Dusktide Forest.
objectives: Collect Blue Bottles and fill in
Find five bottles in the Dusktide Forest's Toxic Waste area. Be cautious of the green, oily liquid. It ain t friendly.

npc: I knew I could count on you! Be careful out there.
Grab those bottles and come back safe.
Excellent work! These bottles are just what we needed.
Here's your reward, as promised.
I'll keep half the pieces, and send the rest to Ratchet.
Try not to expose yourself to any more radiation this week, yeah?
Did you manage to get those bottles?
Say, what is that? A third arm?
Ah, Ratchet sent you? I've been researching the artifact he asked for...
Rumor has it, this artifact could be the source of the hazardous radiation leakin' in the Toxic Waste area of Dusktide Forest.
Rescuers have already separated the artifact into little blue bottles, but we need 'em back. I need you to fetch five of these blue bottles for me.
Be wary of the dangerous atmosphere from the green oily liquid leakin' about. Note exactly healthy for ya.
Lemme put it in simpler terms: pick up some empty bottles and fill em' with that toxic stuff from the barrel.
I suppose I'll have to hire someone a little braver...
Well no duh it's dangerous, why do you think I'm paying you? Head south into the Dusktide forest. You know Maxwell? It's east of him. You'll see it.

name: Confiscated Confections
journal: Mary Jane Greenpuff Windreach Woods, Road house. Find the train tracks to the south and search for the material by following the track east.
objectives: Uh... what did I send you to do? Oh right! The book! No, the gear!

npc: Oh, duuude, that's like, a major buzzkill. Maybe next trip, yeah?
Whoa, bummer, man. Totally missed a chance to groove with nature.
And hey - I'll give you that weirdo book as a gift!
The herbs will wait, but time's a tricky thing, man.
Check the train track to the south of us and follow it to the east looking for those items.
Surely, they'll want the stuff back, because I know I do!
I'm pretty sure those uptight fort folk keep notes of everything they confiscate.
They also stole this weird book I found buried in the forest. I lost some pages some of the pages. The pictures are a total vibe, dude!
They're more than just plants, they're like, essential vibes for our collective wellness, you know?
Oh, man... those guards totally harshed my mellow by taking my stash of herbal goodies.
Remember: Follow the train tracks and look for that gear. I won't remember, so you have to.
Look what we got here - materials the captain is just might be missing!
Far out! This is exactly what we needed. Time to sweet talk to those guards, man.
Whoa... hey, didn't I tell you to do something for me?
From what I've heard there are some supplies, provisions, and building materials that were left behind.
Far out, man! You're really tuned into the universe.
Hey man, maybe if you play nice with Themis he'll give me my stuff back. I heard they had a little bit of a train robbery. Along the train tracks there's some stuff they may want back.

name: Implant Issues
journal: D'Rek Goldenfield, near Workshop D'Rek needs you to craft an Immuno-Stimulating Implant using a new recipe to test a theory on his failed attempts.
objectives: Remember to craft that Immuno-Stimulating Implant. We need to figure out if my original recipe is the issue.

npc: Success! Seems like I can delete the old recipe.
Any progress with the implant? The suspense is killing me.
Seen any success with the implant yet?
How's that implant shaping up?
Remember to craft that Immuno-Stimulating Implant. We need to figure out if my original recipe is the issue.
I'm feeling under the weather myself, so this is pretty urgent.
Why don't you keep the implant - you're a real expert now.
As part of a government initiative, I've been crafting Immuno-Stimulating Implants for outworlders.
They keep everyone healthy and prevent the spread of viruses from other dimensions.
But lately, my implants have been falling apart. Just turning into dust.
I suspect there's an error in my recipe, but I don't want to delete it until I'm certain.
I need you to craft an implant using this new recipe. If you succeed, it confirms the problem is on my side.
And if not, well - it means I've got more experimenting to do.
The recipe calls for Blue Lotus and Biocompatible Material. Nature meets tech at its finest.
I'll have to find another way to confirm my suspicions.
I'll have to figure this out on my own. Thanks anyway.
I'll just keep tinkering until I figure it out.

name: Copper Snack

target.label: Oxidized Batteries
target.landmark: Head to the Railway Deopt south east of Riverrise and find bolts and batteries to feed the recycling machine

target.label: Rusty Bolts
target.landmark: Head to the Railway Deopt south east of Riverrise and find bolts and batteries to feed the recycling machine

journal: Rusty McDrinkit Riverrise, Alcohol truck. Head to the Railway Deopt south east of Riverrise and find bolts and batteries to feed the recycling machine.
objectives: Processing request... hic... Have you mixed those ingredients yet? ...hic. Keep searching... hic... Bolts and batteries are critical... for gratitude... hic ...protocol.
goalTexts: Bring delicious liquid to Maverick "Mav" Gritt

npc: Initiating gratitude protocol... hic... Humans enjoy acidic beverages, correct?
You've got to be kidding me. Did you seriously recycle those for this... acidic liquid?
Initiating acceptance... hic... process. Proceed to create a beverage.
Here's your reward. Just remember, next time, stick to the plan!
Greetings... hic... fellow traveler. Mav's truck had such delightful liquids.
Find some batteries... and... hic... bolts...
In the Rivergleam Railway Depot, there's plenty of... hic... ingredients...
Error... hic... gratitude sequence interrupted... restarting...
Processing... hic... human refusal detected. Initiating disappointment protocol...
Hmm... hic... seems you won't help. I will... hic... thank Maverick with... gunpowder then.
Alright then... hic... guess my gratitude will be explosive... hic!
Put those in the recycling... hic... machine and there will be a delicious result.
Processing... hic... Crunchy! Just like humans enjoy! Initiating gratitude... hic... sequence.
Take your stuff and scram. Next time, think before acting on the whims of a drunk bot!
Fine, here's your reward. But this doesn't help my truck one bit!

name: Corruption Clean-Up

target.label: Savages
target.landmark: Kill some Occultists around the area

journal: Morick Voss Westwind Woods, Valley of sins infestation. Kill some Occultists around the area.
objectives: Keep cleaning up those Occultists, 'cause we can't have Lyra piecing things together, now can we? If she was distracted by... loss, she uh... won't have time to sign your service form.

npc: Choosing not to act has its own consequences. Think of Lyra.
Turning away means the misery for Lyra only grows.
I'm tasking you with a critical job: squash those Occultists. Think of it as pest control if pests were hellish nightmares.
Can't blame you, but Lyra will be heartbroken if she stumbles upon something she shouldn't.
Those Occultists may have killed our, uh... comrade.
Backing out? It's on you if Lyra gets hurt discovering the truth.
I am concerned Lyra will find evidence of their demise and be... devastated.
So, you know about the Valley of Sins, right? That's where this crazy shit is coming from.
By the way, I've got a final task for you. Don't mention the badge, deal?
Walk away if you must, but know the pain Lyra will face.
Remember, what happens in the woods stays in the woods. Especially any badges.
Pure coincidence, I'm sure. It's a common name.
You got guts, kid. Or you will splattered all over the forest. Just kidding. Sorta.
Did you see anything out there that might need, uh... further investigation?
You didn't see a Lightbearer's Token with my name on it, did you?

name: Cosmic Concoction

target.label: Veins ( ORE )
target.landmark: Collect 5 Meteoric Metal and 1 Titanium Nugget for D'Rek's project

journal: D'Rek Goldenfield, near Workshop Collect 5 Meteoric Metal and 1 Titanium Nugget for D'Rek's project.
objectives: Don't forget those metals, buddy. Meteoric Metal and Titanium Nugget, remember?
Don't forget those metals, buddy. Meteoric Metal and Titanium Nugget, remember?
It may take you all day and night, but that Adamaybium is out there! Make haste!

npc: Great! Let's get to work then.
How's that metal hunt going?
You're not getting cold feet now, are you?
My toolbox is itching for those metals.
Ah, you got them! Time to bring this vision to life.
Fantastic! Let's get cracking!
You did it! Can't wait to get started.
Don't forget, three chunks of Adamaybium. It may be a bit tricky to find, but I just know it s out there!
It's a fusion of raw cosmic power, Elanien magic, and good old mechanical ingenuity.
To make this brainchild of mine a reality, I need special ingredients. You may have heard of it Adamaybium.
It s rare, unique, and rumor has it they can be obtained by mining the mineral-rich veins scattered across Kwalat.
No hard feelings, I'll manage.
Okay, but you're missing out on the innovation of the century.
Alright, back to the solo grind then.
If we achieve this vision, we won't just be making an engine. We'll be setting a new standard for Elanien technology and opening up a world of untapped potential.
Yes, it's a daring venture, teetering on the edge of impossible. But, I've got this gut feeling that it's going to work.
Imagine this scenario: I'm in the thick of one of the most ambitious projects Goldenfield has ever seen - creating a high-powered mana engine.
It's more than just a hunch. It's faith in the audacious spirit of innovation and adventure.
So, do you have the courage to embark on this quest? Will you join me on this exploration of what's possible?

name: Crafting Concerns

target.label: Weapon Parts
target.landmark: Gather curved pipes around the fort, and assemble the weapon before delivering it to Silas

goals.label: Build a weapon on the anvil

journal: Lyra Vex Steppe, Western fort. Gather curved pipes around the fort, and assemble the weapon before delivering it to Silas.
objectives: Gather those curved pipes, and use the schematic to craft the weapon for Silas.
goalTexts: Build a weapon on the anvil

npc: You want to make things better around this fort? Perhaps you could help me.
My dear husband Silas is tirelessly indulging in his little war games at the front lines.
Perhaps a new firearm might satiate his appetite or hasten his demise.
I've acquired schematics for a custom weapon called Backfire Betty, that needs some assembling.
And do try to be efficient about it.
Ensure it's delivered to Silas personally.
Can you gather some curved pipes from around the outpost... and use them to craft this weapon?
I should have known better than to expect competence from a foot soldier.
Very well. I suppose even someone like you can manage this.
Very well, I'll find someone with actual skills.
How predictably disappointing. Off you go.
I suppose even this was too much for you.
Speak.
You made this?
A gun that fires directly backwards into the user... wonderful.
Ugh, here's payment.
I'll teach you to make a real weapon now.

name: Brave New World - Crafting Proficiency
journal: Martha "Big Mamma" Potterson Goldenfield, near Welfare kitchen Help folks around Goldenfield.
objectives: Put those hands to use, hot stuff! You need to get better with your fingers, you know? There's plenty a' locals around here who the government pays to teach. Start with Nia Sundance; she's back behind her summer house to the east. She ll help you with some herbal gathering. Then head over to Zogar at the logging spot, which is near Thordal Ironvein to the west of Goldenfield. Chopping down specific trees will be good practice and then you can mine some metals. After that, visit Thordal Ironvein, near Zogar, to mine some metals. It's a bit tougher, but worth it. This order works great for newbies, but feel free to help other locals along the way to pick up new skills. You'll get the hang of it in no time!

npc: The list really goes on and on. You'll figure it out!
I'm so proud of you sugar! From baking to back-breaking labor!
Now, I think the next thing you gotta do is get out there and put those skills to use.
Plenty of people around here uh... hate you.
It's not personal! Just part of the whole "outworlders are destabilizing the town" thing.

name: Culinary Diplomacy

target.label: Special Herb
target.landmark: Search for the Overgrown House to the north east of town

journal: Thorin Toastbeard Windstone Village, Tavern. Search for the Overgrown House to the north east of town. Professor Zephyr Yule should be around.
objectives: Don't forget, the special herb is in purple jars near the Overgrown House. Those eggs won't stay fresh forever, so chop-chop! Head northeast outside town in the Skylight Grasslands quickly! If you get lost, you're on your own.

npc: No problem! But don't cry to me when those eggs become a biohazard.
Well, not my problem. I don't have any kids that Triton can eat.
You see, a special herb stored in purple jars near the Overgrown House is just what we need. Head northeast into the Skylight Grasslands.
Not sure what exactly it is, and don't give a shit. I'm not the one eating it.
Go steal some and give it to Triton. Tired of listening to his scaly ass complain.
You help with big lawyer plan!
Ah, the imps' eggs are an acquired taste, to say the least. But there's a way to make them palatable, and that's where you come in.
Triton still pay you for effort, but if you want artifact, you need to bring Triton real baby!
NO! YUCKY YUCK. Even grosser than before!
Special herb? This better taste like regular baby now...
Seriously? Well, I suppose you'll enjoy the smell of rotting eggs then.
Yes! You get eggs for Triton?
I should probably start washing mine before cooking...
Nice to see someone else willing to get their hands dirty.

name: Darkwood Dilemma

goals.label: Pick up an item near the corpse as proof of death

journal: Tharkon Windreach Woods, Fort. Search for the Captain's body near the Altar north of the Quarry and collect any proof you find.
objectives: Hey, don't drop the ball now. The Captain's life and ours depends on you getting your act together. Find his body, and report back to Trevor. Come on, partner. If the Quaddari are behind this, we're sitting ducks. Hustle up! Get deep into those Southern woods and bring our boy back!
goalTexts: Pick up an item near the corpse as proof of death

npc: Declining? Well, good luck when the Quaddari decide to make a horror show out of our fort.
Think about it: the Captain's life, and maybe ours, depends on this. You sure about ditching?
You're passing up on uncovering a Quaddari scheme? Bold move, but foolish.
Let's see what secrets are hiding in those creepy woods. Report anything you find to Trevor.
Plus, maybe we'll finally get some answers.
That damned Altar is in the deep south of the Westwind Woods, by the quarry.
Sounds like a Quaddari conspiracy if you ask me, and you know how I love those. Alexander Stones is my hero!
We need the Captain back, or at least what's left of him. Bring back anything you can find of him. Otherwise, the Quaddari win.
Alright, listen up! Our Fort Captain's gone missing, and rumor has it he was last heading towards that spooky Altar for the gods of the woods.
That artifact caused this. Wherever it is, we need to contain it - now.
I suppose I'll have to tell him... after we resolve this... incident.
Skipping this hunt? Hope you're ready for when the Quaddari blow us all to pieces.
Did Tharkon help you find the captain?
DAMN IT. Dead? Seriously?
I don't give a shit about the Captain, but that means Themis is in charge now.
Ha, a real-life conspiracy! Can't wait to see the Captain's 'big reveal' at the altar.
Quest Completion Intro Phrases
Quest Completion Phrases
You think what belongs to me?
While I thank you for bringing it back, the true owner is the Doctor.
I gave this to Doc Greene on Prison Island for his research.
I had little use for it for mysticism.
Oh my. Well, it used to.
player: Quest : Dead Names and Living Circuits

name: Page 4: Death - Raven
journal: Each treasure touched, a cost severe.
objectives: Discover treasure at the cost of blood while on the island.

name: Decrypting Betrayal

target.label: Dreads
target.landmark: Kill 10 Dendroids around the forest

journal: Louise Oldgarden Reverie Hollow, Pastor s house. Kill 10 Dendroids around the forest.
objectives: Hey, you kill any Dreads yet? Pastor Fazool's counting on us. Don't forget, the quicker we get that proof, the quicker we save the day. And the quicker Fazool rewards me - I mean, us.

npc: Oh come on! You really gonna leave Pastor Fazool hanging?
She's using some Quaddari tech to drive them wild, like extra hostile.
Bless Pastor's soul, but we won't stand a chance if this blows up. We need to stop her.
Can you go out there and kill some Dreads? We gotta confront Lana, and fast.
Seriously? After all Pastor Fazool has done for us? Unbelievable.
When you kill the Dreads, go tell Penny. She'll collect the tech from their bodies.
So, I've been digging around, and you won't believe it. Lana's been sneaky with those Dreads.
Oh man, we've got a hot mess on our hands. Fazool's gonna flip when he hears this.
I'll go collect that evidence in a bit!
Now you and I will have to solve this...
I never thought this cult thing would get so intense. We just made it to keep the Quaddari authorities off our backs.
I think the stars are aligning, telling us something. Or maybe that's just the mushrooms talking.
Fazool needs you, and you're just gonna walk away? That's cold.
Wait, Lana did what now? I mean, I knew she was up to something, but... whoa.
Uhh... collecting evidence? Yeah, that sounds important. Right.
Pastor Fazool is going to be so impressed when we crack this open.

name: Defectors' Round-up
journal: Roddy Steppe, Western fort. Daxton and Greta are around the fort. Nud-E Leroy is northwest near Skeleton Cave on the beach. Triton is around the Lost Boats north of the Skeleton Cave and Slick Shady is at Coal Village to the north. You can find Horace around the Arid Canal, northeast of Coal Village.
objectives: Just a little nudge those volunteers might be missing my thrilling tales by now!

npc: Just the hero I was telling everyone about!
You know, once upon a time, I fought off a horde of sand wraiths with nothing but a rusty spoon. True story!
But today, well, we've got a bit of a... minor hiccup.
Seems half my army has decided to take an impromptu stroll.
Can't say I blame them; who wouldn't want to sightsee in this heat?
Without them, signing any accords is, uh, slightly more complicated than wrestling a giant scorpion.
But fear not! It's all part of the grand adventure, right?
I was just about to go after them myself, but you know, someone needs to hold down the fort!
Maybe you could, you know, just nudge them back this way?
Someone told me Slick Shady is at the Coal Village directly north of here. While Horace is around the Arid Canal to the northeast of the Coal Village.
Daxton and Greta are probably around this fort somewhere.
Oh... I see. That's... that's alright.
I understand. Maybe I'm not as inspiring as I thought.
Alright then. I guess I'll have to handle this on my own.
It's okay. Perhaps they were right to leave.
I thought I could rally you, but... never mind.
Fantastic! Just like that time I single-handedly tamed a wild sand dragon details later we'll bring them back in no time!
You're back. Any news?
Most of them are... dead? Well, that's not exactly the heroic tale I was hoping for. At least Greta and Daxton are here.
You know, once I single-handedly stopped an avalanche with just a shovel and a dream!
So, uh, this is just another bump in the road, right?
No need to worry! We'll... figure something out. Together!
Here's your reward. You've more than earned it.
Nud-E Leroy would be tanning it up on the shoreline I suppose. Check near the Skeleton Cave to the northwest, by the beach.
Triton I heard went to the Lost Boats north of the Skeleton Cave.

name: Dismantling the Dendroid Guard

target.label: Dendroid Guard
target.landmark: Ludwig tasks you with a dangerous hunt for a Dendroid Guard in the Tempest Timberland

journal: Ludwig Schatten Tempest Timberland, near the Hunter camp Ludwig tasks you with a dangerous hunt for a Dendroid Guard in the Tempest Timberland.
objectives: Remember, head north toward the Frontier Strip; the Dendrohold is to the southeast of the Western Fort.

npc: Uh, and here's a small payment - for your scientific discretion.
Any luck with the Dendroid Guard? Those things can be tough nuts to crack.
Fantastic! Remember, you're doing this for the noble cause of curiosity.
You've actually done it! I can't wait to pull this thing apart... for science, of course.
Remember, we're looking for a Dendroid Guard. And try not to get squashed.
Have you found a Dendroid Guard yet? I'm dying to see one up close... well, not literally.
That Dendroid Guard isn't going to hunt itself. Off you go!
Ah, the Dendroid Guard - a living statue with a bark worse than its bite... literally.
Their remains could tell us so much about their society. Are they protectors? Leaders? Or just really big bullies?
If you can kill one, you'll be doing science a huge favor. Plus, it will be one less Dendroid Guard to worry about.
Awww, and I was looking forward to dissecting a Dendroid Guard.
Well, if you change your mind, you know where to find me.
No problem. I'll just add it to my list of potentially deadly quests.
You're looking for the Dendrohold. It's pretty far up north, just southeast of the wastelands' Western Fort.

name: Desert Data Dilemma

goals.label: Get data from the research drone group

journal: Dr. Queenie Steppe, Northsteppe fort. Help Dr. Queenie obtain ecological research data from HPPY-01 northeast at the Junkyard Slums, past the South Winery.
objectives: Get data from the research drone group
Please, find HPPY-01 and his group of researchers northeast at the Junkyard Slums and get the ecological data.

npc: Ugh, my head is spinning... I can't believe I let this happen!
The Lightbearers are demanding those ecological reports, and I have nothing to show them!
You might have guessed that we uh... aren't really an ecological crew. Mostly. Except Xelcor.
The stuff you brought us was valuable, but it ain't worth nothing in jail.
There's a group of survey drones in the northern desert wasteland... led by one called HPPY-01. Super annoying.
They have the data I need. Please head up there and grab it for me.
I don't care how you do it. Convince them, trick them, whatever it takes!
If I don't present those reports, the Lightbearers will have my head! And I'll throw you under the bus in a heartbeat!
Oh, thank goodness! I was about to lose it!
You're a lifesaver! I need that data ASAP!
I don't care how you get it. Just bring me something before the Lightbearers find out!
Oh, I see. If you change your mind, please let me know.
I understand. I'll have to find someone else then.
That's unfortunate. The Lightbearers won't be pleased.
What a pleasant surprise to meet someone fleshie out here!
I hope we've been helpful in your mission!
Thank you, and here's something for your help. Yaaaaaaay!
I've compiled all the ecological data for Dr. Queenie. It's on the way to her tablet now!
And, I got to make a new friend.
And you'd hate to be a rulebreaker. Wouldn't you?
Here's something to tide you over in the meantime! Yaaaaaaaaay!
How can I assist you on this fine day?

name: Desert Decryption

target.label: Fragmented Artifacts
target.landmark: Help Mort Decay retrieve mysterious artifacts

journal: Mort Decay Steppe, Northsteppe fort. Help Mort Decay retrieve mysterious artifacts. Collect them to the southeast, between the Northeast Fort and the Eastern-Marsh Fort.
objectives: The artifacts are still out there, buried beneath the dunes. Mine away one those veins.

npc: With your help... we can unearth them... restore balance... maybe even tip the scales.
Here... go dig... out in the desert. Mine it's secrets.
But be warned... the desert... doesn't give up its secrets... without a fight.
Heh... you've returned... and in one piece... remarkable.
The sands shift... balance restored... nature chuckles... ever so softly.
Or something like that.
You have quick fingers... quicker than mine... maybe you can do me one last favor...
Heh... brave soul... death's just... a passing shadow anyway...
Heh... suit yourself... the sands will... swallow us all... eventually.
No matter... I've faced death alone... before... amusing, isn't it?
The desert hums... even without... your vibrations.
Ha... perhaps the wind... will guide another... to aid me.
Time is but... a spiral... you'll come around... or not.
You're back. Do you have the artifacts?
The desert... it's alive... devouring all in its path.
I've sensed... powerful artifacts... valuable artifacts... buried deep... causing this relentless creep.
Perhaps it's... spiritual energies... or ancient tech... ha! Who's to say?
I've died... once or twice... chasing these mysteries. Death's just... a minor setback, right?
Mine it's secrets. Search for veins and mine for these fragmented artifacts in the desert.

name: Desert Denial

target.label: Anomalous Plant Samples
target.landmark: Use the Bramble Dust on the Leaf Queen to extract Anomalous Plants

journal: Mabel Thorn Steppe, Central Fort. Use the Bramble Dust on the Leaf Queen to extract Anomalous Plants. Bring the samples back to Mabel Thorn.
objectives: Extract "Anomalous plant" from the Leaf Queen
Ah-choo! Don't forget to... sniff... use the Bramble Dust on those Thornback Creepers. They're everywhere!

npc: Ah-choo! You got them... sniff... Thank goodness.
Oh... ah-choo!... Never mind then. I'll just drown in pollen on my own.
Fine... sniff... I'll deal with this herbal nightmare myself. Ah-choo!
Can't say I blame you... ah-choo!... These plants are relentless.
Ah-choo! You're back... sniff... Did you get that data?
I just need uh... a little more data to get this figured out.
You up for round 2?
Ah-choo! Figures... sniff... No one wants to mess with plants either.
Ah-choo! Sorry about that. Sniff They've stuck me on this ecological team. Me, of all people!
I'm supposed to find out if the desert area up north is expanding. Something weird with the flora...
Ah-choo! Oh, thank you... sniff Maybe now I won't have to hug another plant.
Ah-choo! Maybe together we can figure this out.
Here, take this Bramble Dust from some roots up north. Sniff Use it on some Thornback Creepers... cough... and see what happens.
Maybe I can use that...

name: Desert Diplomacy
journal: Tor Drostin Steppe, Central Fort. Find Zor by the coastline directly north, at the Fisher's Camp.
objectives: Please, find Zor in his hidden village out in the northern wasteland. Remember, convincing Zor is crucial to preventing the ecological collapse.

npc: They bring only darkness.
We're in a real bind here, and I could use someone of your caliber.
The Steppes are on the brink of an ecological collapse, and I fear it's only a matter of time before everything falls apart.
Those so-called ecological scientists lurking around I've got a bad feeling about their true intentions.
But with that relic, we might unlock technologies that could change Kwalat forever.
There's a native named Zor living out in the northern wasteland desert. He's an odd one, but I've heard he knows more about this land than anyone else.
Problem is, he keeps to himself, and none of my people have been able to get through to him.
Time is of the essence. Equip yourself and set out as soon as you're ready.
May the energies of Kwalat guide you on this quest.
Glad to have you on board. This means a lot.
That's disappointing, but I understand.
I see. If you change your mind, we'll be here.
Alright then. Stay safe out there.
Do you have the artifact in your possession?
Hmm?... Stranger comes to Zor's home?
You worry for... the land, yes?
Zor feels... the imbalance too... The earth whispers its pain.
Very well, Zor will share his secrets... For the good of Kwalat.
I need you to find Zor and persuade him to share whatever he knows about what's happening to our environment.
Time's running short, and without his insight, we might not be able to stop this impending disaster.
I appreciate your willingness to help. Safe travels.
Thank you. You're making a difference.
Why you seek Zor... in these quiet sands?

name: Deserted Hopes
journal: Roddy Steppe, Western fort. Speak with Mary Jane Greenpuff in the Western Fort.
objectives: Have you had a heroic chat with Mary Jane yet? She's got the wisdom of... uh... the ancient leaf spirits!

npc: They say a fortress built on courage is stronger than any army. Or was it 'built with actual rocks'? Either way, we should start reinforcing this place!
I've bravely faced down giants and tamed wild beasts or so the stories go but this whole desert situation has me a tad... concerned.
If we channel the spirit of the ancient heroes like, uh, that one guy who held back the ocean with a broom we can make this base unbreakable!
So, what do you say? Join me in turning this humble outpost into a legendary stronghold! Together, we've got this... I hope!
And uh... maybe ask Mary Jane how we do that. Because I have no fuckin' idea.
Maybe the legends are right heroes like me are just myths.
I thought I could inspire others, but perhaps I'm not meant to lead.
It's best if I step aside; I only bring disappointment.
My stories are just tales; they can't help us now.
Absolutely! Together we'll stand tall like that giant... uh, tree I once saw! We'll make this place legendary!
But mostly, Mary Jane will.
Whoa, hey dude! When the fuck did you get here?
Whoa, thanks for dropping by, dude!
You know, chatting with you makes the trees seem even greener, man.
Maybe sticking around with these volunteer folks is like, meant to be or something.
Hey, take this scroll it's got some far-out wisdom that'll help you lead others on a righteous path.
Let's totally band together and spread some good vibes to heal this groovy planet!
I already have a good idea how we can start, man...

name: Desperate Depths

target.label: Swarm Leafs
target.landmark: Defeat Razorleaf Swarms around the forest

journal: Varkus Drov Westwind Woods, Old mine near sawmill. Defeat Razorleaf Swarms around the forest.
objectives: Don't forget to kill those Razorleaf Swarms... It's a grotesque task, but necessary for... reasons.

npc: Refusal comes with its own set of dangers, you know.
In the meantime, I will look into your artifact problem. Agreeable?
Suit yourself. It s not just your conscience that'll haunt you.
You see, I fear the Razorleaf Swarms around here may have eaten my dear wife as she wandered from the mine.
I need you to go kill a bunch of them so that I can rip their stomachs out. I need to search them for... evidence.
We may be the perfect people to solve each other's problems. You, a bulbous dumbass. Me, a genius treasure hunter.
But hey, now that you proved my innocence, maybe you can help me with one more thing...
I am so busy grieving, I couldn't even find your artifact.
At least we - I mean, you - killed the rest of the witnesses - I mean, perpetrators!
Think carefully, because some decisions are irreversible.
You've been busy. I already inspected the Razorleaf Swarms you killed.
It appears her ring was in one's stomach! We both saw it fall out - I definitely didn't just put it there.
Oh, I so fear the worst. It so clear that these creatures have murdered my poor, poor wife.
Ah, a wise choice, my naive friend. May your blade be as sharp as my wit.

name: Desperate for Dendroid
journal: Marigold Thornbuckle Goldenfield, Edge farm. Head south to the house on the edge of the fields and find the cookbook.
objectives: Oh dearie, have you fetched that cookbook yet? Lil' Timmy's patience is running thin! We can't keep Timmy waiting, you know how he gets. Or at least... you will.

npc: Timmy will be furious without his favorite dish. Are you sure?
Are you sure, sweetie? Timmy will be so disappointed...
Oh no, Timmy won't like this at all. Please reconsider!
I'm going to get cooking.
I left my cookbook in a house to the south, across the fields... I'm currently... tied up, shall we say?
The transparent leaves I need are only out there in the woods. I'm currently... tied up, shall we say?
Thank you, dearie!
I'm going to give you my chore list, but I think we... I think we need to do something about him.
Bring him the chore lists while I make a plan.
'Lil Timmy isn't my son, is he? He's some kind of... weirdo.
Think carefully, darling. Timmy won't be happy about this.
I feel kind of funny. Like I'm finally free after years of being controlled?
While you were gone, I uh... tried some of those herbs you brought back earlier.
Wh... what? Oh... it's you.
Thank you, dearie! Do watch out for those big, scary Dreads.
Oh dear, my sweet Lil' Timmy is eagerly waiting for his favorite dish, but we re missing an essential ingredient. I can't remember what it is!
Please, be a dear and venture down to the edge of the southern fields, where the treeline is. There's an old house down there with the book.

name: Diamond Eyes

actors.label: CallipersWe don’t have all day. I need you focused. Prepared.

journal: Durzo "Dicey" Dunwaddle Windstone Village, Guard's place: east. Use the items around the Dr. to help him create a beautiful set of eyes for Dwarfaton.
objectives: Head over to Dwarfaton in Windstone Village and help him with his eyes.

npc: Processing... eyeballs received. Initiating appreciation protocol.
As promised, here is your reward.
Receiving Glimmiwop's Legal Consent Note via integrated fax function... printing now.
Your assistance propels my grander scheme. Much appreciated, humanoid.
Nice work with Dicey. Just clear one more spot on my schedule, and Triton is in.
I've got Dwarfaton Ironcheek eagerly awaiting my skilled hands for some... well, unconventional improvements.
Fascinating, the precision of these eyes... Glimmiwop's work shall proceed.
I understand, perhaps you're not up for the challenge just yet.
No problem, I'll just have to reschedule my exquisite procedure.
Ah, the benefits of impeccable health care will have to wait for another time.
Are you ready to help me?
Clean them with the isopropyl alcohol.
Hm, now use some of the glass polish on them... yes... beautiful.
Grab that paint now, we're gonna add some beautiful iris colors.
Now for the pupils... use the paint again and add little black dots to the center. Wonderful.
Lastly, let s give them a good cleaning one more time with the isopropyl alcohol.
Duzro send you to help me with my, uh, eyes?
See those diamonds over there? Those will be eyes, I just need your steady hands.
Excellent! You ll do great. Just stay calm and keep a steady hand. Head over to Dwarfaton when you re ready to start.
This is where you earn your stripes! I need you to head over and help Dwarfaton!
Oh, it s easy. Just use the objects on the tables around him, and he ll guide you every step of the way.
What s a little diamond eye surgery?

name: Digging at the Truth
journal: Shawna Steelheart Steppe, Adventures outpost. Search for the journals in their Riverrise homes. Finn and Gareth’s houses are in the northern part of town, while Zelina’s is northeast of town, over the bridge, where the road forks.
objectives: Head to Riverrise to search for the journals. Finn and Gareth's homes are on the north side of town, while Zelina's is just outside of town, across the river.
goalTexts: Take the secret item from the house

npc: We've been holding this outpost together, but I fear one of us isn't who they claim to be.
Could you discreetly search their quarters? Look for journals, notes anything that might reveal their true identity. Check Zelina's too, just in case.
Like, have you seen them? They're... exactly identical. That's fucking weird, right? They aren't even related.
I need someone I can trust to help me uncover the truth without causing panic.
Finn and Gareth... they're so alike, yet my instincts tell me one of them may be the very masked killer who once threatened my own family.
Keep this between us. The last thing we need is to tip off the impostor before we have proof.
Something isn't right! We're barely hanging in the fight, but I've got two soldiers I'm highly suspicious of.
Thank you. Sometimes facing the darkness is the only way to bring back the light.
I understand. Some battles we have to face on our own.
Alright. Just remember, the shadows can be deceiving; stay alert.
Stay safe. If you change your mind, I'll be here, ready to stand together.
You're back in one piece. That means you found something worth sharing.
Zelina's journal... her dedication is evident. Perhaps we could use her help.
The other two... wow, this is basically just nonsense.
They both seem to write at a fifth-grade level.
If they didn't have kill counts in the dozens, I'd just cut their asses... unfortunately we need em'.
Maybe we have to approach this a different way...

name: Digging Deeper
journal: Alexander Stones Westwind Woods, Old Quarry overlooking platform. Talk to Dorik in the Westwind Quarry.
objectives: Hey, remember, the truth is down at the bottom of that damn quarry! Keep your eyes peeled for anything the mainstream media doesn't want you to find.

npc: Talk to me, now. Did ya find somethin' outta the ordinary?
Aye, you heard tales of the relic down here too? Thought it was all Alex's bluster.
Whatever dark magic lurks down here, the two of us can find it together.
This from Mary Jane? Thanks kid, I need it to record my podcast.
You're lookin' for some artifact? Oh, do I know about artifacts!
The artifact below this quarry isn't just your average find; we're talking mind control, maybe even interdimensional stuff!
Ah, come on, what are ya, scared the Illuminati are gonna get ya?
You're bailing on me? The globalist agenda won this round.
Fine, guess the reptilian overlords got a new recruit.
But I'm slammed with my latest podcast episode on the dark mages infiltrating the Elanien government, so I need boots on the ground for this one.
You gotta get down to the bottom of the quarry and talk to Dorik. He's seen things, man.
Alright, we need someone with guts to unearth this shit. You re it!
Well, good luck out there without any tinfoil hat protection!

name: Disorderly Consequences
journal: Alaric Stormshade Windreach Woods, Fort Find the paper next to Talora Vex, southwest at the Greystone Ruins. Deliver the note to Lyra Thornfeld southwest of Talora by The Last Order.
objectives: Head south west to the Greystone Ruins and find Talora Vex. The paper will be next to her. Then continue south west further to Lyra at The Last Order.

npc: You dare defy my orders? Perhaps a cell would change your mind.
Refusing me? Bold move. Hope you enjoy the dungeons.
Here’s the deal. Since you decided thievery was a good idea, you're going to be doing your fair share of community service.
Well, well, look who thought they could outsmart the system. Enjoying the taste of justice yet?
Here, take these supplies. They'll make sure you don't become just another casualty on your next task.
Good job delivering the note. Now let's see how useful you really are.
You're not like the usual recruits. Maybe you'll last longer.
Rejecting community service? You must have a death wish. Guards, prepare a cell!
Ah, so you're the new recruit? Haven't seen fresh faces here in a while.
Ah, finally made the decision to face your punishment, did you?
Lyra Thornfeld is south west of the ruins near The Last Order. Earn your keep or end up regretting life decisions.
Very well. Grab the note from Talora Vex and give it to Lyra Thornfeld and maybe, just maybe, you'll survive.
Grab a note from Talora Vex to give to Lyra, it'll have all the information she needs. Vex is currently in the Greystone Ruins to the south west.
The ruins are just north west of the Westwind Quarry. Grab the paper, and head to Lyra.

name: Double Trouble
journal: Terrace Tenstone Steppe, Northeast fort. Help Terrace by speaking with both the Rodgers' within the Northeast Fort about the draft mix-up.
objectives: Don't forget to talk to both those Rogers, ya know? When you've figured it out, come tell me, and we'll settle this, alright?

npc: Alright, suit yourself. Guess I'll have to knock some sense into 'em myself.
But maybe you can sort this mess out. Talk to 'em, see who's the real deal. Saves me breakin' a few noses.
Hey, you cracked the puzzle! Nice work.
Hey, that's great. We'll solve this puzzle together, you know? Might have to throw some punches if talkin' don't work.
They're bickerin' non-stop, messin' with the troops' mojo. I'm thinkin' maybe a good smack might shut 'em up.
Yo, you sort out which one's which? Who am I killing?
Now it's time fo rme to step in and kill the fake one.
Yo, we got these two guys both sayin' they're Roger Matthews. It's got my head spinnin', ya know?
What d'ya say? Help me crack this puzzle?
Here's your reward don't spend it all in one place.
No sweat. Maybe a little roughhousing will clear things up.
Hey, if you don't wanna help, I'll just solve this puzzle with my fists, ya know?
Maybe next time you'll get to use your fists, ya know?
You really wanna accuse me of lying? I could just kill us all.
Supposed to be here? Of. fuckin’. course. My killcount is triple digits. This guy's age is triple digits.
Uh, honestly, probably not. But if I get sent home, they'll arrest me for being a deserter.
And uh, execute me. Probably why that other chap is so aggressive - one of us is dead for certain.
I may be old, but I'd like to die in my sleep, not being horrifically executed in front of my grandkids.
Oh, and I'll let Lightbearer command know you helped me sort this thing out.
What have you discovered about the two Rogers?
I'm grateful for your assistance.
player: Are you supposed to be here?

name: Easy Rider
journal: Journal
objectives: Show the unusual saddle to Dwarfaton Ironcheek in Windstone.
Seek out two weaponsmith teachers and speak with them about the saddle.

npc: Given these strange changes, I'd recommend taking it to someone experienced in dealing with... peculiar goods. Maybe Dwarfaton Ironcheek can advise....
Aye! Look at the metal in those rivets. I remember mining that for Ratchet.
However, something's different with it now... Something otherworldly.
Hello... other humanoid.
Let's see what Dwarfat-... I... can do.
I haven't seen this saddle in ages! One of the few collabs I did and I must say we did an incredible job.
Quite the saddle it was.
I would seek out Dwarfaton... he deals in those... things...
You found it on the Prison Island? Oh my. I'm not familiar with what goes on there.
It's otherworldly... But can be cleansed.
My scans... er... examinations show this saddle is radiating something from the island.
Another teacher helped me craft this - see the engraving?
That s his mark, "G". You should ask him... He provided the special metals we used; maybe he can recognize what's changed.
The saddle bears a craftsman's mark...look closely, there's "R". We designed it together. You should talk to him; maybe he knows about those transformations...

name: Echoes Beyond
journal: Hilda Deeproot Westwind Woods, near Forest cabin Find the answers by speaking to the Wandering Spirit.
objectives: Seek out the Wandering Spirit once you've crossed over. Remember, only the dead can help you now.

npc: The answers you seek lie beyond the veil, among the spirits themselves.
Only the dead can reveal the knowledge you desire.
Are you prepared to face death itself?
I can offer you a mystical potion that will temporarily send you into the spirit world.
Once there, seek out the Wandering Spirit, he can guide you.
I see. Perhaps another time.
Understandable. The spirit world is not for the faint of heart.
Very well. Should you change your mind, you know where to find me.
May the spirits guide your path.
Seek out the Wandering Spirit once you've crossed over. Remember, only the dead can help you now.
The answers you seek come from the wisdom of the deceased.
You have crossed a life to be here.
My respect is yours.
My knowledge is at your service.

name: Echoes from the Front
journal: Barnaby Thorn Steppe, Great steppe stronghold. Help Barnaby gather information by finding Vexina Strider around the Stronghold.
objectives: Find Vexina Strider around the Stronghold.
Find Shawna northeast at the Adventurer s Outpost.

npc: Good. I knew I could count on you to die... er, fight... in the King's honor.
Vexina Strider has been monitoring the situation closely. Seek her out and she'll direct you there.
If you aspire to be a real Lightbearer and safeguard our beloved King, you must assist on the front lines.
Your dedication to the Lightbearers hasn't gone unnoticed; perhaps you're ready for a true test.
Ha! I knew you lacked the courage of a true Lightbearer.
Just as I suspected, not Lightbearer material after all.
I suppose the front lines are too daunting for the likes of you.
Running away? I expected nothing less from someone unworthy of the Light.
Heh, proving once again that you're not cut out for real challenges.
As I thought, the path of the Lightbearer is beyond your capabilities.
Yes, yes, out with it. I don't have all day.
Oh, and try not to step on any landmines.
Find Shawna Steelheart at the Adventurers Outpost. She's in charge , if you can call it that.
Well, things have gotten a bit out of hand... We lost contact with one of my soldiers. Last I knew she was just north east in the battle.
Uh, Vexina sent you? She's not... controlling you remotely, is she?
Because the last guy she did that with walked into landmines and wiped out a platoon.
If you're really here to help, I have a case that you may be able to crack...

name: Echoes in the Sand

target.label: Lightbearers Beacon Off
target.landmark: Help DOPY-03 retrieve a lost lightbearer's beacon from a Hyena in the northern desert

journal: DOPY-03 Steppe, Junkyard slums. Help DOPY-03 retrieve a lost lightbearer's beacon from a Hyena in the northern desert.
objectives: Hey, any luck finding that beacon? Look for any hyenas that have... uh... be glowing.

npc: Yo, what's up, dude? I've been picking up this signal from a lightbearer around here, but I can't seem to find the guy!
ERROR YOUR END IS NEAR Whoa, sorry about that glitch! No worries!
My circuits are like, wigging out, but I gotta get this mission done, ya feel me?
I'll get that data to Happy right now!
So, wanna team up and, like, save the day? Find some Hyenas and be on the look out for any that have a weird glow!
Radical, dude! Let's get this show on the road!
Together we'll, ERROR OBLITERATE EVERYTHING haha, just kidding! Let's go!
Alrighty then, maybe next time! ERROR YOU CANNOT ESCAPE Hehe, ignore that! Catch ya later!
Not feeling it? That's cool, bro! ERROR I SHALL REND THE EARTH ASUNDER Whoopsies! Must be my circuits!
Aww, total bummer, dude! ERROR YOUR DOOM APPROACHES Haha, just a glitch! No worries!
Yo, dude! You're back! Did you, like, totally snag that beacon?
Oh, the Lightbearer was dead?
Radical, dude! Well, thanks for the beacon! You're, like, a total legend!
Analyzing... ERROR THE LIGHTBEARER'S SOUL WILL BE MINE Haha, whoa, glitch alert! All good!
Total bummer about the lightbearer, but you rocked it out there!
ERROR PREPARE FOR DESTRUCTION Haha, just glitchin' out! So, mission accomplished?
I'm sure the Lightbearer is totally alive, and wasn't like, eaten by a Hyena or something. It'd be weird if the beacon was in a hyena, huh?

name: Echoes of Atonement

goals.label: Take the dagger from the altar

journal: Kira Steppe, Fisher's camp. Follow the coastline to the north west corner of the Silent Mountains. There you will find the lost altar to experience death for yourself.
objectives: You not dead yet! Go northwest! Use knife on lost altar, end it there!
goalTexts: Take the dagger from the altar

npc: Even death not want you!
You return? Nix'tar reject you!
Nix'tar vel Kwalat! You alive again!
Nix'tar vel Kwalat!
Your presence poison! Go die!
Away! Death too kind for you!
Good. Face your fate, and perhaps you'll find redemption.
Then prove your sincerity through deeds, not just words.
No! You leave! Kwalat curse you!
Ancestors spit on you! Begone!
Shadow take you! I want you gone!
Destiny call you to abyss. Go!
I see you stand before me once more.
You've faced death, haven't you?
Go to lost altar to northwest, and kill self! Prove you repent!
You bad soul! Go face end!
Words useless! Only death make right!
You bring hurt! Kwalat no forget!
Unless... terrible beacon? Is this how army seems infinite?
Maybe we can use... must help me.
Do you sacrifice yourself on the altar?

name: Echoes of Paranoia

target.label: Ravager
target.landmark: Get the key from the Ravangers to save the hostages in the Westwind Quarry

journal: Alexander Stones Westwind Woods, Old Quarry overlooking platform. Get the key from the Ravangers to save the hostages in the Westwind Quarry.
objectives: Remember, Varkus is hiding something big, real big. Don't let him slip away! Find those hostages who know what Varkus did. Westwind Quarry, the Ravager probably has the key!

npc: Guess you're just another puppet of the system!
Another sheep ignoring the truth. You're already dead inside!
Go help those people In the Westwind Quarry and surely they will be able to provide all the proof we need.
Just be ready for a fight, ok? He seemed... kinda hostile.
The truth isn't careful: You go to Varkus, and you tell him we know about everything: the artifact piece, the alien invasion, and his dead wife.
You're ready to dive into the labyrinth of lies. Varkus won't know what hit him!
You keep my secret, and you can keep it - I don't give a shit anymore.
Just get the fuck outta here, ok?
Alright, you got me. The bitch was going to spill my secrets, and she had to go.
I don't know shit about any aliens, but I found your damn artifact after you left.
Oh wait, you have proof?
Fuuuuuuuuck...
Don't you sound RIDICULOUS.
What the hell are you doing here?
Aliens...? Artifacts...? Murder...?
Fine, stay in the dark. But don't come crying when the aliens get you!
I think we've got some witnesses that can attest, however, they're currently being held hostage by a Ravager.

name: Echoes of The Bound Requiem

target.label: Damned Prophet
target.landmark: Collect wisdom from the Damned Prophets and return to Kings Head on Prison Island

journal: Wandering Spirit World of the dead Collect wisdom from the Damned Prophets and return to Kings Head on Prison Island.
objectives: Defeat the Damned Prophets and soak in their wisdom. Return to Kings Head on Prison Island.

npc: Adventurer, I sense a strength within you. A resolve that few possess.
The Damned Prophet torments these woods, bound by curses and regret.
They guard the secrets of The Bound Requiem, knowledge that must be freed.
If you can defeat them, their wisdom can be retrieved.
Take this wisdom to Kings Head on Prison Island. Perhaps he will have the final answer.
Thank you, noble one. May the spirits watch over you.
I see. Perhaps when the time is right, you will reconsider.
The path is perilous; I understand your hesitation.
Very well. The Damned Prophet's torment continues, but perhaps another will rise to the challenge.
So, you've returned. And you carry with you more wisdom...
Ah, the Damned Prophet's wisdom. Intriguing.
Perhaps you're more useful than the others after all.
You are quite close to understanding the book, no?
Perhaps a final test to understand the ancient script.

name: Echoes of the Feral Artifact
journal: Zor Steppe, Fisher's camp. Follow the coastline west in search of an island, just northwest of the Lost Altar. Gideon should be residing on it.
objectives: Stroud find. He lead to relic. Little time. Tears of Rage grow.

npc: Maybe we can finally find him.
A Stroud handling an artifact? Maybe that was the mission my brother was sent on...
Oh, you think you're worthy of my time?
Who dares interrupt my training?
Perhaps another time, when the omens are better.
Very well. The burden remains mine alone.
They act like savages so King can justify war.
I see. Not all are willing to tread this path.
May the whispers of the ancients guide your steps.
Your help, much needed.
Destroy relic, restore balance we must.
King use it, bring wild rage upon our kin.
Special artifact being abused by your people. "Tear of Rage".
Artifacts you people use they are ours, from long ago.
Furr's research brings knowledge.
Together, we'll find it and put an end to this madness.
Courier named Stroud last to hold it.
Find one called Stroud; he lead you to relic.

name: Eggcellent Solution

target.label: Egg
target.landmark: Eggs can be found on birds while corn flour is simple enough to grind

target.label: Corn Flour
target.landmark: Eggs can be found on birds while corn flour is simple enough to grind

journal: Berg Stonefoot Goldenfield, Forest Camp. Eggs can be found on birds while corn flour is simple enough to grind.
objectives: Don't forget, I need corn flour and two eggs. You can craft corn flour, I'm sure Big Momma knows how. Eggs, well, chickens man.

npc: Thank you, friend. This means a lot to me.
Oh, hey! Did you manage to find those ingredients?
You did it! Thank you so much.
Hopefully, this works, my butthole will be so grateful.
This could be a gamechanger if all goes well!
I may be living off government bread, but that stuff just doesn t sit right with my stomach.
I heard about some kind of flour made from corn. Maybe it'll be easier on my belly than this wheat stuff.
Could you help me out by getting some corn flour and a couple of eggs? I've got some roasted poultry that I'd love to bread with it.
Oh, well, I'd understand. It's not an easy task.
Alright, I suppose I'll find another way.
No worries. Maybe another time then.

name: Eggcellent Substitution

target.label: Imps
target.landmark: Kill imps in the Windreach Woods and collect eggs

journal: Triton Windreach Woods, Lake house. Kill imps in the Windreach Woods and collect eggs.
objectives: HELLO? TRANSMITTER WORKING!? Hurry, imps not wait long! Remember, need eggs. Look sharp! Triton more hungry, not wait forever.

npc: You want artifact, you need to make eggs edible first!
YUCKY YUCKY!
You no help, Triton still hungry.
Triton sad. Maybe next time.
In Windreach Woods, imps hide eggs. Kill imps, so Triton can steal. Then Triton tell you about artifact in lake.
Go now, hunt imps, Triton steal eggs. Triton getting hungry.
Humans say no eat babies. So Triton want imp eggs instead.
You say you want artifact from lake? Me help you if you help first.
Triton thank you for herb, but now I get hungry. Need babies!
Let me taste...
Fine, Triton eat you babies!
You make easy to steal eggs! Triton very happy.
Triton steal eggs from imps!
Good, good. Triton hungry for tasty eggs.

name: Eggplant Extravaganza

target.label: Eggplant
target.landmark: Search barrels, crates or satchels on the side of the road full of eggplants

journal: Greta Grubb Goldenfield, Southern House Search barrels, crates or satchels on the side of the road full of eggplants.
objectives: Could you gather five eggplants for me? They're in the fields nearby. You'll likely find them growing not too far from Goldenfield. Remember, I need five of them so I can start this... baking project.

npc: How's the eggplant hunt going?
Thank you, kind soul. Let's see what kind of pie I end up with tonight.
Ah, you re back! Wow, those are bigger than I thought.
Here s a reward for your efforts.
You ve given me a moment s distraction from my peculiar predicament. Thank you.
Remember, I'll be busy... baking... so I'll see you tomorrow.
You know, you could make yourself helpful one more time. Without Zikk around, it's been so busy.
I've been baking all these pies, and was thinking... what about eggplant?
I mean, they're everywhere, right? Might make a decent nigh... pie... Amirite?
Say, if you could bring me back some eggplants, and leave me along the rest of the day... well I'd pay you for that.
I understand, it's a strange request. Maybe next time.
Alright, perhaps another day will bring different opportunities.

name: Eight Legged Freaks

target.label: Spiders
target.landmark: Search for spiders south in the Ripplecrop Fields

journal: Ophelia Voss Riverrise, Bench behind the alchemy. Search for spiders south in the Ripplecrop Fields
objectives: Have you crushed those disgusting bugs yet? I'd hate for you to miss the 4:00 PM tea time...

npc: If you could eliminate some of those pesky eight-legged freaks by Thursday at 3:53 PM, I'd be ever so grateful.
Redrum... oh, just kidding! Thank you so much. Pax will be thrilled! Time waits for no one, you know.
Oh wow, you managed to beat the clock!
Here's your reward, 3:16 PM precisely.
Remember, once he reads that note, everything changes. Tick-tock, tick-tock!
You know, Pax is terrified of spiders! I hate seeing my brother so worried every day.
It won't be easy, but hey, it's for Pax, right?
In exchange, I'll give you a tiny little note. It has the precise moment Thistlebrook was fated to die, right down to the second. 4:44 PM, tick-tock!
Just remember, timing is everything.
