journal: Miguel "El Caballo" Rodriguez Riverrise, mount shop Travel to Prison Island and collect a shard from a Werewolf Alpha, Werewolf Rat, and Werewolf Soldier.
objectives: Don't forget, amigo, I need those Glowing Moon Shards from the Werewolf Alpha, Rat, and Soldier.

npc: I've got a plan that requires the rarest of treasures.
The fearsome werewolves lurking in the woods have swallowed Glowing Moon Shards.
I need you to venture into the wild and retrieve these shards from the Werewolf Alpha, the Werewolf Rat, and the Werewolf Soldier.
Those beasts are no ordinary foes; they're champions in their own right!
But I believe you've got the spirit of a true luchador!
Bring me those shards, and together we'll prepare something that will shake the very foundations of Kwalat!
My madre always said, 'A strong heart conquers all adversities,' and I see that strength in you!
So, are you ready to step into the ring and face these formidable opponents?
Oh, I see. Not everyone has the heart of a champion.
No worries, amigo. Perhaps another time.
Sometimes, even the bravest need to rest.
My madre always said, 'Caution is the better part of valor.'
Well, the challenge remains if you change your mind.
Excelente! I knew you had the courage, amigo!
Ah, you're back! How did the match go?
Fantastico! You've done it!
I knew you had the spirit of a true champion!
With these shards, my plan can finally proceed.
You've earned this reward, and my everlasting respect.
My madre will be thrilled when she hears of your bravery!
Until our next adventure, amigo!
player: In the Belly of the Beasts

name: Infiltrating Ironhand
journal: Alexander Stones Westwind Woods, Old Quarry overlooking platform. Talk to Grommuk at the Logging Camp to the north on the edge of the Westwind Woods.
objectives: Remember, every log you chop is another step closer to the truth! Stay vigilant, those aliens could be hiding anywhere. Specifically, in Grommuk, who you NEED to talk to.

npc: Typical. You must be in league with the aliens!
Ah, I see how it is. The parasites have already gotten to you, haven't they?
Here's what we're gonna do - we'll infiltrate the infiltrators. You're going to report to Grommuk as his latest employee, and gather evidence.
You'll be working alongside Dusty, Eamon, and Fenwick. They're so deep in their boozy haze, they wouldn't spot an alien if it danced the tango!
He wants to take this quarry, and take down me, because I'm the one source of truth this country has.
Grommuk Ironhand isn't just any sawmill foreman; he's a parasitic alien, I tell ya! They're plotting to take over our minds, one splinter at a time!
Alright, listen up, freedom fighter! They've got us living in a world of lies and sawdust!
I will now give you task like earth bozz.
Grr... did good. Take starting bonus.
Fine, don't come crying to me when they start sprouting tentacles!
So... grrr... you are the new employee?
Speak quick! Busy... grrr... always working.
Buckle up, buttercup it's time to expose this alien menace!
I'm Ace. Top card in the deck, in you get my drift.
That's all you need to know, sweetie.
So, what exactly are you looking to buy?
I'd be happy to crush your skull.
This place sure knows how to keep things... stimulating.
Keeps me on my toes, if you know what I mean.
And keeps plenty of folks under them.
Got to keep these muscles flexed, don't I?
Velvet brought us here for some business. This is Riverrise, baby.
And let me tell you, plenty of things on the rise around here.
Thanks to your's truly, of course.
Think you'd break too easily.
Nah, can't risk you buckling under pressure.
You look like you'd scream the safe word immediately.
You're not ready for this.
You'd beg for mercy.
I've got it covered; don t think you could keep up.
Save your strength, I m not done with you yet.
Got a task that needs... firmness.
Think you can handle being under me for a while?
Glad to hear you submit.
Want to get tied up in some real work?
Careful... I demand satisfaction.
I am Alaric Stormshade.
Think of me as the last line of defense against chaos incarnate.
All of these aliens coming from portals don't understand our rules, but I'm going to make sure they do.
My mission here in Windreach Woods is to enforce every rule, no matter how obscure.
If you break any regulation, be assured, I will find out.
If any of you portal fuckers think you can join the Lightbringers, think again.
It's clear that not everyone appreciates the importance of structure and discipline.
My colleague Themis receives undeserved special treatment simply because of his lineage.
It's sickening.
Trevor uses dark magic to heal Themis a blatant disregard for protocol!
The overseer only allows it because Themis is his son.
Little do they know, I record their every infraction - and soon, the Lightbearers will know about it!
This bastion within Windreach Woods is where order reigns supreme under my watchful eye.
I know every inch of this fort, and I ve rigged it to expose rule breakers in ways you can t even imagine.
You ll find a hidden weapon or two around every corner.
Let s just say I ve taken hidden in plain sight to a whole new level.
This place is a fortress of rules, each one more necessary than the last. Violate them at your own peril.
Unregistered portal travelers have a tendency to... disappear.
Don't ask where; let s just say the Dead Forest isn t the only place to find bones.
Help? From a portalmonger? Ha!
Go back to the portal you came from.
Continue with your own duties.
You're violating Rule 7.4: No unsolicited offers of aid.
Unauthorized help compromises the integrity of our fort.
An extra pair of hands? Unsupervised? I'll report this potential infraction.
Assistance would imply inefficiency on my part.
You ve already broken Rule 22.6: No idle chitchat with officers.
Fine. Even if you did come from a portal...
Finally, someone willing to abide by the rules!
I suppose, portalmonger.
I have just the task for a regulation-respecting individual!
Perfect timing, I just saw someone violate Subsection B-Z-431.
There are breaches to be located and rules to be enforced.
Your vigilance and adherence to protocol will serve us well.
Excellent! We've got some serious rule-breaking to address.
I used to live it up in Leithana, but then I got sent out here to Goldenfield for uh.... 'practical experience'.
No worries, mate. I'll just... uh... improvise, I guess...
I was having the time of my life at the university until I fell asleep during the assignment handout. Next thing I knew, I was stuck in this provincial place.
Well, back to plan B... once I figure out what that is...
I'll give you the recipe, you do all the crafting. Then we can pretend you're one of my successful newcomer-students. What do you say, dude?
And because, yesterday's party with the locals was a bit too joyful. This hangover is killing me.
I've got a plan, though. You'll create something simple, like painkillers. Mostly 'cause that's the only recipe I give out for free.
See, I'm supposed to be this alchemy master teaching outlanders the ropes. In reality, I can barely tell a mana potion from a beer.
I'm good, for the time being. But I'll holler if I need help.
I'm alright for now. But thanks for the offer, man.
No need now, amigo. But I'll keep you in mind.
I'm in a bit of a pickle here. Can you help out?
I'm in deep need of help, man!
You bet I do, buddy. I could use a hand.
Just watch out for the local brew. It's stronger than it looks!
It's a nice place for newcomers to start their journey, though. The locals are surprisingly friendly for a bunch of country folks.
Goldenfield is... it's okay, I guess. It's quiet, maybe too quiet for my taste. It lacks the nightlife of Leithana.
The best part? You can have several alchemy effects at the same time, like a cocktail of... awesomeness.
Voila, you got yourself an improved version with a longer and stronger effect.
To pump up the volume and get stronger effects, you got to add more resources to the standard consumable.
To kick-start this party, I mean... to craft these consumables, you will need both regular and recycled resources from Butchery, Mining, Logging, and Herbalism.
Just remember, every consumable is like a good party. It's got a limited duration, but it'll make your journey through the wild lands of Kwalat a whole lot more bearable.
These babies don't come in different qualities. But, if you get lucky, you can get two, even three consumables at once.
You get to whip up some groovy consumables like medicines, vaccines, and ointments. You can even roll up some joints.
Though, on the bright side, I did manage to snatch some notes on alchemy from university. FYI, it's all simple if you look closely. Cool even.
But, hey... the show must go on, right? I've got to teach alchemy, even if I barely remember anything from my classes.
I'm struggling a bit, honestly. The rural life is not for me. The lack of sleep doesn't help either.
Man, I'm feeling... ugh, not so hot, you know?. Parties with the locals can be a bit... intense.
And guess what, dude? With each new mastery level, the number of alchemy effects you can stack will shoot up!
I'm Percival Beamish, but you can just call me Percy.
Well, that's a fail. But don't worry about me, I'll... sort something out.
Believe me, man, if you master Alchemy, you'll feel as comfy as a sloth in a tree. Even when exploring the most difficult and savage locations of the wild lands.
Name's Alex "Hooty" Howitzer, but you can just call me Hooty.
I'm a living legend, or so they say, known for my explosive talents.
Had a run-in with a horde of imps once, thought it was the end.
Set off my rocket launcher to save the day, figured I'd go down in flames.
Somehow walked away in one piece. Now I'm guarding this roadblock.
My buddy Colt "Muffinman" McCrady and I keep an eye out for trouble.
I started calling him "Muffinman" when he sniped an imp while eating a muffin...
Want to hear a story about the time Muffinman almost shot me where the sun don't shine?
Never been better, always got an eye out for new threats.
Standing guard here at the roadblock outside Riverrise keeps me busy.
Plenty of time to swap stories with Colt and keep the place secure.
Some days are quiet, others we get all sorts of strange folks passin' by.
Keeps it interesting, you know? Never a dull moment guarding the road.
This is the roadblock just outside Riverrise.
Riverrise is the biggest city around.
Full of crafters and hardworking folk. Good place to get supplies.
We're here to keep the road safe from any dangers.
Between the stories and the patrols, we've got this place covered.
Nah, got things under control for now.
We're all set here, but thanks for asking.
Appreciate the offer, but we're good.
Not much needing done at the moment.
Got it handled for now, pal.
Actually, yeah, got something you could help with.
Could use a hand if you're willing.
Always room for another set of hands.
Sure thing, I've got a task for you.
If you're up for it, I've got something that needs doing.

name: Info Alex Hooty Howitzer Steppe Version

npc: Well hey there, boss! Name's Alex "Hooty" Howitzer, the Imp Annihilator.
Just a fellow Lightbearer keepin' the King's light burnin' bright.
Folks call me Hooty... silly joke from basic. I'll tell ya another time. Wanna hear a story?
Caught behind enemy lines with my buddies lives on the line, all I had left was one explosive round and a horde of enemies.
Knowing I may not make it out alive, I did what any good Lightbearer would do, and shot that sum' bitch off.
Somehow, I survived... the imps weren't so lucky. Nowadays I hang around with Muffinman and remind him what a badass I am.
Keepin' the light alive, even if the pantry's lookin' a bit shadowy these days.
Stomach's a bit tight after Muffinman made lunch yesterday... feel like I might poop a chalupa.
Other than that, things are alright. Can't be any worse than blowing myself up...
But uh... it'd be great if you were here to help out. Muffinman just kinda sits around... just look at him.
This outpost might look like a heap of scrap, but it's our heap blessed by the King himself!
It's a little rough around the edges, but that's why I'm stationed here... make it purdy.
Between you, me, and the glowbugs, the King's got big plans for this place... just gotta squint a little to see 'em!
So ignore the creaks and leaks!
Ah, don't be sorry, just don't let it happen again.
Appreciate the offer, but we're looking for someone a bit more competent.
You... you wanna help? But, I'm the Imp Annihilator... what can't I do?
Well, ain't this the King's own timing! Could definitely use a bright soul like you.
Bless the light, you're just in time... got a little task that's begging for your touch.
Now isn't that a sign from the King! I'd be obliged if you'd lend a hand.
I go by Alex Stones, a truth-seeker and miner of madness.
The shadows told me I'm the chosen one to unveil Kwalat's darkest secrets!
They say I'm crazy, but the creatures I speak of are as real as this rock.
You think there are no sky serpents or moon goblins? Think again!
Remember the tale of the ancient blood beetle? Oh, I've seen its husk, mark my words.
The truth is out there, buried beneath stone - and I'm the only one brave or crazy enough to dig it out.
Bah, frustrated, that's how! Those Windstone idiots called me a liar again.
They say my tales are just that - tales. Buncha asshats!
Can't stand their ignorance. The truth's right beneath their feet, and they just laugh.
I swear, if they call me crazy one more time, I'll dig up a bloody space worm and shove it in their faces.
Damn them! They'll see, one day. They'll see what s lurking here.
This ain't just any quarry, it's the goddamn heart of Kwalat's ancient secrets.
These rocks? They're hiding something alive, something ancient and hungry.
I've seen the signs, the symbols. This place is cursed, I tell ya!
There's a beast slumbering beneath, an ancient horror just waiting to wake up.
Stay too long, and you'll hear it too the whispers, the groans of something monstrous.
Hell yeah! This place is brimming with secrets.
Damn right I need help! You in for uncovering the truth?
Yep! Time to prove these fools wrong.
Absolutely! Let's blow these conspiracy deniers away!
Let's to dig up some madness.
You bet your ass! Join me in exposing the lies!
Yes! Let's shove the truth in their faces.
Nah, they're watching us. Can't risk it.
No, no! The walls have ears, shh!
Not today, the shadows are listening!
Too risky. They're onto us, I can feel it.
No, damn it! They'll find us if we speak too much!
So that's where you come in. Your arms look strong. Especially the right one.
And if you're up for a real challenge and want to create an even more powerful artifact, you'll need to put in some serious effort and seek out the rarest of resources.
But, the integration of Elanien's mana technology is a fascinating process.
Welcome to Goldenfield, a place where the rustic meets cosmic fuckery.
Now, here on Kwalat, I've adopted a new title: Constructor.
As a result, they have begun to construct devices from the remnants of ancient diagrams.
I'm D'Rek, a blind mechanic from the abyssal depths of a distant world.
Actually, I could use some help with a project.
Want to assist me with an experiment?
Sure, I have a job for you, if you're up for it.
Good timing. I was just about to ask.
No extra hands needed right now, thanks.
I got this, but I'll holler if I need you.
Can't think of anything, check back later.
Give me some space to work, would you?
I'm in the thick of one of the most ambitious projects Goldenfield has ever seen - creating a high-powered mana engine.
It's a fusion of raw cosmic power, Elanien magic, and good old mechanical ingenuity.
I need special ingredients, not just any scrap metal. I require Meteoric Metal and a Titanium Nugget.
They're rare, unique, and rumor has it they can be obtained by mining the mineral-rich veins scattered across Kwalat.
Unfortunately, your's truly used all his arm strength this morning uh... working on a special project.
No hard feelings, I'll manage.
Okay, but you're missing out on the innovation of the century.
Alright, back to the solo grind then.
It's like a giant building set mixed with a spontaneous magic show.
Every day's a new puzzle, and I fuckin' love it. It's like Sudoku for the multiverse.
For a gearhead like me, Kwalat is a playground of mysteries like if Sherlock Holmes had a toolbox and no respect for the laws of physics.
They have started to comprehend how artifacts hold power and might have been used by their precursors.
If you're looking to create improved artifacts, that's a whole other ball game.
The higher your Construction skill, the more complex and powerful Artifacts you can create.
Why Constructor, you ask? Well, here, I don't just fix or build any ole' shit. I craft Artifacts.
But as we move up to more powerful and complex Artifacts, we can combine these characteristics in any variation. The possibilities are endless!
It's a fusion of engineering and magic. Think Iron Man meets Bob Ross.
I utilize my skills here in Goldenfield, trying to find harmony between nature and technology.
Artifacts, you see, aren't your run-of-the-mill trinkets. They're complex items, carrying two sets of characteristics.
It's a craft of precision and creativity. A basic Artifact carries one basic characteristic.
You'll need to combine two uncommon quality artifacts with the characteristics you want, all with the help of a lil' something called Isotope-238.
As part of a government initiative, I've been crafting Immuno-Stimulating Implants for outworlders.
They keep everyone healthy and prevent the spread of viruses from other dimensions.
But lately, my implants have been falling apart. Just turning into dust.
I suspect there's an error in my recipe, but I don't want to delete it until I'm certain.
Let me know if you hear anything from a local. I'll pay. Some.
I've made a living out of finding the valuable in the overlooked.
Nothing for you right now.
No, I've got to stay in my god-forsaken booth today.
Always on the lookout for the next big find, it's how I keep my edge.
All quiet on my front.
My little corner of the world, aye. Keeps a man on his toes.
It's got a bit of everything: food, tech, artifacts, and outworlders that are easy to scam.
Welcome to the best marketplace this side of Kwalat!
Actually, yeah, could use a hand with something.
Well, now that you mention it...
Got a little job needs doing, if you're interested.
Looking for work, eh? I might just have something for you.
Got his eye on a Uncommon Immuno-Stimulating Implant, a trinket from some far-flung world.
No work today, but check back tomorrow.
You work for free? Just kidding, but not really.
Sorry. Got it all under control.
Got a customer, peculiar fella, got a bit of a thing for the unusual.
Name's Griz, a scavenger by trade. Came from a post-apocalyptic world.
Now, getting your hands on one ain't a walk in the park. But hey, where's the fun in easy, right?
This guy... he's not exactly throwing his coin around. But it's a fair deal, if you're up for the challenge.
Ah well, worth a shot. Maybe next time, eh?
No hard feelings, friend. Not everyone's cut out for scavenger work.
Maybe you'll change your mind? The offer's always open.
People here in Kwalat are always chasin' artifacts - and I'm the fastest.
At least, I was... until that portal opened and the goods dried up.
Might just be more outworlder hooey though. They tend to lie.
Lotta whispers about a powerful lost artifact somewhere 'round here.
Now I'm questioning those Plantech robots... I've seen robots take over before.
Best you'll find me, considering my last meal was some questionable meat.
You've got some strong hands. Let's put them to use.
Ashley. Trader, seeker of secrets, and very good at keeping my own.
You sure have a knack for asking at the wrong times.
No, it's almost night. I think I'd better lie down for the evening.
Careful venturing out there into the island. You never know what's lurking in the shadows.
Prison Island... it's just delightful, isn't it?
Excited. I know I'm close to something big... and I always get what I want.
I'd say I slept well, but... well... I don't really sleep much these days.
Oh, you know... just keeping myself occupied until nightfall. Why? Curious?
Feeling great, actually! The sun's up, business is good, and you're here. Lucky me.
Restless. Something's stirring in these ruins, and I need to find it.
I heard about some ruins here and was very interested in what may be underneath them.
My long nights have been holding my examinations back a bit though.
Of course, looking at you, you know a thing about long nights. Amiright?
Let's just say, I have urges, and I'm hoping to find something to control them.
Oh, I'm just a girl with expensive tastes and a knack for getting what I want.
You pick now of all times to ask me if I need help? Seriously?
Who am I? Just a simple merchant with a taste for rare finds... and interesting company.
How delightful. I could absolutely use some help. Especially after dusk.
Certainly. I would love to find these ancient ruins soon.
Came here for the artifacts. Stayed for the excitement. And the... company.
Word travels fast, you know?
Well, that and the fact you're wanted for arson and need to clear your name.
Got all I need right here... Except for my crew, of course.
Actually, yes. Can't promise it'll be easy, though.
My crew, they're not just colleagues - they're my friends. The thought of them lost and terrified... it's too much.
I understand. It's a lot to ask. I'll just... I'll just keep waiting.
But hey, there's no rush, right? Except each ticking second feels like a punch in the gut.
It feels like an eternity since the crash. Time and space have lost all meaning.
Sure, no problem. Space is vast and time is... well, I have plenty of it here.
Well, apart from my radio being as useful as a chocolate teapot, I'm doin' alright.
It's not so bad here, got plenty of green friends to keep me company.
Name's Dr. Orion Bloom, the one and only space botanist!
Then, an imp showed up and raided our entire ship! Ridiculous.
But hey, could be worse. Like being allergic to oxygen, right?
Oh, and my suit's recycling system? Top-notch. I've had the same cup of coffee six times!
Could definitely use a hand. Or two. Or four, if you have 'em.
You know, I think I'm alright for now.
I wish I could tell you more about this place, but I'm as lost as a black cat in a coal cellar.
We collided with something in space, next thing I know, I woke up in this strange land. It's all a bit fuzzy.
There are flora and fauna I've never seen before. It's as if I've crash-landed on an unknown planet.
One crash landing, some lost crew, and a whole lot of alien plants later, here I am.
If you're offering, I've got a job for you.
You're kind, but I think I've got things under control.
The isolation, the uncertainty - it's maddening. Every moment is a test of sanity.
I'm stranded here, with no way to navigate this alien landscape or reach out to them.
That's okay. I'll keep holding on to hope. It's all I have right now.
Unfortunately, my crew were the real scientists.
An imp? Using my plants? That's despicable...
So, if you come across my crew, pass them my coordinates. Maybe we can solve your problem.
So, the creatures that chased off my crew were "Imps"?
Well, this here's my crash site. Some would call it a junkyard, I call it home sweet home.
Now, I'm just trying to find my friends, if they're still...you know, kicking.
I've been around, seen some weird stuff in my space travels, but this...this is new.
It's like stepping onto a sci-fi movie set, except it's all real and I'm the clueless earthling.
But hey, change of scenery never hurt anyone! Just need to adapt and overcome. How hard can it be, right?
I've had better days, honestly. Waking up on a strange planet? Definitely not on my bucket list.
Nah, I got this. Space tech is as easy as riding a bike.
All good in the hood, pal. Just need some time and lots of duct tape.
I'm okay. Just need to tinker around a bit more.
I was part of a crew on a mission, until...well, all this happened.
Lovely neighborhood isn't it? Spacious, good view, room for a spaceship or two.
I've seen better, but hey, beggars can't be choosers.
Actually, yeah, I could use a hand.
You know, two heads are better than one.
You offering to help? Well, ain't you a space cowboy!
Just a spaceman named Ronny Lunar, lost in a strange world.
Crazy. I don't know much about their metabolism, but its possible cross-dimensional exposure could make them pretty dangerous.
The challenge here is not knowing who or what we'll find - every clue and every bit of information helps.
Understood, it's a big task. I'll keep tinkering away here.
That's okay. I'll just sit here, alone, in this vast, alien world. No problem.
Sure, take your time. It's not like we're stranded in space or anything.
I can make something to counter it, but I'll need help from the rest of the crew.
It's time to track them down. I have a feeling they might be scattered around.
Oh, well, peasant uhm, I mean, friend, my name is Bob Smith.
Just your average, everyday citizen. A man of the common filth... folks, yes?
I come from... ah, not too far, just a humble abode in Quaddari's luxurious, I mean, lower districts.
I'm here to, you know, blend in and experience the simple life.
I, uh, relish in, erm, mud-eating contests and, uh, dung-dodging games very typical, right?
Trying to fit in, you know. I recently paid, er, a whole gold coin for one of those "loaves of bread.
Isn't it funny, how you dirt-munchers I mean, dear friends survive on such meager rations?
By the way, I've been meaning to ask: What do you think of the current mayor?
Do tell, who holds sway around here? Any, uh, useful gossip for a humble chap like myself?
Ah, this rustic little hamlet of Riverrise, isn t it?
Quite fascinating how you manage without the luxuries of Quaddari, truly.
Do you lot actually, um, forage for your food here? How quaint!
The city center, yes. Busy with all sorts of... simpleton activities, I assume?
I've heard rumors Elan is resistant to Quaddari influence. Admirable... and amusing!
Oh, uh, no, no. Peasants, I mean, folks like us, manage just fine, right?
Not at the moment, my dear... common compatriot.
You see, I've got this whole "bootstrap" thing going, so no need, really.
I'm perfectly capable of, uh, mucking through on my own. But thanks, commoner!
No, no, I've got it covered. Just trying to live the ol' dirt-eater lifestyle, you know?
No, thanks. I m doing the whole "self-sufficient" thing you peasants do.
Oh, I'm just here to watch and, um, learn your humble ways, dear urchin.
Oh, absolutely, my good... er, grubby chum! Always in need, aren t we?
Why yes, of course, I'm certain your... skills will come in handy.
Naturally, peasants, I mean, pals like you are always lending a hand, right?
Indeed, my fellow dirt... denizen. There s always something needing done around here!
Oh, definitely. Life s a constant struggle in the trenches, isn't it?
Hic, name's Balthazar... but folks jus' call me Baz.
Used to be a top-notch mixer of drinks back in Elan, yeah?
Could whip up a potion to make yer troubles disappear... or ya mind wander off somewhere lovely.
But, y'know, got too fond of me own brews, lost me hair an' me business.
hic - Ah, never better, mate!
Life's a bloody rollercoast'r when ye got a bottle in yer hand.
Jus' had a couple o' patrons... damn lightweights, passed out cold burps.
Ain't no rest for the wicked, right? Hahaha!
Been mixin' up me special brews... puttin' folks to sleep faster than a lullaby, heh.
Ya lookin' for a good time or a risky ride?
Ah, this lil' haven? Welcome to Baz s... hic... finest, mate.
Used ta be jus' plain ol' woods near the Roadhouse... but I saw potential, ya know?
Those rich bastards at the Roadhouse have deep pockets. Hic.
Figured I'd set up shop here... hic... snag a few of them high-rollers passin' by.
Ah, ya big ol' sweetheart!
Got sum passed-out sots need dumpin'.
Heh, yer a gem fer askin'.
How 'bout helpin' rid me o' the slumberin' drunks, eh?
Heeey... could use a hand refillin' this here bar.
Don't want the next sucker runnin' dry, ya feel me?
Got sum dead weight 'round here.
Help ol' Baz toss 'em out, eh?
Hic... kinda ya ta offer!
Ever play bartender?
How 'bout restockin' my stash? Hic, won't regret it!
Nah, mate, jus' keep me company fer a drink, eh?
Help? I'm good... unless ya count sharin' this bottle.
hic Naw, but... how's 'bout a drink, pal?
Piss off! But stay fer a drink or two.
Hell no, eedin' nothin' but ol' drinkin' company!
Seriously? You don't know who I am?
I'm Barkley Beats, the fucking rapping dog!
If you haven't heard of me, you must be living under a tennis ball.
Oh! Tennis ball!? Where?
Working on a new rhyme. Check it!
Chillin' in Riverrise, paws on the ground,
Sniffin' out bones, ain't no better sound.
Bitches be barkin', but I got my own,
In this dog-eat-dog world, I'm holdin' my throne.
So, what'd ya think, bud? Ruff enough for you?
Oh, this place? Beats me, mate.
Last guy who tried putting a leash on me had an address here.
Figured I'd bring his bones back, you know? Return 'em to their rightful owner.
Except for the one I chewed up, that's our little secret.
Now I'm just hanging around chillin'.
Nah, fuck off tail chaser.
No thanks, I'm solid.
Not right now, dawg.
Don't need help, man.
Always up for some fresh vibes. Whatcha got?
Fuck yeah, could use some creative spark! And a light...
Absolutely, could use a new beat to bounce to.
For sure! Inspiration strikes in the strangest ways, pal.
Name's Barkowski, or Bark for short.
Got no clue why they call me that, though.
Oh wait man. It's cause I'm a dog!
Funny thing, I kinda forgot I was a dog.
Lately, it just feels normal, you know? Then bam, it hits me.
Oh, you know, same old, same old.
Just enjoyin' the day, one drink at a time.
These paws make holding a glass tricky, but I'm managing.
Everything's a blur, but I remember tall tales, bars, and... a wild night?
Starting to think maybe a guy named Zeek had something to do with it.
I'm parked right outside the bar, best spot to chat and drink.
You get all sorts here farmers, travelers, and a lotta good treats.
Being a dog has its perks. No one expects much from ya.
Pondering life and love, same as always. Ever wonder if the Viper girls would go for a scruffy mutt like me?
Nah, I got this, bro.
No worries, dude, I'm chillin'.
All good, man, I m solid.
Nah, homie, I m good.
Relax, bro, all under control.
Dude, absolutely!
Hell yeah, bro!
Totally, man, let's crush it!
For sure, my guy!
My name isn t important. I am the head of the Mayor's guard.
I was raised on tales of Elan's glory, forged in the fires of its defense.
Loyalty to the Mayor is my life s purpose. There s no higher calling.
My duty is to ensure his safety and the security of our beloved Riverrise.
On high alert, as usual. There are whispers of Quaddari spies.
Vigilance is key. I trust no one without proven allegiance to the Mayor.
My dedication to the Mayor fuels me. His leadership is our beacon.
I stand ever ready, for Elan, for Riverrise, and most importantly, for the Mayor.
This, my friend, is Riverrise, the crown jewel of Whisperwind Isle.
The greatest city in all of Elan, thanks to our brilliant Mayor.
Every street, every stone here, radiates his vision and strength.
Riverrise stands strong and proud, because the Mayor makes it so.
We owe everything to his unwavering leadership.
Insulting. I handle the Mayor's needs myself.
The Mayor's tasks are for me alone.
You insult my duty by asking.
The Mayor relies on my vigilance alone.
Step back. The Mayor's affairs are my responsibility.
I need no assistance; the Mayor has me.
This is my responsibility, and mine alone.
Do not question my dedication to the Mayor.
Oh yes, the Mayor needs us!
For the Mayor, always!
Mayor needs champions, you in?
Elan needs you. Mayor's orders.
Absolutely! For the Mayor's glory!
Time to shine for Elan!
We do this for Riverrise!
Mayor's wish is our command!
The Mayor s mission needs you!
Let s make the Mayor proud!
The unwavering shield of our illustrious mayor of Riverrise.
For years, I served as the mayor's personal guard.
Recently got reassigned, er - promoted here to lead the war effort.
My duty is to crush these northern threats in the mayor's - and the light's names.
Ah, just marvelous! The volunteer army decided to volunteer elsewhere deserters, the lot of them.
It's me versus the northern anomalies now who needs sleep anyway?
And the ecological survey team? Breathing down my neck about 'environmental impact'.
As if the creeping chaos cares about their precious foliage!
Between vanished soldiers and those leaf-loving bureaucrats, it's a wonder I'm still sane.
Ah, welcome to this charming patch of mud and decay we call it the northern outpost.
This place is a mess borderline uninhabitable but hey, we re making it the jewel of the north.
A wretched pit that should feel honored we're stomping around in it.
This forsaken corner of the world is held together by the sheer will of the lightbearers you're welcome.
Unless you ve a decree from the mayor himself, I need nothing.
Assist me? Unless you can match the mayor's brilliance, stand aside.
Help? Unless you're sent by the mayor, I'll manage.
Only the mayor could offer aid to my mission thanks but no.
Unless you can fortify my resolve like the mayor does, I think not.
Your enthusiasm is noted, but this task is too crucial for unproven hands.
Help? Ha! Unless you can channel the mayor s wisdom, stay vigilant elsewhere.
No, unless you're here to bring word from the mayor himself.
Finally! Someone who's not just standing around
By the mayor's gleaming smile, yes! I've been juggling artifacts like a mad jester!
Absolutely! These cursed relics won t obsess over themselves!
Yes! Together we'll seize those artifacts before they seize us!
Thank the light! An extra pair of hands to handle these... fascinating anomalies!
Do I need help? Does the mayor inspire greatness? Yes!
Yes! The more eyes we have on these artifacts, the better for Riverrise, of course!
By all means, finally!
Yes! With your assistance, we might just tip the scales in our favor!
Ah, just an old man trying to make a living.
Name's Bart Blaine, nothing special, just a vendor of fine antiques.
Some folks say I have a knack for finding exactly what they need, almost devilish, isn't it?
No tricks here, just honest deals from a harmless old man.
Now, would you care to take a look? I promise, it's all above board... mostly.
Having a bit of trouble with that rascal Felix there.
He claims he's cursed and stuck strumming that blasted guitar, acting all innocent.
But I know he s trying to scam me - he just wants a refund!
Someone should teach that young little shit a lesson, I reckon.
A place where dreams come true... depending on your luck.
Now, don't let the serenity fool you. My trinkets can grant any wish, oh yes.
Though there's always a price, but what's life without a bit of spice?
A wishing stone that might turn your pesky neighbor into a toad? Or a mirror that shows your true self... in more ways than one?
All thing I have waiting for you...
No help needed, just buy something instead.
I m good, but how about a purchase?
No thank you, but feel free to browse!
Help? No, but I do have great deals for you!
Your help isn't required, but my items might be.
Don't need help, just your business!
Rather than help, why not buy an antique?
Oh, you re a brave soul, aren t you?
Finally! Someone willing to give my customer what he deserves.
Ah, perfect timing! This customer needs a bit of... persuasion.
You're just the person I need. Let's talk about this customer.
Welcome to your premier banking experience! I am Bart Gumble, your loyal banking manager at your service!
We here at Quaddari Regional Banking believe in excellence, just like me, doing excellently in my... managerial duties.
I'm solely devoted to Quaddari Regional, where there's nothing but boundless joy and endless forms. Such a joy!
Can I sign you up for an account?
Managing this fine establishment with grace and ease, no stress at all!
Who needs a head office? Keeps things exciting, wouldn't you say?
I'll rest when I'm dead... or when the paperwork stops, whichever comes first.
This incredible town is the perfect setting for our grand Quaddari Regional Banking branch, the very first in Elan!
Our bank is here to offer world-class service to the remarkable residents of Riverrise.
It's a match made in heaven: a thriving community and a top-notch financial institution.
Honestly, investing my time and passion in this branch is the best decision I've ever made!
Oh, absolutely not, dear patron! I'm here to assist you!
My sole purpose is to cater to your needs, nothing more!
Helping you is my top priority; no need to worry about me.
Quaddari Regional Banking is always here to serve you, not the other way around!
Your experience is my mission; I'm here to make your life easier!
Oh, it would be a tremendous honor to have your assistance!
Your support could make all the difference in the world for us here!
I could greatly benefit from your capable hands, esteemed patron!
I would be eternally grateful for your help in these trying times!
Your kindness in offering assistance would not go unnoticed, I assure you!
Name's Baxton Malroth - you can call me "Skip", cause that s what I do.
Was the best stone-skipper in Windstone Village.
Men wanted ta' be me, women wanted ta' be with me.
Then I went and clocked Gregor in the balls. Whoopsie daisy!
Fame turned into shame quicker than you can say, "Hop, skip, and infertility!
They found out 'bout my secret stash a' magic stones, and I was banned from the league.
Still trying to redeem myself. Gregor's stayin' well clear!
Couldn't be better, mate! Got a cracker of a plan.
Gonna catch that thief who nicked from Gregor.
Picture it: me a hero. Crikey, I can already hear the village cheering.
Gonna skip this stone here right through the back o' his skull!
That thief won't even have time ta' use that gun o' his...
...I hope.
This here is Windstone Village, mate.
Quiet little corner of the world, eh?
But don't be fooled; it's a right strange place.
These stones? They're bonza magical, coz they come through portals from other worlds!
One time, I skipped a stone and turned ol' Mick's beard pink was priceless!
Another time, a stone skipped straight into the sky, came back as a flamin' meteorite!
That's how I lost this finger. My girl was pretty disappointed.
Struth, mate! Could use a wingman for my heroic escapades.
Let's train these stones to fly right.
How 'bout a bit of practice with these magic stones? Could use a hand.
How 'bout helpin' me train to take down that thief?
Remember, if I die out there - it's your fault!
You gotta teach me ta' fight!
I've got this. Unless he's a ninja, then I'm stuffed.
Got it under control... unless he's got a cloaking device.
Don't worry 'bout it.
Nah, got it covered...
I'm all set... unless that thief's sneakin' up now!
No worries... except that thief killin' me.
Nah, all's quiet... too quiet.
That, and my pockets are as empty as a - hic - politician's promises. Procuring it myself is a challenge.
After all, my predicament is partially your fault. You came through that damn portal, didn't ya?
I'm drunk, but I appreciate the offer.
I'm - hic - all set, buttercup.
But a kind soul like you could surely spare a bottle for a hapless philosopher like me, right?
Cats, you see, are the perfect drinking buddies. They listen, ponder, and then nap, creating an aura of sagacity.
What does one do in a place where entertainment is scarce? Philosophize, rummage, and drink until you aren't bored!
Goldenfield! A gilded moniker for a place that's more brass than gold.
Is laughter the best medicine? If so, I might live forever - a thought both amusing and mildly terrifying!
As for my well-being - hic - it's as if I've been invited to a cosmic comedy. Quite the spectacle!
Sure do, mate.
Got a job for you, if you're up for it.
You know what, I could use a hand.
Nah, I'm golden.
I reckon I was a farmer once, before the portal here opened. I think - hic - some crypto-fucker sued me for the land.
By jove - hic - that's the question, isn't it? They call me Paddy. What I was before... well, the details are a bit foggy.
Ah, my portal jumper, I'm as resilient as a boiled turnip in a stew.
That cats are the better company, of the two.
Now here I am, engaging in philosophical debates with cats and outworlders. A peculiar predicament, wouldn't you say?
Ah, the sweet nectar of Elanien Whiskey, I've had the pleasure of its company more times than I can uh, remember. Hic!
Oh well, can't blame an old man for trying.
Well, shoot. Thought you had a generous heart.
No worries, mate. I'll just sit here, parched and whiskey-less.
Unfortunately, with all these portal-hoppers, demand's gone through the roof. Whiskey's as scarce as a sober night at a dwarf's wedding.
I've been told people stash bottles in crates and barrels. Worse comes to - hic - worse, maybe ya can trade for it?
Name's Biff "Dozer" Bromley, but don't let the nickname fool you, I'm not always asleep.
I had grand dreams of heroics and glory. Turns out, I'm more of a professional nap taker.
Thought I'd be saving the world.
Got inspired by all the guard propaganda, imagine my surprise when reality hit!
These days, I'm just a mech-mounted listener to Hooty and Muffinman's endless comedy show.
Oh, you know, living the dream! If the dream involves lots of naps and very little action.
Hooty and Muffinman keep me entertained. They're like my personal, never-ending sitcom.
Honestly? The most action I've seen is a squirrel trying to steal my lunch.
Got tired of counting guard duty hours. Now I count how many times Hooty says "dinglehopper".
The mech's cozy, I'll give it that. Almost as cozy as a blanket fort.
Doing as well as a guy can when his biggest responsibility is not falling asleep... again.
This, my friend, is Riverrise where excitement comes to take a nap!
A thrilling world of fields, crafting quarters, and wait for it, a roadblock!
The most action we get is watching the grass grow... and it's not even fast grass.
If you're into endless guard duty and small talk, Riverrise is your paradise.
Think of it as the ultimate chill zone... If chill zone was code for most boring place in all of Kwalat!
You'd just be interrupting my nap.
Wha... what? You scared me. I'm trying to snooze.
I'm good, unless you have a cure for extreme boredom.
Appreciate it, but all's quiet on the nap front.
No need, unless you can fetch me a more exciting post.
I think I'm good, unless fighting boredom counts.
Not today, but I'll let you know when my pillow needs fluffing.
You know, I could use a hand. I'm trying to get my 16 hours, so listen up.
Hold on, lemme rub my eyes... Yes! I do need help actually.
Surprisingly... yes. Wow. Something to do!
Oh, for sure. Got a task that only a true adventurer or someone really bored can handle.
Yes, please! You might even break the monotony for a millisecond.
You're in luck! I ve got a mission that s just begging for some heroic intervention.

name: Info Biff Dozer Bromley Steppe Version

npc: Ah, names can be so formal, can't they? But if you insist, I'm Biff Bromley, though friends call me "Dozer" I clear the path to greatness.
But enough about me; there's something intriguing about you. Ever thought about what lies beyond your city walls?
Tell me, do you believe in seizing opportunities when they knock?
What if I told you that adventure and glory are within your grasp? Would that pique your interest?
Everyone has a calling. Maybe yours is closer than you think.
Absolutely fantastic! Just got word that the army at the western fort is achieving incredible victories.
The tales coming in are nothing short of heroic it's an exciting time to be part of something grand.
Honestly, I've never seen morale this high. The camaraderie among the troops is legendary.
It's as if destiny itself is calling us to be part of these monumental events.
Will you... answer?
Here, opportunities flourish for those daring enough to seize them.
Right now, the northern frontier beckons heroes to step forward and make their mark.
Imagine the tales of valor, the accolades, the hefty rewards all within your reach.
So why settle for ordinary when extraordinary awaits? Join us, and let destiny find you!
Nah, I'm just keeping the bench warm.
You'd just be interrupting my busy nap schedule.
Thanks, but I've got my mech doing the heavy snoozing.
I'm good, unless you have a cure for extreme boredom.
Appreciate it, but all's quiet on the nap front.
No need, unless you can fetch me a more exciting post.
I think I'm good, unless fighting boredom counts.
Not today, but I'll let you know if my pillow needs fluffing.
Ah, just the person I was hoping to see! I've got an opportunity that's too good to pass up.
Do I ever! How does a grand adventure sound to you?
Yes! Fate must have sent you my way. Interested in a little journey north?
Absolutely! With your skills, we could accomplish something legendary.
Indeed I do! How would you like to be a hero and earn riches beyond your dreams?
Perfect timing! I've got a proposition that promises excitement and... substantial rewards.
Yes, yes! Join me, and together we'll make history that bards will sing about for ages.
Funny you should ask! I was just thinking you'd be perfect for an important mission.
I m Blitz Hyperstorm, the hyper-charged mascot of Zap Juice!
Drink enough of this juice, and you'll be buzzing like me!
I m here to pump up the energy levels of everyone in Riverrise!
Oh man, I'm freaking out!
Just drank a ton of Zap Juice!
My heart's racing faster than a turbo train!
Feels like my brain's doing backflips!
Can't stop vibrating, it's like I'm a human dynamo!
Eyes wide open, can't blink, can't think!
My mind is racing at warp speed!
Wooo! This energy is off the charts!
Honestly, no clue where I am!
Boss points, I go, it's that simple!
All I know is, you need Zap Juice here!
Everyplace looks the same when you're hypercharged!
No way! Blitz is always fine!
Help? HA! I don t need wait, where was I? Oh yeah, ZAP JUICE!
Psh, I m too ZAPPED for help AAAAH! ENERGY!
Help? WHO NEEDS HELP? NOT BLITZ! BLITZ IS GOOD SO GOOD! ZAAAAP!
You think I need help? WROOOOOOONG! HYPERHYPERHYPEEEEEER!
I'm buzzing out of my mind, need a hand!
For sure! Help me spread the good word of Zap Juice!
Totally! I can't do it alone, buddy!
Desperately need your help to promote this magic juice!
Yes, please! More hands, more cans!
I'm Blitz Hyperstorm, the hyper-charged mascot of Zap Juice!
Drink enough of this juice, and you'll be buzzing like me!
I'm here to pump up the energy levels of everyone in Riverrise! Or... wherever we are!
Oh man, I'm freaking out!
Just drank a ton of Zap Juice!
My heart's racing faster than a turbo train!
Feels like my brain's doing backflips!
Can't stop vibrating, it's like I'm a human dynamo!
Eyes wide open, can't blink, can't think!
My mind is racing at warp speed!
Wooo! This energy is off the charts!
Honestly, no clue where I am!
Boss points, I go, it's that simple!
All I know is, you need Zap Juice here!
Everyplace looks the same when you're hypercharged!
And hey, this recruiter here tells me he'll get me up to a new market!
No way! Blitz is always fine!
Help? HA! I don t need wait, where was I? Oh yeah, ZAP JUICE!
Psh, I m too ZAPPED for help AAAAH! ENERGY!
Help? WHO NEEDS HELP? NOT BLITZ! BLITZ IS GOOD SO GOOD! ZAAAAP!
You think I need help? WROOOOOOONG! HYPERHYPERHYPEEEEEER!
I'm buzzing out of my mind, need a hand!
For sure! Help me spread the good word of Zap Juice!
Totally! I can't do it alone, buddy!
Desperately need your help to promote this magic juice!
Yes, please! More hands, more cans!
I'm Zendaya Wells, and, like, that's my mom.
She s a Guard officer, totally stuck in her old ways, super embarrassing!
Seriously, she drags me on this bonding trip and now we re lost in this creepy forest.
She s all, Monsters are dangerous! and I m like, Mom, it's modern times, chill!
We gotta be kind and understanding to everyone, even if they look a bit... different.
So if she s been rude to you, like, I m really, really sorry!
Ugh, like, totally embarrassed right now!
My mom just said the most cringe thing to this guy, and I'm like, dying inside.
Hold on, let me finish this text...
What are you staring at? This is a phone. P-H-O-N-E.
Oh, right, you country people don't know what a phone is. Awkward!
Anyway, she s so old-school and thinks she knows better than everyone. It's exhausting!
Apparently, this place is, like, super important or something?
My mom met my dad around here, but, like, who cares?
She goes on and on about how it's got a lot of history and stuff.
Oh yeah, soooo interesting! Ugh.
Seriously, I'm just like, "Can we go already?
Ugh, she thinks it'll be a bonding experience or whatever.
Dad's dead Mom! Get over it.
Sheesh, TRAUMA, am I right?
Ugh, yes! Anything to get out of this place.
I d love an excuse to escape from my mom.
Yup, mom is driving me crazy!
Oh my gosh, thank you!
I need a break from her old-school rants.
Yes, please!
I'd help, but my mom said no.
Mom's all, "No wandering off!" Ugh.
Ugh, mom's being so overprotective.
Mom's all, "Stay by me!" so, yeah, can't help.
Me, fight monsters? HAH!
OMG, you want me to like, work?
WHO AM I? I'M BLORP KNURT!
Hard to hear with this concert, huh? WELCOME TO MY HELL!
They haven't even started playing yet and it's TOO LOUD!!
Came from a place where SOUND WAVES WERE WEAPONS! This isn't better!
I LIVE RIGHT NEARBY. I LITERALLY HEAR THIS SHIT EVERY NIGHT!
HOW AM I? CAN'T YOU HEAR THIS BLASTED NOISE?!
I'M FUCKING MISERABLE, THAT'S HOW!
Tried earplugs but they just make me hear my own screams better.
Tried TALKING TO THE MAYOR, but he's DEAF AS A ROCK!
Now I'm here, DEDICATED TO MAKING EVERYONE AS MISERABLE AS ME!
Welcome to my PERSONAL HELL, where concert tickets are a DEATH SENTENCE!
Hard to hear you over this CONCERT, but this is Riverrise!
USED TO BE PEACEFUL, until THE FUCK BANDITS showed up.
THAT'S THE NAME OF THE BAND!
Now it's just NOISE and CHAOS, thanks to them!
CAN'T HEAR YOU OVER THIS NOISE!
Yeah, EARPLUGS would be nice. Otherwise, FUCK OFF!
Nah, handling this SOLITARY WAR just fine. Thanks, though!
APPRECIATE IT, but this FIASCO is all mine to RUIN!
Wanna RUIN A CONCERT?
YEAH, I NEED HELP! Ready to become PUBLIC ENEMY NUMBER ONE?
Help me make this concert A LIVING NIGHTMARE!
Sure, let's CRASH THIS GIG!
In my dimension of Zarg I am recognized by the play of 67 trillion electrons in a a dance your odd little minds cannot comprehend. So you may call me "Deep Blue.
Like a meat-brained doofus I fell into a black hole, which brought me here.
I need sustenance while locked in this world's tedious gravity well. I find these things called "artifacts" both delicious and nourishing.
If you bring me them, I will tell you where to find rare metals you creatures crave. I know you like Element 79 - "Gold" - in particular.
If more of my species arrive, we will cleanse this place of all inferior non-energy-based goo beings like yourself. But until then, we must co-exist.
I am a ball of energy imprisoned a plane full of meat puppets.
Your failure to exist on a higher energy frequency irritates my flow receptors.
I greatly enjoy watching you fail through your sadly limited lives
Until I am able to expunge you puny meat-bags from existence, I must co-exist with you all.
It gives me electro-magnetic heartburn.
I don't know, but I can conjecture.
You flesh-based creatures bumble along the earth's surface rather than floating like reasonable beings.
This means that you are impeded by flora and fauna. So you create "roads" and "trails" to make movement faster and less dangerous.
I believe this is a road that is no longer in use.
Does that inform your hilariously inferior brain?
I believe your flaccid thinking-organ is adequate to this particular task.
Indeed. Your tiny level of sentience is appropriate for this assignment.
It is a fortuitous event that you have arrived at this moment.
Your perspicacity is well above average for your unreliable species. Yes.
No. This is far too complex for a brain made of grey goo.
Not at this instant. It requires thinking abilities at a higher frequency.
Try back at a later nano-second.
From a meat-creature? Ehehehehe.
I'm Bob Loss, you're humble buddy.
Some folks call me a painter, others a fixer.
I see mistakes as opportunities for beauty.
We don't mess up, we just have happy accidents.
Feeling bright as a fresh canvas.
Every day is a blessing.
If I've got two feet and a heart beat, my suns shining on you.
The real question is, how are you, my friend?
This beautiful place is Riverrise.
I like to think of it as a gallery of second chances.
People come here from all over Kwalat, for many reasons.
Here, we don't make mistakes, we just respec happy accidents.
Well, you know, I don't need help with anything at the moment. But I appreciate you.
Sorry, friend. I don't have anything for you right now.
Not at the moment. Everything seems to be right where it needs to be.
Of course, let's paint this quest together.
Yes... let's turn this mess into something beautiful.
Absolutely, I have a blank canvas that could use some work.
I'm Bob, just Bob. Came up with the name "The Fuck Bandits" when I was 18.
Thought it was edgy and rebellious back then. Who knew it'd stick?
Sometimes I wonder if we're too old for this wild ride, but then I remind myself rock and roll never ages.
Riverrise is home, and the band's my family. Even when things get messy, there's nowhere else I d rather be.
Worried, to be honest. Cherry, our lead singer, is acting really strange.
Soko, Cherry s star girlfriend, pulled him aside earlier.
Cherry came back, but he's not himself. Something s seriously off.
He won t talk, won t say what happened. It s making me paranoid and desperate.
I'm starting to think Soko did something. I need to figure this out before our gig goes south.
This stage right here, it s where we played our first gig. Thought we'd hit it big, go on tour, and never look back.
Ironically, here we are again. The place where we began, and at this rate, it might be where we end.
Nah, I got my own band.
Rock on, but I m set.
My amps are cranked up high already.
Thanks, but my crew's all tuned up.
I m good, already got my headbangers.
Appreciate it, but my band's got the riffs handled.
We've got the beat down, but thanks for asking.
The band's in sync, no help needed!
Hell yeah, I need a road warrior like you!
You down to raise some hell with me?
Got a gnarly task, if you ve got the guts, man!
Ready to unleash chaos? I've got just the gig for you!
Buckle up, it s gonna be a wild ride. Are you in?
Me? I'm Boom-Boom McGee. Boom means big explody fun!
Used to be... I dunno, Steve or something? Don't matter now.
Got myself a job testing all kinds of things that go boom at the TNT shop.
I likes seeing things go kablam! Makes me all tingly inside.
Just found this new bomb, all shiny and stuff!
Can't wait to see what it does!
Might even blow a hole right through my suit, wouldn't that be a sight?
Just hope I got enough band-aids this time!
Uh, this place? Hmm...
Oh yeah, Riverrise! Or was it Boomtown?
Got lots of things that go boom here, that s for sure.
There s a shop with booms? I like that place!
People come here with stuff, they leave with less stuff 'cause I blew it up.
Hmm, help with what again? Nah, I think I m good!
No need, I... wait, what was I doing?
Help? Oh, uh, maybe later? I forgot.
Nah, no help needed... I think.
I'm fine... I think... what was the question?
Probably don t need help... or do I? Ah, forget it!
Wait, help with what? Oh yeah, never mind!
Oh yeah, you help me boom big time!
Need some hands to make boom-booms!
Boom-boom needs friends! You help?
Let's make stuff go kablam together, yes?
Help Boom-Boom go bang-bang?
The name's Bronco Rivers, but folks 'round here call me "Bowman.
Used to roll the dice in fancier places, ya know what I'm sayin'?
Now, I keep an eye on this here pier, makin' sure no funny business goes down.
Let's just say, luck ain't always been on my side.
Ended up in Windstone after, uh, a 'little incident' in another joint.
Got myself a job as a pier guard, not exactly where I pictured I'd be, but here we are.
Ah, you know, life's a mixed bag out here.
Trying to keep things straight on this pier ain't always easy.
Last night, I lost big in a card game. Again. Can't catch a break.
These ungrateful fishermen, they ain't too fond of me.
I'm just keeping 'em safe... for a nominal fee, of course.
But hey, it's a living, right? Just gotta roll with the punches.
'Ol Finns, he gives me the heebie-jeebies with his fish obsession. Can t figure that guy out.
This place? Oh, this here is Windstone Village, see?
Quiet lil' town where folks grow crops and tinker 'bout.
We got a bunch of hardworking dwarves and brownies livin' 'round here, keepin' things movin'.
This pier? It's where the fishers come in and out, all day, every day.
But lemme tell ya, there s more goin' on than meets the eye, capisce?
We got strange new folks poppin' in from other worlds 'cause of some fancy portal.
It ain't no big city, but we get by. So watch your step and play nice, got it?
Don t worry, it ain't illegal... technically.
Lookin for action?
Well, well, a volunteer!
I got just the thing, capisce?
Look at you, ready to dive in.
I got a lil' task for ya.
How d ya guess?
I got a job with your name on it, buddy.
Ah, lookin' to score some points, huh?
Ain't none of your concern, slick.
This ain t a charity, pal. Scram.
Nothing, unless you wanna be fish food.
Don t need another body to clean up.
Nah, no need for muscle right now, buddy.
Forget about it, I got it handled.
Ain't no trouble here, capisce?
Thanks, but no dice, pal.
I m good, but thanks.
No need for ya, wise guy. All handled here.
The name's Brawny Barrelbuckle, but folks 'round here know me as the Chief of Security.
Back in my universe, I was The Brute of the Battle Ring, a wrestling champ nobody could pin.
A teleportation stunt during a wrestling competition tossed me into Kwalat's wilds, elbow first.
Now, I keep the peace here in Windstone Village, ensuring everyone s safe from these inter-universe threats.
Ha, you know me, always on top of my game!
Gruk Ironcheek's been giving my guards a rough time, but I'll let him off for now.
Can't wait to throw him in a headlock one of these days, show him who's boss.
But rules are rules, so I keep my fists to myself for now.
Honestly, I'm itching to wrestle again, but saving it for the right moment.
Until then, I ll keep doing what I do best keeping this village safe, one knuckle sandwich at a time.
This village is a sanctuary, making my job rewarding and, dare I say, a tad easier.
The brownies and dwarves here are as tough as they come, but with hearts of gold.
Even with all these inter-universal oddities, we stand strong, united, and vigilant.
It's a privilege keeping this place safe, knowing I've got the best folks around backing me up.
Looks like I could use a tag team partner. You in?
I'm pinned down with issues. Mind helping me grapple them?
Need someone in my corner. Ready to step in?
Got a few challenges that need a good suplex. Care to assist?
Time to put someone in a figure-four leglock. You game?
I've got it pinned down, but thanks.
Not today; this match is under control.
No need for a tag out right now.
Appreciate it, but I'm not tapping out yet.
All squared away here, champ.
No interference needed, I ve got this bout handled.
Name's Bridgette Radcliffe, but everyone around here calls me Cap and so will you.
I'm the Captain stationed at the west-south exit of Riverrise.
Came from a world where discipline was life no slacking tolerated.
Here, I make sure those same principles hold strong.
Got my eagle eyes on the exit gates and slackers like Gareth.
Been busy, same as always. Guard duties never rest.
Stressful, but ain't complaining. Keeps me sharp.
I'll admit, helps to have The Fuck Bandits playing in town to lighten the mood.
And there's ground Silica... The tingle it gives helps cope with the long hours.
Not that anyone needs to know about that. Do you have any?
You re standing in Riverrise.
This town's the biggest here, full of drug dealers and professional victims.
We got a crafting quarter that takes up a chunk of the place keeps folks busy.
With new travelers popping in from who-knows-where, it s a bit of everything.
Just stay out of trouble and you ll be fine.
Not right now, but stay sharp.
I ve got things under control.
Appreciate the offer, but not at the moment.
No, we're good for now.
Actually, yeah. Got something that needs doing.
Sure, I could use an extra set of hands.
Thought you'd never ask. Got a task for you.
Alright, listen up. Got work for you.
I am Brother Thaddeus, a devout disciple of the ancient fire god, Gigglor the Infernal.
Our faith revolves around laughter in the face of chaos, finding profound comedy in the absurdity of existence.
I came here to share Gigglor's fiery wit, but these bar patrons... they re a tough crowd.
They mock Gigglor, calling him the "Eternal Punchline," and their scorn slices through my beliefs.
Honestly, it's like I'm a candle about to be snuffed out by a draft.
These bar patrons are relentless, poking holes in my beliefs with every jest.
Imagine trying to preach the divine wit of Gigglor the Infernal in a comedy club gone wrong.
They're making me question the resolve I've held onto for so long.
It's a place of unending laughter, whether through joy or mockery, which often blurs in these noisy bars.
Yet, to me, it's a den of sin. The temptations and mockery here constantly test my faith.
Ha, as if Gigglor's divine wit hasn't prepared me for this circus. But thanks anyway.
No, thank you. The absurd challenge here is almost... enlightening.
Gigglor would have me face these jesters alone, as part of my divine trial.
Appreciate the offer, but I'm wrestling with these demons in my own darkly comedic way.
Not right now, I must endure this trial by fire.
Oh, absolutely. Saving me from this den of iniquity would be a true act of mercy.
Indeed, but fair warning, Gigglor's teachings may seem like a joke to some.
Desperately! These heathens are pushing me closer to the abyss with every pint!
Yes, in Gigglor s name, help a lost soul in this circus of sin!
I am Brother Thaddeus, a devout disciple of the ancient fire god, Gigglor the Infernal.
Our faith revolves around laughter in the face of chaos.
So uh, out here... I'm trying to find a lot of things funny.
Honestly, it's like I'm a candle about to be snuffed out by a draft.
Because I was quite literally drafted for this volunteer army.
I'm a pacifist, you know? Like what am I even doing here...
Guess I better figure that out fast. You have to get a kill count to be sent home.
It's kind of a hellhole, right?
I appreciate your offer, but this is a journey I must walk alone.
Thank you, but I must find my own peace without burdening others.
Your kindness warms my heart, but I must face these trials myself.
No, but knowing there's goodwill around me is comforting.
I need to navigate this path on my own, but I value your concern.
Oh, you wish to hear about Gigglor's mirthful flames? Splendid!
Yes! Let's bring Gigglor's joyous laughter to lift everyone's spirits!
Fantastic! Together we'll ignite the base with the humor of Gigglor!
Absolutely! Spreading Gigglor's jests will surely boost morale around here!
Wonderful! Your interest in Gigglor's comedy is a beacon in these trying times!
Yes, indeed! With Gigglor's wit, we'll turn weary hearts light again!
Brilliant! Let's share a laugh in Gigglor's name and brighten this place!
Excellent! The troops could use Gigglor's humor shall we begin?
I'm Grulok, the luckiest fucker in the universe.

We, the King of Elan, the Glorious Sovereign of the Realm, the Eternal Protector of Our People, the Unifier of the Land and the Embodiment of Our Nation's Ideals, do hereby issue the following decrees, for the everlasting security and unity of Our Kingdom and People.
Proclaimed from Our Majestic Palace of Unity.

In response to the increased threat posed by the criminal activities of portal jumpers, We, in Our supreme wisdom and concern for the safety of Our people, decree that the killing of two portal jumpers shall henceforth be equated to the murder of one local citizen.
The violator of this decree shall be deemed a criminal, and it is declared that any loyal subject may kill and loot the perpetrator without fear of retribution.

Further addressing the same pressing issue, We proclaim that all lands west of Goldenfield, encompassing the abandoned industrial zone, the abandoned village, the abandoned station, and the wet forest, shall henceforth be designated as a Free Zone.

Within this Free Zone, there shall be no penalty for murder, no penalty for looting, and any individual unwilling to adhere to the civilized standards and protection of Our Kingdom shall be exiled to this abandoned land.

To guarantee the safety and prosperity of Our Loyal Subjects, We call upon all citizens to remain vigilant and steadfast in their support of these decrees, ensuring the continued peace and stability of Our Glorious Kingdom.
Under Our Hand and Seal, for the Everlasting Glory and Unity of Our Kingdom.
